	??Land forces are using helicopters to map the area near the border to locate infiltrating groups. Naval forces are continuing to impose blockades on Yemeni ports and islands to ?ĆÄhinder smuggling operations and to deprive the Houthis of back-up.??
	??The Houthis attempted to deceive the media by pretending they control more of Aden than they actually do.??
	??The coalition now controls the suburbs of Aden, in order to prevent pockets of Houthi ?ĆÄmilitia inside the city from receiving support. The coalition's forces are sparing no effort in restoring normalcy to Aden.??
	??The need to deploy land forces will depend on the success of the coalition's airstrikes.??
	??Brig. Gen. Asiri denied that the coalition forces targeted civilians or non-military targets. The Yemeni government investigators on the ground have verified that the two controversial sites were subject to ground-to-ground shells. An international investigation will be launched to hold the culprits of these attacks responsible.??
	??The coalition's forces have called on members of the Yemeni army who were persuaded ?ĆÄor coerced into joining the Houthi militia abdicate their position and re-join the legitimate army. The Yemeni government is currently with military commanders and is ready to ?ĆÄprovide assistance in regrouping, restructuring and reorganizing the Yemeni army in its fight against the militias.??
	??A lack of legitimacy in Yemen would cause the spread of terrorist groups. Such areas are ?ĆÄconsidered ideal incubators for terrorist organizations, including al-Qaeda and the Houthis.??
	??The Yemeni army is an active participant in the coalition to save Yemen. The groups are working together to liberate the Yemeni people from the grip of the Houthi militias.

title: Saudi Ambassador: We Will Continue to Support the People and Legitimate Government of Yemen
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir today expressed confidence that military operations in Yemen were on track to achieve the goal of securing peace.Speaking to a group of foreign policy experts and congressional staffers at a Capitol Hill forum, Ambassador Al-Jubeir said, ?Ć£The coalition?ĆÖs activities are achieving their objectives, it will take a little bit more time, but we?ĆÖre determined to prevail in Yemen.?ĆØ He continued, ?Ć£We are determined to strengthen and restore the legitimate government of Yemen and protect the people of Yemen from this radical group,?ĆØ backed by Iran.The Ambassador said Saudi Arabia and its allies have repeatedly attempted to negotiate a peaceful solution with the Houthis who have broken their promises and escalated their military campaign, making it necessary for the coalition to respond.He said that the coalition?ĆÖs air strikes have targeted the Houthi militias?ĆÖ air force, air bases, ballistic missiles and heavy weapon depots, and that claims that the coalition?ĆÖs air force targeted civilian areas were false. Houthi militias have bombed these facilities to generate sympathy for themselves and hostility towards the coalition?ĆÖs forces, he said.A ground offensive is not under consideration at the present time, he told the group, but said that there are sufficient forces positioned and prepared should that change.He underlined the coalition?ĆÖs goal ?Ćō that the fighting ends soon so that a process leading to a political solution can be resumed.???Ć£We recognize the Houthis are Yemenis. We recognize that the Houthis have a right to be part of the political process in Yemen and we are not denying this,?ĆØ stated Ambassador Al-Jubeir. ?Ć£But they cannot be a militia and they cannot have heavy weapons outside the scope of the state. That is a situation that is not tolerable. We see the consequences of that in Lebanon with Hezbollah, where a militia pretty much dominates the state and threatens state institutions. We will not allow this to happen on our doorstep in Yemen.?ĆØThe forum was hosted by the National Council on U.S.-Arab Relations. Video:??http://bit.ly/1Ho6LQn

content:

Gen. ?ĆÄAhmed bin Hasan Asiri, advisor in the Office of the Minister of Defense.During the past 24 hours, the coalition?ĆÖs operations have focused on north Yemen, where ?ĆÄenemy fire injured a number of border guards, killing one.

Yesterday, these groups attempted to control some areas in the city, in an attempt to create media attention, rather than a real military success. The coalition?ĆÖs forces are working in collaboration with the popular committees to mop up remaining Houthi militias and their allies, who have fortified themselves in some small areas in Aden and have attempted to gain control of government buildings. They were ?ĆÄnot successful.??

	??Buildings where Houthi groups or militias fortified themselves in were also targeted today.??

	??The Houthi militias, following continuous attacks, have scattered in different directions in the northern and central regions of Yemen.??
	??The Houthis attacked a number of prisons to set prisoners free, with the goal of causing further havoc and escalating the situation.??
	??The goal of the coalition at the beginning of the operations was to protect the Yemeni people. The coalition will continue to operate at this tempo in order to thwart the militia's goal of imposing chaos and disorder in Yemen.??
	??Land forces are using helicopters to map the area near the border to locate infiltrating groups. Naval forces are continuing to impose blockades on Yemeni ports and islands to ?ĆÄhinder smuggling operations and to deprive the Houthis of back-up.??

	??The coalition now controls the suburbs of Aden, in order to prevent pockets of Houthi ?ĆÄmilitia inside the city from receiving support. The coalition's forces are sparing no effort in restoring normalcy to Aden.??
	??The need to deploy land forces will depend on the success of the coalition's airstrikes.??
	??Brig. Gen. Asiri denied that the coalition forces targeted civilians or non-military targets. The Yemeni government investigators on the ground have verified that the two controversial sites were subject to ground-to-ground shells. An international investigation will be launched to hold the culprits of these attacks responsible.??
	??The coalition's forces have called on members of the Yemeni army who were persuaded ?ĆÄor coerced into joining the Houthi militia abdicate their position and re-join the legitimate army. The Yemeni government is currently with military commanders and is ready to ?ĆÄprovide assistance in regrouping, restructuring and reorganizing the Yemeni army in its fight against the militias.??
	??A lack of legitimacy in Yemen would cause the spread of terrorist groups. Such areas are ?ĆÄconsidered ideal incubators for terrorist organizations, including al-Qaeda and the Houthis.??
	??The Yemeni army is an active participant in the coalition to save Yemen. The groups are working together to liberate the Yemeni people from the grip of the Houthi militias.

title: Saudi Ministry of Defense Daily Briefing: Operation Decisive Storm
content:
The following is a summary of the daily press briefing held today in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, on the developments of Operation Decisive Storm. The briefing was conducted by Brig. Gen. Ahmed bin Hasan Asiri, advisor in the Office of the Minister of Defense.The coalition is urging all relief efforts, provided by countries or humanitarian organizations, to submit applications through diplomatic channels, citing the need to prepare material capabilities to accommodate such relief assistance to match them with the current military operation.
	??The Najran airport resumed operating after being suspended due to random firing originating from the vicinity of the airport.??
	???ĆÄThe coalition's operations are continuing towards targets in Aden and its surrounding areas. Intelligence has shown Houthi militia are stationed within the city and along the roads leading to it.?ĆÄ??
	???ĆÄThe coalition forces intensified operations during the past 24 hours, particularly in northern Aden. All targets moving towards the city were attacked.
	??The operations are also continuing in the northern Yemen region of Saada, including the cities of Dalea and Shabwa where it's reported that Houthi militia is beginning to enter to find shelter from coalition airstrikes.
	??The air campaign continues to target all air defense sites in addition to ballistic missiles with some of them targeted for the second time to ensure their complete destruction.
	??Intelligence found a group of Houthi militia inside Aden before the start of the operations. Both the People's Committee stationed inside Aden and the coalition forces from outside of the city are working towards cutting their access to supplies, and preventing them from moving or inflicting harm.
	???ĆÄAircrafts are on standby to immediately deal with the situation as soon as the People's Committee issues a report of activity by any Houthi group.
	???ĆÄIn reference to the size of Saudi forces near the Yemeni border, Brig. Gen. Asiri said all units have been allocated for a certain mission, stressing that the existing forces are sufficient to deal with any threats.
	???ĆÄThe airspace and ports of Yemen are under the complete control of the coalition forces. Existence of locations outside the territory of Yemen is not a goal, but the goal is that Houthi militias have no impact on the operations inside Yemen.?ĆÄ ?ĆÄ??
	???ĆÄThere have been civilian casualties due to Houthi militias' attempts to enter the city of Dhamar.
	???ĆÄThere is coordination among the coalition forces for supporting and providing relief to civilians in Yemeni.???ĆÄ ?ĆÄ??
	???ĆÄHouthi militia are trained and supported by Iran and Hezbollah.

title: Saudi Foreign Minister: Saudi Arabia Committed to Defending: Legitimate Government of Yemen
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal today addressed the 26th session of Majlis Al-Shura (Consultative Council) and stated that Saudi Arabia, along with its Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) allies and international partners, is committed to reaching a peaceful, diplomatic solution in Yemen. Prince Saud affirmed the continuation of Operation Decisive Storm to defend the legitimate government until Yemen is a safe, secure and stable nation.?Ć£The Houthi militia and agents of the former president ?Ćō supported by Iran ?Ćō insist on meddling in Yemen, re-shuffling the cards, robbing the Yemeni will, waging a coup against the government?ĆÖs constitutional legitimacy, and rejecting all peaceful solutions,?ĆØ said Prince Saud.Prince Saud reiterated that the security of Yemen is an essential aspect of the security of the Gulf region. He stated, ?Ć£The coalition to defend the legitimacy of Yemen enjoyed wide blessing and comprehensive support from our Arab and Islamic nation and the world.?ĆØPrince Saud also expressed concern in regards to the recent developments in Syria and Iraq, calling both situations ?Ć£humanitarian tragedies.?ĆØ Prince Saud reiterated the Kingdom?ĆÖs support for the people suffering in these countries and the efforts needed to end the conflicts.??On Iran, Prince Saud stated, ?Ć£We are not going to condemn or discharge Iran, but we will test its intentions, by extending our hands to it as a Muslim neighbor in order to open a new page, and if we may have the right to consider Iran as a country of civilization - and we do - it should, out of duty, promote security and peace in the region rather than destabilize it.?ĆØ

title: Saudi Ministry of Defense Daily Briefing: Operation Decisive Storm
content:
The following is a summary of the daily press briefing held today in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, on the developments of Operation Decisive Storm. The briefing was conducted by Brig. Gen. Ahmed bin Hasan Asiri, advisor in the Office of the Minister of Defense.
	Coalition forces continue to devastate the Houthi militia weapons storage facilities and monitor their movements.
	
	Ballistic missiles were targeted, following an unsuccessful attempt by Houthi militias on Sunday to launch a ballistic missile from 60-65 kilometers on the outskirts of Sana?ĆÖa. Coalition fighter jets were dispatched to locate and destroy the missile launch pad.
	
	Efforts are underway to destroy the remaining ballistic missiles, particularly mobile missiles. Yesterday, warplanes destroyed a missile storage facility.
	
	Great intelligence efforts are being exerted to determine the locations of those missiles which the Houthi militias are hiding inside homes and in residential areas.
	
	The coalition continues to monitor and target Houthi militia movements and their weapons and supplies storage facilities.?? Attempts by the Houthis to move tanks between cities has been thwarted.
	
	Coalition operations have targeted roads leading to Aden to hinder any advance by the Houthis and their supporters.
	
	Houthi commanders, supplies and vehicles have been the target for airstrikes in an effort to paralyze the Houthi movement.??
	
	The coalition forces have a strong commitment to avoiding harm to civilians.
	
	On the question if coalition forces erroneously targeted a refugee camp in Hajar, Brig. Gen. Asiri said that he will check on the reports. Brig. Gen. Asiri added that Houthi militias are trying to implant their forces within residential areas, putting civilians in harm?ĆÖs way.
	
	Coalition warplanes were fired on from a residential area, forcing a decisive response from the coalition forces.
	The military campaign is based on goals, not a timetable.?? In coordination with Yemeni tribes on the ground, the campaign is divided into several phases. These objectives still appear on schedule. The current phase includes protecting the Saudi border from Houthi militias.?? If grounds troops are needed, the coalition will make the announcement.
	??The Saudi Navy has deployed its forces along the border and coalition naval forces have imposed a blockade on Yemen's ports to prevent to weapons from reaching Houthi militias.

title: Saudi King: Yemen Campaign to Continue until Goals are Achieved
content:
(WASHINGTON, DC)??- The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin ?ĆÄAbdulaziz today vowed that the Saudi-led coalition against Houthi rebels in Yemen will ?ĆÄcontinue until the country is stable and safe, blaming foreign intervention for the unrest. The ?ĆÄcomments came during a speech at the 26th Arab League Summit in Sharm El-Sheikh, Egypt.?ĆÄ?ĆÄ?Ć£The excruciating reality of terrorism, internal conflicts and bloodshed experienced by a number ?ĆÄof Arab countries are only the inevitable result of an alliance between terrorism and sectarianism, ?ĆÄwhich is led by regional powers whose blatant interventions in the Arab region have resulted in ?ĆÄundermining security and stability in some of our countries,?ĆØ said King Salman. ?Ć£In Yemen, the ?ĆÄforeign intervention enabled the Houthi militias to overtake the legitimate authority, occupy ?ĆÄSana?ĆÖa, and disrupt the implementation of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Initiative, which ?ĆÄhas sought to preserve Yemen's unity, stability and security.?ĆØ?ĆÄ
?ĆÄ
?Ć£Riyadh opens its doors to all Yemeni political parties committed to Yemen's security and ?ĆÄstability, to meet under the umbrella of GCC and within the context of maintaining the ?ĆÄlegitimacy, rejection of any legitimacy breaching, ensuring the return of the State control over all ?ĆÄYemeni territory, returning to the State all weapons, and holding no threat the security of ?ĆÄneighboring States,?ĆØ said King Salman. ?Ć£Operation Decisive Storm will continue until these ?ĆÄobjectives are achieved, so that the Yemeni people are able to enjoy security and stability.?ĆØ?ĆÄKing Salman called on the Arab world to unite against terrorism while addressing other conflicts ?ĆÄacross the region, including Palestine, Syria, and Libya. He stated, ?Ć£The scourge of extremism ?ĆÄand terrorism are among the challenges facing the Arab nation, targeting the security and stability ?ĆÄof our countries, and requiring us to be vigilant and be in solidarity when taking necessary actions ?ĆÄto confront and eradicate its roots.?ĆØ ?ĆÄKing Salman met with Egyptian President Abdel Fattah El Sisi and Yemini President Abdrabu ?ĆÄMansur Hadi on the sidelines of the summit.

title: Statement by Saudi Ambassador Al-Jubeir on Military Operations in Yemen
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- Today, the Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir hosted a press conference at the Royal Embassy of Saudi Arabia and issued the following statement:?Ć£Saudi Arabia has launched military operations in Yemen, as part of a coalition of over ten countries in response to a direct request from the legitimate government of Yemen. The operation will be limited in nature, and designed to protect the people of Yemen and its legitimate government from a takeover by the Houthis. A violent extremist militia. The Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) countries tried to facilitate a peaceful transition of government in Yemen, but the Houthis have continuously undercut the process by occupying territory and seizing weapons belonging to the government. In spite of repeated efforts by the GCC, G10 countries and the Special Representative of the U.N. Secretary General to seek a peaceful way to implement the GCC initiatives and the outcomes of the national dialogue that define the political transition in Yemen, the Houthis have reneged on every single agreement they have made and continue their quest to take over the country by violent means. They captured the capital city of Sana?ĆÖa, they placed the legitimate president, prime minister and cabinet members under house arrest, they seized the security services and they continue to expand their occupation of the country.?ĆØ?Ć£In a letter dated March 7, 2015, President Hadi of Yemen made a request of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to convene a conference under the auspices of the GCC to which all Yemeni political factions seeking to preserve security and stability in Yemen would be invited. The Houthis rejected this invitation and continued their violent onslaught in Yemen to the point where they were threatening to occupy the city of Aden, which had become the temporary capital for the legitimate government of President Hadi after he was able to escape from Sana?ĆÖa. In a letter, dated March 24, 2015, President Hadi requested, based on the principle of self-defense, enshrined in Article 51 of the U.N. Charter, as well as in the Arab League charter?ĆÖs collective defense mechanism, a request for immediate support ?Ćō by all means necessary ?Ćō including military intervention to protect Yemen and its people from the continued Houthi aggression and to support it in fighting al Qaeda and ISIL.?ĆØ?Ć£Based on the appeal from President Hadi, and based on the Kingdom?ĆÖs responsibility to Yemen and its people, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, along with its allies within the GCC and outside the GCC, launched military operations in support of the people of Yemen and their legitimate government.?ĆØ?Ć£May God Almighty protect the brave soldiers and may He grant them success in their noble mission.?ĆØ

title: Saudi Foreign Minister: Iran Shouldn?ĆÖt Get ?Ć£Undeserved Deal?ĆØ
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal said Iran shouldn?ĆÖt get an ?Ć£undeserved deal?ĆØ on its nuclear program. Prince Saud was speaking during a joint press conference yesterday in Riyadh with British Foreign Secretary Philip Hammond.??While discussing efforts to resolve the Iranian nuclear issue through peaceful means, Prince Saud stated, ?Ć£[solution] is possible through the League of Arab States to make the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) and the Middle East region free of weapons of mass destruction and that in return, it is not possible to grant Iran an undeserved deal.?ĆØ??On the escalating crisis in Yemen, Prince Saud reiterated support to the Yemeni government and stressed the importance of the call by President Abdrabu Mansur Hadi to hold a conference in Riyadh under the GCC umbrella and attended by all factors of Yemen who are interested in maintaining security and stability. ?Ć£Our aim is to provide the vehicle for the president to return peacefully to Yemen and provide the leadership as required to bring this country back,?ĆØ he stated.Other topics at the forefront of regional issues addressed in the press conference included the tragedy in Syria, the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, strengthening Saudi-Iraqi relations and joint efforts within the international coalition to fight Daesh.

title: Saudi Arabia Condemns Aden and Sana?ĆÖa Terror Attacks
content: Dispatches medical supplies to Yemen and offers to treat victims in Saudi Arabia.(WASHINGTON, DC)??-?? The government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia issued ?ĆÄthe following statement today on the terrorist attacks in Aden and Sana?ĆÖa:?ĆÄSaudi Arabia today condemned the terrorist attacks in Yemen as a threat to the security and ?ĆÄunity of the country. The Kingdom has followed with great concern the painful events in Yemen, ?ĆÄincluding Thursday?ĆÖs aerial bombing of the Presidential Palace in Aden and adjacent areas, as ?ĆÄwell as today?ĆÖs bombings in Sana?ĆÖa. Saudi Arabia condemns these terrorist attacks, which will ?ĆÄonly further destabilize the security and stability in Yemen and subject its people to strife and ?ĆÄdestruction.?ĆÄThe Kingdom confirms that it supports the legitimacy of the government of Yemen and stands ?ĆÄby the brotherly people of Yemen with all its resources.?ĆÄThe Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has ordered dispatch of ?ĆÄurgent medical aid to treat the wounded and offered to transfer victims of the terrorist attacks in ?ĆÄAden and Sana?ĆÖa to Saudi Arabia for medical treatment.?ĆÄThe Kingdom stresses the importance for all political factions in Yemen who are interested in ?ĆÄmaintaining the security and stability of the country to promptly confirm that they will participate ?ĆÄin the conference sponsored by the Gulf Cooperation Council in Riyadh.

title: Saudi Arabia Pledges $4 Billion in Assistance to Egypt
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- Crown Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz announced today that Saudi Arabia will provide Egypt with $4 billion in assistance during the Egypt Economic Development Conference (EEDC) in Sharm El-Sheikh. Crown Prince Muqrin is leading the Saudi delegation to the three-day investment summit on behalf of Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz.Crown Prince Muqrin noted that $2 billion will be deposited in Egypt?ĆÖs Central Bank, while the remaining funds will be distributed as development aid through the Saudi Development Fund.The Kingdom?ĆÖs assistance is part of a $12.5 billion Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) financial assistance package to Egypt announced at the EEDC.During his remarks, Crown Prince Muqrin reiterated the Kingdom?ĆÖs commitment to the development and stability of Egypt. He also pointed out that Saudi Arabia is the largest foreign investor in Egypt and that areas of growth for the two nations will continue to flourish. Crown Prince Muqrin denounced the terror acts in Egypt and cited Saudi Arabia?ĆÖs firm commitment behind the government in the fight against terrorism and extremism.

title: Saudi King Calls for Economic Strength and Stability
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz on Tuesday announced his domestic and foreign policies to a group of government officials, scholars and other dignitaries in Riyadh. King Salman emphasized his plan to build a more diversified economy and defend Islamic causes globally.King Salman addressed falling oil prices, assuring the Saudi people that the nation will remain economically stable. ?Ć£We encourage and support medium and small enterprises to grow so that they become a strong economic area for a large segment of society,?ĆØ King Salman stated. ?Ć£The coming years will witness rich and great achievements that enhance industrial and services roles in the national economy.?ĆØKing Salman reinforced the Kingdom?ĆÖs commitment to the teachings of Islam, stating that Saudi foreign policy would continue to defend Islamic causes globally. ?Ć£Saudi foreign policy is always committed to the teachings of our religion, calling for love and peace in accordance with its principles,?ĆØ said King Salman.In response to the growing threat of extremism and violence in the region, King Salman condemned terrorism in all forms. He stated, ?Ć£The Kingdom is focused on the fight against extremism and terrorism in all its forms and manifestations, whatever its sources, and in cooperation with brotherly and friendly countries and the international organizations in the fight against this detestable menace, and in seeking eradicating its roots and causes.?ĆØKing Salman also emphasized the need for the Kingdom to prioritize national security stating, ?Ć£Security is everyone?ĆÖs responsibility and will not allow anyone to tamper with our security and stability.?ĆØKing Salman urged the people of Saudi Arabia to stand united and said that Saudi Arabia would continue its efforts to achieve comprehensive, integrated and balanced development for all regions. ?Ć£Ever since the Kingdom?ĆÖs founding, the government has been making continuous efforts for development and modernization, without deviating from religious principles and social values while protecting the rights of all citizens,?ĆØ said King Salman.

title: Saudi Foreign Minister Meets with U.S Secretary of State
content:
(WASHINGTON, DC)??- Today, Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal and U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry held a joint press conference in Riyadh to discuss a wide range of issues of mutual interest; including Iran?ĆÖs nuclear program, Yemen, Syria and the Middle East peace process. During his visit, Secretary Kerry also met with foreign ministers of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) and separately with Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz.With regards to Yemen, Prince Saud said, ?Ć£The international community expressed its full support to the legitimate government in Yemen, led by President Abd Rabbo Mansour Hadi. This is clearly reflected in the statements issued by GCC, Arab League and the U.N. Security Council.?ĆØPrince Saud reiterated the importance of the global anti-Daash [ISIS] coalition, calling on the coalition to fight the militants on the ground. ?Ć£The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia underscores the importance of this coalition in fighting Daash in Iraq and Syria, and stresses the need to provide the military means necessary to fight this challenge on the ground,?ĆØ said Prince Saud. He later added that the crisis in Syria ?Ć£made Syria a safe haven for terrorist organizations, with the endorsement of the illegitimate Bashar Al-Assad's regime.?ĆØRegarding the latest negotiations concerning Iran?ĆÖs nuclear program, Prince Saud said, ?Ć£The Saudi government supports the efforts of P5+1 in view of its keenness to solve this file peacefully and reach a successful agreement that dissipates the doubts and protects against shifting it into a military program that threatens the region and the world.?ĆØ He continued, ?Ć£Saudi Arabia also supports the P5+1 position in seeking a firm international inspection system to ensure that Iran is not seeking the manufacture or the possession of nuclear weapons, together with maintaining Iran's right and the rights of all countries in the region to the peaceful use of nuclear energy according IAEA's standards, requirements and supervision.?ĆØPrince Saud said that Saudi Arabia continues to hold the same position on the Middle East peace process, stating ?Ć£Our view will always be based on the principles and the resolutions of international legitimacy, as well as the Arab Peace Initiative aiming at creation of a viable, independent Palestinian State.
Unfortunately, these efforts are still not yielding results.?ĆØ He concluded, ?Ć£On our part, we stress the importance of the international community shouldering its responsibility towards the inhumane practices of Israel against the Palestinian people.?ĆØ

title: Saudi Arabia Calls for Greater Regional Cooperation
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- On behalf of Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal, Prince Dr. Torki bin Mohammed bin Saud Al-Kabeer, Undersecretary of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs for Multilateral Relations, led the Saudi delegation to the ministerial conference on regional security yesterday at the Arab League headquarters in Cairo. The growing terrorism threat, challenges facing Arab national security and nuclear non-proliferation were discussed during the meeting.Speaking at the opening of the conference, Prince Torki warned that the Arab region is witnessing unprecedented dangers and threats which has led to the loss of security and stability in a number of countries and threatens others in the region.Prince Torki refuted the claim made by terrorists belonging to Islam or planning to enforce Sharia [Islamic] law, citing their flagrant violations of human rights in the name of Islam.While outlining Saudi Arabia?ĆÖs counterterrorism efforts, Prince Torki said the Kingdom has joined all international and regional agreements and treaties seeking to fight terrorism and dry up sources of financing.He said that Iran has the right to nuclear energy for peaceful purposes under complete UN supervision and in line with the nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty.Prince Torki concluded that Arab countries should aim for constructive cooperation in the political, economic and security fields, as per the Arab League charter and other bilateral cooperation agreements between Arab countries.

title: King Salman: Saudi Arabia Waging Relentless Effort to Defeat Terrorism
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz said that Saudi Arabia is combatting terrorism and extremism at all levels during a statement yesterday in Makkah at the opening ceremony of the conference on Islam and counterterrorism, organized by the Muslim World League. King Salman?ĆÖs remarks were presented by Prince Khalid Al-Faisal, governor of Makkah and advisor to King Salman.King Salman said that Saudi Arabia has ?Ć£tried hard to fight terrorism as an ideology and practice and used its national security apparatus to combat terrorism relentlessly.?ĆØ He continued, ?Ć£?Ć? our Air Force is part of the international coalition fighting these terrorists.?ĆØKing Salman said Islamic extremists have violated all Islamic principles and are a danger to Islam. He said that these radicals, who are carrying ?Ć£a falsified Islamic banner,?ĆØ have promoted a version of Islam which is ?Ć£fueling the international opinion to hate Muslims.?ĆØ He continued, ?Ć£Islam is a religion of tolerance, love and peace that rejects violence, terror and extremism.?ĆØAlso speaking at the opening session were the Sheikh of Al Azhar Dr. Ahmed Mohammed Al-Tayeb; the Secretary General of Muslim World League (MWL) Dr. Abdullah bin Abdulmohsen Al-Turki; and Sheikh Abdulaziz Al-AsShaikh, Grand Mufti of Saudi Arabia, Chairman of the Council of Senior Ulema (religious scholars) and the General Presidency of Scholarly Research and Ifta, and Chairman of the MWL. They called on the Muslim world to shoulder its responsibility to fight terrorism while stressing the importance of education and the media to portray the true religion of Islam.Saudi Arabia vehemently condemns terrorism. Last year, the late King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz provided the United Nations Counter-Terrorism Center with a donation of $100 million to enhance its capabilities and effectiveness in helping countries combat terrorism.

title: Saudi Arabia Reaffirms Commitment to Fighting Terrorism
content: (WASHINGTON, DC) ?Ćō Saudi Vice Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Abdulaziz bin Abdullah reaffirmed the Kingdom?ĆÖs commitment to fighting terrorism during a statement yesterday at the White House Summit on Countering Violent Extremism. Prince Abdulaziz led the Saudi delegation at the summit.???Ć£Saudi Arabia is fully committed to fighting terrorism and all its sources regardless race, color, religion or doctrine,?ĆØ said Prince Abdulaziz.During his remarks, Prince Abdulaziz stressed collective responsibility in fighting extremist ideology and terror financing.?Ć£In this regard, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia has issued many internal procedures and regulations and penalties to counter and eliminate this phenomenon.?ĆØSaudi Arabia vehemently condemns extremists and terrorists who aim to subvert the Islamic faith. Saudi officials and religious scholars have long been outspoken against terrorism and extremism.

title: OIC Secretary General Speaks on Islamophobia
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- Today, Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir hosted a reception for Secretary General of the Organization of the Islamic Cooperation (OIC), Mr. Iyad Ameen Madani, at the Royal Embassy of Saudi Arabia. Guests included ambassadors from OIC member states.??During a briefing on Islamophobia, Mr. Madani stated, ?Ć£The OIC condemns terrorism no matter who commits it and where it takes place.?ĆØ He continued, ?Ć£One of the most important challenges we face is from within. The faith is being hijacked by extremists.?ĆØMr. Madani said that one has to understand what attracts people to extremist groups like Daesh [ISIS]. The Secretary General pointed out that in the case of Iraq and Syria, the decline of national identity and its replacement by sectarian identity as well as the collapse of civil structure have fueled radicalism. In the case of Boko Haram, economic issues have fill the ranks of the group. The majority of Boko Haram?ĆÖs recruits come from a marginalized and underdeveloped region, and they find in group to be a source of income and purpose.Mr. Madani also stressed the need to give youth hope. ?Ć£The OIC has to work on creating hope for youth, otherwise we will have a lost generation if we cannot reach out to them,?ĆØ he said.Ambassador Al-Jubeir also provided remarks, stressing the importance of public condemnation. ?Ć£As the party of the world that suffers the most from terrorism, we need to make sure our views on terrorism are heard,?ĆØ said Ambassador Al-Jubeir.Mr. Madani will be attending the White House Summit on Countering Violent Extremism tomorrow.The OIC is comprised of 57 nations and is the second largest international body after the U.N. The OIC was established in 1969 and is headquartered in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia.

title: Saudi Embassy Dismisses Moussaoui's Allegations
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- The following statement was issued by the Royal Embassy of Saudi Arabia:There is no evidence to support Zacarias Moussaoui's claim. The September 11 attack has been the most intensely investigated crime in history and the findings show no involvement by the Saudi government or Saudi officials. As confirmed by the 9/11 Commission, there is "no evidence that the Saudi government as an institution or senior Saudi officials individually funded the organization [Al-Qaeda]."Moussaoui is a deranged criminal whose own lawyers presented evidence that he was mentally incompetent. His words have no credibility.His goal in making these statements only serves to get attention for himself and try to do what he could not do through acts of terrorism - to undermine Saudi-U.S. relations.

title: GCC Condemns Houthi ?Ć£Coup?ĆØ in Yemen
content: (WASHINGTON, DC) ?Ćō Today, Foreign Ministers of the Gulf Cooperation ?ĆÄCouncil (GCC) condemned the use of terrorism by the Houthis against Yemen in a statement ?ĆÄfollowing their 41st GCC Extraordinary Ministerial Meeting, which was held in Riyadh.?ĆÄ?ĆÄ?Ć£The GCC council considers what happened in Sana?ĆÖa on Tuesday, Jan.??20, 2015, a coup ?ĆÄagainst legitimacy?Ć?undermining the political process in the Republic of Yemen,?ĆØ the ministers ?ĆÄsaid. ?ĆÄThe GCC confirmed that Yemen's security is an integral part of national security in the region. ?ĆÄ?ĆÄ?Ć£Yemen's stability and unity is a top priority for the GCC countries?Ć?the GCC will take the ?ĆÄrequired measures to protect its member states' security, stability and vital interests in Yemen.?ĆØ?? ?ĆÄIn addition, the ministers reaffirmed their ?Ć£continual support for the Yemeni people and strongly ?ĆÄcondemns these terrorist acts, including infringing on the constitutional legitimacy, storming the ?ĆÄpresidency palace, abducting Dr. Ahmed Awad bin Mubarak, Director of the Office of the ?ĆÄPresident ?Ć? and attempting to assassinate Prime Minister Khalid Mahfouz Bahah.?ĆØ ?ĆÄThe council demanded that the Houthis withdraw from the presidential palace and the residences ?ĆÄof the President and Prime Minister, lift the siege of the presidential palace, release Dr. Mubarak, ?ĆÄand normalize the security situation in Sana?ĆÖa and return government institutions to state control.???? ?ĆÄThe council called upon all parties and political forces to put the interests of Yemen as top ?ĆÄpriority and offered to send an envoy to mediate an end to the crisis.?ĆÄ?ĆÄ?Ć£The Ministerial Council stresses the need to implement the Yemen-relevant resolutions from the ?ĆÄU.N. Security Council ?Ć?what is happening in Yemen represents a threat to international peace ?ĆÄand security in the region and the whole world.?ĆØ ?ĆÄGCC Foreign Ministers statement on the situation in Yemen??

title: Saudi King Reaffirms Commitment to Combating Terrorism
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- On behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, Crown Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, delivered a speech today inaugurating the third year of the sixth session of the Consultative Council [Majlis Al-Shura]. King Abdullah confirmed that the Kingdom will not allow any threat to national security, and urged others to promote the cultural and moral values upon which Saudi Arabia and the Islamic and Arab identity was founded.?Ć£On this occasion, and from this platform, we assure everyone that we will not allow any threat to the national unity,?ĆØ said King Abdullah. ?Ć£Let those who have pledged themselves to external organizations or states know that they have no place among us and that they will be confronted firmly and strongly. We also reaffirm our resolve to continue the intellectual and security work to counter terrorism. We will not rest until we fortify our dear country from this danger.?ĆØKing Abdullah spoke on domestic and international efforts to combat terrorism. He stated, ?Ć£We confronted it through several levels, including the approval of the terrorism crimes and finance regulation, as well as the efforts by scholars, preachers and intellectuals to clarify the deviation of this thought and its danger to faith and security. Senior scholars have issued numerous statements on the danger of terrorism. In addition, the Kingdom continues security work to counter it through proactive security moves to thwart the terrorists' plans, arrest them and bring them to justice.?ĆØ?Ć£On the external front, your state has taken the lead in warning against terrorism through our call for the establishment of the International Centre for Fighting Terrorism, as well as the call to confront terrorism at the international level,?ĆØ stated King Abdullah. ?Ć£The Kingdom provided this center with a donation of $100 million to urgently activate the role of this center.?ĆØKing Abdullah also highlighted ongoing achievements and other priorities of Saudi Arabia in the upcoming year. Plans to improve conditions in the Kingdom; including the standard of living, services and facilities, sustainable development, partnerships with private sector, national workforce development and more, were addressed.

title: Saudi King Condemns Pakistan School Attack, Offers Condolences
content:
(WASHINGTON, DC) ?Ćō The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz and ?ĆÄCrown Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, sent cables ?ĆÄof condolences to Pakistani President Memnon Hussein for the victims of Tuesday?ĆÖs terrorist ?ĆÄattack against a school in Peshawar.
?ĆÄ
?ĆÄ?Ć£As we strongly condemn these criminal acts, we express to your Excellency, the families of the ?ĆÄvictims, and the brotherly people of Pakistan our condolences,?ĆØ said King Abdullah.
?ĆÄ
Saudi Grand Mufti Sheikh Abdulaziz Al-AsShaikh, who is also Chairman of the Council of ?ĆÄSenior Ulema (religious scholars) and the General Presidency of Scholarly Research and Ifta, also ?ĆÄcondemned the attack, describing it as a corruption, injustice and aggression. The Grand Mufti ?ĆÄstressed that all religions prohibit murder as unjustly and prevent it.?ĆÄ

title: Saudi Minister of Interior Meets with President Obama
content: (WASHINGTON, DC) ?Ćō Today, Saudi Minister of Interior Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz?ĆÄ met with U.S. President Barack Obama at the White House to discuss the latest regional developments. Prince Mohammad conveyed greetings to the president on behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, and the President asked Prince Mohammad to convey his best wishes to King Abdullah.During the meeting, President Obama and Prince Mohammad reaffirmed the strong and enduring partnership between Saudi Arabia and the US. President Obama expressed appreciation for the Kingdom?ĆÖs contributions to the global coalition to counter Daash (ISIS) and the Kingdom?ĆÖs constructive role in Yemen. President Obama and Prince Mohammad reaffirmed the importance of sustaining mutual cooperation aimed at supporting President Abdu Rabu Mansour Hadi and Yemen's new legitimate government. President Obama noted the Kingdom?ĆÖs critical role in upholding regional peace and security and the Kingdom?ĆÖs contributions to the international response to the Ebola epidemic.Vice President Joe Biden, National Security Advisor Susan Rice, Assistant to the President for Homeland Security and Counterterrorism Lisa Monaco, and U.S. Ambassador to Saudi Arabia Joseph Westphal also attended the meeting.Prince Mohammad was accompanied by Saudi Ambassador to the U.S. Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.

title: Saudi King Abdullah Donates $35M to Help Fight Ebola in West Africa
content: (Washington, DC) ?Ćō The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz donated $35 million to help fight Ebola in West Africa.This was announced by President of the Islamic Development Bank (IDB) Dr. Ahmad Mohamed Ali. The donation will be implemented by the IDB.Dr. Ali said that the donation includes providing thermal sensors and medical examination equipment to schools, airports, railway stations and bus stations to diagnose the disease, ensuring early treatment, thereby preventing further transmission of the disease.The donation will also be used for establishing medical testing centers in Sierra Leone, Guinea, Liberia and Mali.?Ć£King Abdullah believes that, together, humankind inhabits a small planet and the plight of those in need affects us all. It is our mutual responsibility as citizens of the world to help those who are suffering,?ĆØ said Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.

title: Saudi Minister of Interior Meets with Acting Deputy Secretary of State

content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir hosted a dinner last night in honor of Saudi Minister of Interior Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz. The reception was ?ĆÄattended by a number of U.S. administration and congressional officials, as well as businessmen, diplomats ?ĆÄand embassy staff.?ĆÄPrince Mohammed is in Washington this week for a working visit. title: Saudi King Abdullah Donates $104M to the World Food Program
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz contributed $104 million to the World Food Program (WFP) to help meet an urgent need for funding to continue food distribution to millions of refugees sheltering in countries neighboring Syria, in Ethiopia and in Kenya.?Ć£The contribution to the World Food Program reflects King Abdullah's desire to help those in need, regardless of religion, sect or ethnicity,?ĆØ said Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.The contribution includes $52 million to assist nearly 1.7 million Syrian refugees; $42 million for refugees in Ethiopia, many from South Sudan; and $10 million to refugees in Kenya.?Ć£We are extremely grateful for this assistance, which comes at a crucial moment for these refugees ?Ćō all of whom have fled protracted conflict,?ĆØ said WFP?ĆÖs Executive Director Ertharin Cousin. ?Ć£The compassion and commitment shown by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud will help save the refugees, especially children, from the pain and debilitating effects of hunger.?ĆØEarlier this year, Saudi Arabia provided $500 million for humanitarian relief efforts in Iraq, including nearly $150 million to WFP.

title: Saudi Arabia Arrests 135 on Terror Charges
content: (WASHINGTON, DC) ?Ćō The Ministry of Interior announced today that 135 terrorism suspects have ?ĆÄbeen arrested nationwide, including 26 foreign nationals.The 26 foreign nationals arrested included 16 Syrians, three Yemenis, an Egyptian, an Afghani, ?ĆÄan Ethiopian, a Bahraini, an Iraqi, a Lebanese citizen and a person whose nationality has not been ?ĆÄestablished.?ĆÄThe individuals are accused of terrorism-?ĆÄrelated offenses, including joining extremist groups, ?ĆÄterror financing, traveling abroad for terrorist training, arms smuggling, firing at security forces ?ĆÄand planning to carry out terrorist acts to destabilize the Kingdom?ĆÖs security.Saudi Arabia is committed to stopping terrorism in all its forms. In February, the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz issued a royal decree on counterterrorism. The decree reinforced the Kingdom's long-held policy that acts of terrorism, including membership in terrorist organizations, and ?ĆÄparticipation in hostilities outside the Kingdom, are criminal acts that will not be tolerated.

title: Adnan El Shukrijumah is Not a Saudi citizen
content:
(WASHINGTON, DC) ?Ćō The Royal Embassy of Saudi Arabia continues to correct the issue regarding the nationality of ?ĆÄAdnan G. El Shukrijumah, who was reportedly killed this weekend by the Pakistani Army. Mr. ?ĆÄEl Shukrijumah was wanted by U.S. authorities on terrorism charges.
?ĆÄ
In March 2003, the Ministry of Interior announced that Mr. El Shukrijumah was not, and never ?ĆÄhas been, a Saudi citizen, as has been incorrectly reported.?ĆÄHis father, Gulshai El Shukrijumah, worked in Saudi Arabia for 27 years as an expatriate ?ĆÄemployee until 1986 when the family moved to the United States. He also did not have Saudi ?ĆÄcitizenship.

title: Saudi Foreign Minister Calls for Strengthening of Moderate Forces Against Daash (ISIS)
content: (WASHINGTON, DC) ?Ćō Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal addressed partner countries in the coalition against Daash (ISIS) during an international conference held in Brussels, Belgium today. Prince Saud stated that the Kingdom has been and still is at the forefront of fighting terrorism.During his remarks, Prince Saud stated, ?Ć£We meet today to declare to the world our determination to move forward in the fight against terrorism in all its forms and manifestations and whatever sources.?ĆØPrince Saud stated that the regional conference hosted by Saudi Arabia last September formed the nucleus of the international coalition against ISIS. ?Ć£The contribution of the Kingdom has not been confined to participation in military operations against the group, but also has extended to include humanitarian assistance to the brotherly Iraqi people and coordination with the international community to dry up the financing sources of terrorist groups and expose the criminal nature of these groups, which are contrary to the tolerant teachings of Islam,?ĆØ said Prince Saud.?Ć£We believe that these efforts require the presence of combat troops on the ground,?ĆØ said Prince Saud. ?Ć£To achieve an end to the violence in Syria, there is an urgent need to strengthen the forces of moderation represented in the Free Syrian Army and all other moderate opposition forces, and to seek their annexation with the regular forces within the framework of transitional ruling authority set forth in Geneva Declaration 1, except for those who committed war crimes against humanity and stained their hands with the blood of innocent Syrians.?ĆØPrince Saud also addressed Iraq, stating, ?Ć£It requires the unity of the internal front, the restructure of the army under a collective doctrine, its rehabilitation with national and comprehensive participation by all components of the Iraqi people away from the sectarian exclusion policy with the removal of all aspects and activities of armed militias working outside the framework of the Iraqi State.?ĆØ

title: Saudi Authorities Arrest 77 for Deadly Attack Linked to ISIS
content: (WASHINGTON, DC)??- The Ministry of Interior announced yesterday that 73 Saudis and four foreigners were arrested for the Nov. 3 attack in Al-Dalwah village in Al-Ahsa governorate. Documents and media devices discovered during the investigation revealed that the terrorist cell was directly connected with ISIS.A spokesman for the Ministry of Interior, Major General Mansour Al-Turki, said that Saudi criminal investigators were able to collect crucial information, which led to the arrest of the four attackers and their supporters. The spokesman added that the four Saudi perpetrators of the terrorist attack, identified as Abdullah bin Saeed Al-Sarhan, Khalid bin Zuid Al-Anizi, Marwan bin Ibrahim Adhifr and Tariq bin Mussad Al- Maimouni, are in custody and that three of them have previously been convicted of crimes and released after serving their sentences.The lead attacker, Al-Sarhan, received orders from ISIS abroad ?Ć£specifying the target, the targeted, and the timing of the attack,?ĆØ said Major General Al-Turki.Major General Al-Turki confirmed that all the leading perpetrators of the attack were arrested, along with 73 suspected terrorists and supporters. Those in custody include a Syrian, a Jordanian, a Turk and a person whose nationality has not been established.Earlier this month, Saudi Grand Mufti Sheikh Abdulaziz Al-AsShaikh condemned the terrorists who carried out the criminal attack. In a televised speech on Nov. 4, Sheikh Al-AsShaikh stated, ?Ć£What happened in Al-Ahsa is an example of brutal aggression and a great injustice. This is carried out by sick minds seeking to incite fitna (sedition) between people, God forbid.?ĆØ

title: Saudi National Guard Minister Concludes Official Visit to the U.S.
content: (WASHINGTON, DC) ?Ćō Saudi Minister of National Guard Prince Miteb bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz concluded his official to Washington, D.C. today. The three-day visit focused on the bilateral relationship and international issues of common interest.??Prince Miteb met with several senior U.S. officials throughout his visit, including President Barack Obama, Secretary of Defense Chuck Hagel, Director of Central Intelligence John Brennan, Chief of National Guard Bureau General Frank J. Grass and members of Congress. Prince Miteb also visited with Saudi students studying abroad in the U.S.Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir hosted a dinner ceremony last night in honor of Prince Miteb. The ceremony was attended by a number of U.S. officials and Saudi diplomats.The National Guard delegation included Chief of Military Apparatus, General Mohammad bin Khalid Al-Nahidh and Chief of the Ministry's Aviation Authority, Air Vice-Marshall Rashid bin Abdullah Al-Zahrani.

title: King Abdullah Interfaith Center Hosts Unity Conference
content: (WASHINGTON, DC) ?Ćō The King Abdullah International Center for Interreligious and Intercultural Dialogue (KAICIID) in Vienna hosted a three-day interfaith conference this week titled, ?Ć£United Against Violence in the Name of Religion.?ĆØ??During the conference, high-level representatives of the major world religious and social institutions gathered to unite against violence in the name of religion, especially in the context of the recent crisis in Iraq and Syria.??Saudi State Minister for Foreign Affairs Dr. Nizar Madani stated, ?Ć£Those who have embraced terrorism unfortunately attribute everything they do, every oppression they practice, to Islam. Islam has nothing to do with them.?ĆØ??Religious leaders from communities across the Middle East jointly issued the Vienna Declaration. It emphasized the right of all to be treated with dignity and humanity regardless of their religious tradition. Atrocities committed in the name of religion are crimes against humanity, and crimes against religion. The declaration also rejected and denounced the support or sponsorship of terrorism.Speaking after the meeting on Wednesday, KAICIID Secretary General Faisal bin Abdulrahman bin Muaammar said, ?Ć£The manipulation of religion in Iraq and Syria has horrific human consequences. Today we witnessed a reason for hope: the conviction and resolve of religious leaders from many different faiths, speaking with one voice, to say that ISIS and other extremist groups do not act in the name of religion.?ĆØIn 2011, Saudi Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal signed an agreement with the then Austrian Vice-Chancellor and Federal Minister of European and International Affairs Dr. Michael Spindelegger and then-Spanish Minister of Foreign Affairs and Cooperation Trinidad Jim??nez Garc??a-Herrera to establish the center to build and foster relations between different faiths and cultures. The Board of Directors is comprised of representatives of major world religions (Judaism, Christianity, Islam, Hinduism and Buddhism) and cultures. Additional information about the center is available at??www.kaiciid.org.

title: Saudi Minister of National Guard holds meeting with President Obama
