title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet finally adjourned after reviewing a number of local issues on its agenda. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's strong condemnation of the terrorist bombings that took place in northern Sinai in Egypt and the terrorist attack on a camp for displaced people in Anbar province in Iraq, expressing its condolences to the Egyptian and Iraqi governments, reconfirming the Kingdom's solidarity and stand with Egypt and Iraq and its firm positions against terrorism and extremism. The Cabinet reviewed the joint statement issued by Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Bahrain, and Egypt after receiving the Qatari reply from Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, Emir of Kuwait, renewing its thanks and appreciation to the Kuwaiti Emir for his endeavors and efforts to resolve the crisis with the Qatari government. The Statement was initially directed at the Qatari government to correct its track seeking to destabilize the countries in the region and intervene in their affairs. The Cabinet praised the security forces for thwarting a terrorist act during the holy month of Ramadan to target the Grand Holy Mosque in Makkah and its visitors. It also condemned the terrorist attacks that targeted security patrols in Qatif Governorate which resulted in martyring two security men and injuring others. The Cabinet stressed that security authorities in the Kingdom are capable of confronting such criminal plans and anyone else who tampers with the Kingdom's safety and stability. The Cabinet also expressed thanks to the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) in its fight to contain the cholera epidemic in Yemen and the provision of $66.7 million to the World Health Organization (WHO) and The United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) in this regard. The Cabinet commended the King's approval to adopt a public health policy, reflecting the keenness of the leadership over the health of its citizens. The Cabinet also expressed its appreciation to Germany, represented by Chancellor Angela Merkel for the good organization and management of the activities of the G20 leaders' summit, which discussed numerous issues including international efforts in combating terrorism and extremism. The Cabinet also appreciated the welcome of leaders of the G20 summit countries for the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's hosting G20 Summit in 2020. The Cabinet valued the King's invitation to hold a Saudi-African Summit in the Kingdom at the end of this year 2017 or beginning of next year 2018, as the invitation was conveyed by the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir to the President of the 29th ordinary African Summit held in Ethiopia. At the outset of the session, the King welcomed Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz on the occasion of his selection as Crown Prince and his appointment as Deputy Prime Minister.?é?? King Salman also expressed his appreciation to Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz for all his efforts exerted in the service of the homeland.?é?? In addition, King Salman welcomed Prince Abdulaziz bin Saud bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Minister of Interior, wishing him success in his new post. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah on Monday afternoon.

title: King Salman Chairs Weekly Cabinet's Session
content:
The Cabinet stressed the Kingdom's strong condemnation of the bombings in the Iraqi capital, Baghdad, the series of bombings in the Afghan capital, Kabul, the terrorist attacks that took place in London, expressing condolence to the governments and peoples of Iraq, Afghanistan and Britain on the victims of bombings and terrorist incidents, wishing the wounded a speedy recovery. 
?é?? In line with a noble directive, the Cabinet was briefed by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, on the outcomes of his visit to Russia, meeting with the Russian President Vladimir Putin, discussion of reinforcing Saudi-Russian understanding on the oil accord, led by the Kingdom, which guarantee for the first time, in the history of the region the cooperation of OPEC member states and non-member states, including Russia, in addition to review bilateral relations, in the field of investments and various oil and petrochemical industries and uniting efforts to combat terrorism and extremist thought. 
The Cabinet pointed out that the decision of various sisterly countries to severe diplomatic relations with the state of Qatar was an expression of keenness to preserve the unity of the Arab nation and take stance against Qatari acts, aimed at destabilizing security, in the region, contravening accords, charters, principles of international law and good neighborliness. 
?é?? The Cabinet confirmed that the decision to cut diplomatic and consulate relations with Qatar came emanating from the Kingdom's observing its sovereign rights guaranteed by international law and in protection of its national security against the dangers of terrorism and extremism as the Kingdom has taken this decisive decision due to the grave violations practiced by the authorities in Doha secretly and publicly throughout previous years. This aimed to strike a wedge in the Saudi domestic ranks, instigating people to disobey the State and breach its sovereignty through securing safe havens for numerous terrorist and sectarian groups targeting the stability of the region. The Cabinet reiterated that the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia will remain supportive for the Qatari sisterly people and their security and stability regardless of the hostile practice committed by the authorities in Doha. King Salman began the meeting by briefing the Cabinet on the outcome of his audience with King Abdullah Al-Hussein of Jordan, the contents of a verbal message he sent to Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmed Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, Emir of Kuwait; his meeting with Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Crown Prince of Abu-Dhabi, the contents of the telephone call the monarch received from President Emmanuel Macron of France, as well as his meeting with the Secretary General of the GCC Dr. Abdullatif bin Rashed Al-Zayani. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the weekly Cabinet meeting at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah this evening.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet also lauded the exchange of the memorandum of understanding between the GCC countries and the US to establish a center to dry-up the sources of funding of terrorism. The Cabinet asserted that the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques' announcing and launching of the Global Center for Combating Extremist Ideology "Etidal", in the presence of U.S. President Donald Trump and world leaders. The King also drew the attention to the importance of achieving peace between Palestinians and Israelis, intensifying efforts to resolve the Syrian crisis, refusing to harm the Islamic countries' relations with friendly countries or exploiting Islam as a cover for political purposes fueling hatred, extremism and terrorism as well as religious and sectarian conflicts, citing acts done by the Iranian regime, its affiliated groups and organizations and other terrorist organizations. The Cabinet highlighted the valuable contents of the speech delivered by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques pointing to the King's emphasis on the responsibility of the Arab and Islamic countries to be united in the fight against the forces of evil and extremism and terrorism in all its forms. The Cabinet affirmed that the Kingdom's hosting of the U.S.-GCC Summit and the historically unprecedented Arab-Islamic-American Summit reflects the great posture the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia has internationally enjoyed, as well as its remarkable efforts to enhance security, peace and stability in the region and the world. The King also reviewed historical relations between the two countries and ways of enhancing them in various fields and discussed the latest world developments, They also exchanged of a number of trade agreements and investment opportunities exceeding $280 billion. King Salman said that the historic agreement signed by the GCC with the United States take firm measures against the financing of terrorism; and the announcement of the launch of the Global Center for Combating Extremist Ideology aimed at spreading the principles of moderation. King Salman began by expressing his appreciation to US President Donald J. Trump and leaders of Arab and Islamic countries who participated in the 17th Consultative Meeting of Leaders of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), the U.S.-GCC Summit, and the Arab-Islamic-American Summit hosted by the Kingdom yesterday. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh on this afternoon.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet condemned the terror attack on a development project in Al-Masurha District in Qatif Province. King Salman expressed his trust May 20 Saudi-US Summit will contribute to strengthening and enhancing strategic relations and global security between the two countries.?é?? He also said he is looking forward to the Gulf-US Summit, and the Arab-Islamic-US Summit which he said will enhance greater cooperation with the US.consultative meeting to be held on May 21 at will contribute to consolidating the Gulf solidarity for enhancing security, stability and peace in the region. The King also stressed that the Arab-Islamic-US Summit to be held on the same day will establish a new partnership in confronting extremism and terrorism, disseminating the values of tolerance and coexistence, and enhancing security, stability and cooperation. The King welcomed the upcoming participation of US President Donald J. Trump, and leaders of Arab and Islamic countries in the 17th consultative meeting of leaders of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), the Gulf-US Summit, and the Arab-Islamic-US Summit, being hosted in Riyadh this weekend. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's weekly session at Al-Sallam Palace in Jeddah on this afternoon.

title: King Salman Names New Ministers, Governors, Ambassador to the US
content: Hail Province Governor Prince Saud bin Abdulmohsin has been replaced by Prince Abdulaziz bin Saad and Prince Faisal bin Khalid bin Sultan replaced Prince Mishal bin Abdullah as governor of the Northern Border Province. Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman was named minister of state for energy affairs, Prince Khalid bin Salman was appointed ambassador to the United States, Abdullah bin Amer Al-Sawaha was appointed Minister of Communications and Information Technology, and Dr. Awwad bin Al-Awwad was appointed Minister of Culture and Information. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today issued a number of royal orders, including the appointments of a new ambassador to the United States and ministers of Culture and Information, and Communications and Information Technology.?é?? King Salman removed Civil Service Minister Khaled Al-Arj and ordered an investigation of possible?é??abuse of?é??office. The royal orders also?é??included the appointment of two new governors and a number of deputy governors. King Salman ordered?é??the restoration of?é??financial allowances for civil servants and military personnel that had been suspended under earlier austerity measures.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet stressed the strength of the Saudi economy as the Ministry of Finance has received strong demand from investors for the first international issuance under the sukuk program which confirms the role being played by the Ministry of Finance's Public Debt Management Department in achieving the Kingdom's Vision 2030. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh on Monday afternoon.?é?? King Salman began the meeting by briefing his cabinet on the results of his meetings and talks with President Rodrigo Duterte the Philippines, British Member of Parliament and head of the Saudi-British Group in Parliament Charlotte Leslie, and Chairwoman of the Russian Federation Council Valentina Matviyenko.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet lauded efforts security forces in pursuing terrorists and anyone who tries to undermine the Kingdom's security and threaten its citizens and residents.?é?? The Cabinet commended the security forces for their recent success in raiding a terrorist hideout north of Al-Awamiya in Qatif Governorate and foiling their terror plans. The Council of Ministers renewed the Kingdom's condemnation terrorist attack in Parachinar, Pakistan and expressed its condolences to the families of the victims, Pakistan and its people. The Council of Ministers condemned the declaration by the Israeli occupation authorities to establish a 2,000 new settlement on 241 acres of Palestinian land in the West Bank for the first time in 20 years. The Council reaffirmed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös positions towards the Palestinian cause, including its condemnation of all actions taken by the Israeli occupation authorities and the Kingdom???Ć?Ös repeated calls to the international community to compel Israel to cease all arbitrary and racist measures against the Palestinian people. King Salman also briefed the Cabinet on the results of his meetings with Arab leaders on the sidelines of the summit expressing his pleasure over the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's hosting of the 29th Arab Summit in Riyadh. King Salman began the weekly session by expressing his deep thanks and appreciation to King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan for the warm welcome and generous hospitality accorded to him and the accompanying delegation during his visit to Jordan.?é?? King Salman also expressed his appreciation to the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the King, the Government and people for their great efforts exerted to ensure the success of the 28th Arab Summit Conference. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace this afternoon.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet renewed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös condemnation of the Syria air force bombing of a UN aid convoy, which led to the suspension of aid convoys in Syria.?é?? The Cabinet also expressed its concerns about the Syria war crimes. Then the Cabinet briefed on Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense???Ć?Ös visit to the United States and his meeting with President Donald Trump and senior US officials. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's weekly session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.?é?? At the outset of the session, the King briefed the Cabinet on his Asia tour and referred to his meetings as positive and productive.?é?? King Salman also briefed the Cabinet on the telephone calls with King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa of Bahrain and French President Francois Hollande.

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet reiterated the condemnation of terrorist attacks which took place at the military hospital in Kabul, Afghanistan and a military base in Mali. The Cabinet stressed the decisions of the session, confirming the full commitment and adherence to the Arab Peace Initiative without change, and the need to enable the Palestinian people to exercise their inalienable rights. The Cabinet was then briefed on the latest developments on the Arab and Islamic arenas. The Cabinet lauded the decisions adopted by the 147th session of the Council of Arab Foreign Ministers held in Cairo. In this context, the Cabinet expressed appreciation to the Arab foreign ministers for thanking the Saudi-led Arab Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen in its support of Yemen's legitimacy and people, the resumption of the peace process, and endeavors to stop war and restore the Yemeni state.?é?? The Cabinet also appreciated the humanitarian efforts of the King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSRelief) in providing assistance to the Yemeni people.?é?? The Cabinet also welcomed the Council of Arab Foreign Ministers???Ć?Ös statements unilaterally condemning the coup leaders in Yemen, their violations against the people of Yemen as well as the Iran???Ć?Ös continual interventions that violate the security and stability of Yemen. Crown Prince Mohammed briefed the Cabinet on the contents of the message sent by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah of Kuwait, the telephone call King Salman had with President Joko Widodo of Indonesia, and his meetings in Indonesian.?é?? Crown Prince Mohammad also briefed the cabinet on his meeting with New Zealand???Ć?Ös Foreign Minister Murray MuCully. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif chaired the Cabinet's weekly session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet Session
content: Turing to domestic issues, the Cabinet approved a number of memoranda of understanding and cooperation, including a draft MOU with the Chinese National Space Administration on cooperation regarding Chang E-4 to explore the moon. Following a review of King Salman???Ć?Ös ongoing tour of Asia, the Council reviewed recent regional and international developments. The Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif chaired the Cabinet???Ć?Ös weekly session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.?é?? At the outset of the session, Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif reviewed the success the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz???Ć?Ös visit to Malaysia, Indonesia and Brunei Darussalam.

title: Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet also approved a scientific cooperation agreement with Belarus; authorized King Saud University to discuss a MoU for academic cooperation with Shanghai University in China.?é?? The Cabinet also authorized the Minister of Culture and Information, or his designate, to discuss with Indonesia and Malaysia a program to exchange news between the Saudi Press Agency and Indonesian and Malaysian news agencies. The Cabinet finally adjourned after discussing a number of topics including cooperation agreements between the Kingdom and each of Kyrgyzstan and Russia in combating crime and protection of classified information; approved the memorandum of understanding with Afghanistan on cooperation in judiciary and legal affairs and the memorandum of understanding with Japan in the field of social development. The Cabinet reviewed the latest developments at the regional and international arenas including the resumption of Syrian negotiations in Geneva under the auspices of the United Nations. The Cabinet expressed hope that the negotiations would bring about a comprehensive ceasefire and a political solution to the Syrian crisis based on Geneva Declaration I and the UN Security Council Resolution 2254. The Cabinet was briefed on the outcomes of the visit of Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir to Iraq, where he held talks with a number of Iraqi officials, reiterating the affirmation of the ties binding the two countries. Crown Prince Mohammad briefed the Cabinet on the outcomes of the talks that the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman held in Riyadh during the week with President Mohammed Abdullah Farmajo of Somalia and President Prokopios Pavlopoulos of Greece. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammed bin Naif Saud chaired the Cabinet session Monday afternoon at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet authorized the General Commission for Statistics to conduct the population general census for 2020. The Cabinet approved the incentives and privileges offered to donors who purchase antiquities or masterpieces or special collections displayed in national museums; approved the regulatory provisions of the General Authority for Sports; The Cabinet approved the security cooperation agreement with Djibouti signed in Riyadh on 10/07/1437 AH; approved the memorandum of understanding (MoU) between the Ministry of Interior???Ć?Ös Saudi Financial Investigations Administration (SAFA) and the Financial Investigation Unit (FIU) of India as regards cooperation on the exchange of the investigations related to money laundering, terror financing and related crimes, signed in Riyadh on 25/06/1437 AH; approved MoU for cooperation in the field of renewable energy between King Abdullah City for Atomic and Renewable Energy and China???Ć?Ös National Power Management, signed in Riyadh on 09/04/1437H. The Cabinet finally adjourned after discussing a number of domestic issues. The Council strong condemnation of the murder of Russia's Ambassador to Turkey, Andrey Karlov and the terror attack in Berlin, Germany, that resulted in the death of 12 people.?é?? The Cabinet also the double terrorist bombing of a market in Baghdad and the attack in Istanbul.?é?? The Cabinet offered its condolences to the families of the victims. The Cabinet also reiterated the Kingdom's appreciation to U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry???Ć?Ös statement on the final solution of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, as well as the adoption by Security Council Resolution 2334, which condemns Israeli settlement and calls for stopping it in the occupied Palestinian territories. The Cabinet also discussed the outcome of the 97th OPEC meeting in Cairo and commitment by OPEC members to honor the November 2016 agreement on reducing oil production. The Cabinet welcomed Security Council Resolution 2328 on the deployment of international observers in Aleppo to supervise the evacuation of civilians. The Cabinet was briefed on a number of reports including the emergency meeting of the Executive Committee of Foreign Ministers of Organization of Islamic Cooperation that the massacres being committed in Aleppo, Syrian, can be classified as a war crime and crimes against humanity. King Salman briefed the Cabinet on the results of his two phone calls with King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan and President Abed Rabbo Hadi of Yemen, as well as his two meetings with Palestinian President Mahmud Abbas and Chairman of the Board of Directors of Al-Quds Fund Muneeb Al-Masri, in addition to the messages received from King of Jordan; Emir of Kuwait and President of Comoros. The Cabinet appreciated King Salman???Ć?Ös order to organize a popular campaign for the Syrian people relief of the Syrian people.?é?? King Salman???Ć?Ös directive to allocate SR 100 million ($26.67 million) for the campaign and his personal SR 20 million ($5.33 million) donation.?é?? The Cabinet also express its appreciation to the SR 10 million ($2.67 million) donation by Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif and the SR 10 million ($2.67 million) Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman???Ć?Ös SR 8 million ($2.13 million) to alleviate the sufferings of Syrian people, especially the displaced from Aleppo. The Cabinet began by congratulating King Salman on the second anniversary of his ascension to the throne.?é?? The Cabinet then commended the State???Ć?Ös general budget for the fiscal year 2017; for its sufficient strength to cope with economic and financial challenges. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh on Monday afternoon.

title: Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet has also reviewed a number of issues on its agenda, including the annual report of the general organization for social insurance on a previous fiscal year. The Cabinet issued relevant instructions towards them. The cabinet also approved the appointment of Jamal bin Ali Al-Kishi and Eng. Omar bin Nabil Al-Khudairi as two members of the private sector in the Board of Directors of the Saline Water Conversion Corporation for a period of three years. The Cabinet approved an amendment to Table (2) regarding the joint committees list attached to the Cabinet's decision No. 2 dated 03/01/1428H, so that the Saudi side at the Saudi-Indonesian Joint Committee is chaired by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, and that the Committee shall meet every six months. The Cabinet concluded by approving a memorandum of understanding the Peoples Republic of China on enhancing joint cooperation on the silk road economic belt and the initiative of naval silk road for the 21st century and cooperation on productive energy which was signed in Riyadh on 9/4/1437 H;?é?? authorized the Minister of Islamic Affairs, Call and Guidance, or his deputy, to negotiate a memorandum of understanding in the field of Islamic Affairs with Mauritania, sign it, and forward the final version for taking further procedures; approved a general cooperation agreement Mauritius which was signed in New York on 12/12/1436H; authorized the Minister of Foreign Affairs, or his deputy, to negotiate with Norway and New Zealand draft memoranda of understanding on political consultations, sign them, and submit the final versions for taking further authentication;?é?? authorized the Acting Secretary General of King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives, or his deputy, to discuss with the United Arab Emirate a draft memorandum of cooperation between King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives Saudi Arabia and the National Archive of the Ministry of Presidential Affairs of the United Arab Emirates, sign it, and submit the signed final version. Also praised was the Kingdom's participation in the international conference on the preservation of endangered cultural heritage, held in Abu-Dhabi in the attendance of Prince Khalid Al-Faisal, Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque and Governor of Makkah Region, who led the Kingdom's delegation on behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque. The Cabinet lauded the holding of a number of cultural activities, citing the opening by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques of Trade Roads in the Arab peninsula exhibition, the masterpieces and antiquities of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia across history, held at King Abdulaziz international cultural center in Dhahran, and the king's okay of the exhibition's tour of east Asia following its successful nine tours of Europe and America. To mark the occasion of the International Day for Solidarity with the Palestinian people, the Cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's call for the world community to remain committed towards the rights of Palestinians and retard Israel from its procrastinating policies against them. The Cabinet praised the 7th Bosphorus Summit.?é?? The Cabinet hailed the cooperation agreements in the fields of investment signed by Saudi Arabia and Turkey during the Summit. The Cabinet also praised the cooperation and contribution of the main oil producing countries outside OPEC to the restoration of stability and balance of international petroleum market. The Cabinet was briefed on the outcome of OPEC ministerial meeting which was held last week in Vienna. The Cabinet praised the outcome of the meeting, the decisions that were taken as well as the clear cooperation among all OPEC countries to restore stability in the international oil market by reducing excess supply. The Cabinet commended the outcome of the 35th Meeting of GCC Interior Ministers, expressing hope that the outcome of the meeting will contribute to enhancing the efforts of GCC countries in the field of security. The Cabinet reviewed a number of reports on regional, Arab and international developments, and looked forward to the outcome of meetings the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques will hold with leaders of the United Arab Emirates, the State of Qatar, the Kingdom of Bahrain and the State of Kuwait during his tour that emanates from his keenness to communicate with Gulf Cooperation Council leaders to serve the interests of their peoples and to enhance ties of brotherhood among GCC countries. Crown Prince Mohammad welcomed Minister of Labour and Social Development Dr. Ali bin Nasser Al-Ghafis in his new portfolio, wishing him success. He also thanked the outgoing officials and members for their efforts. The Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques congratulated the Kingdom's Grand Mufti; members of the Senior Scholars Commission; full-time sheikhs, members of the Standing Committee for Fatwa (affiliated to the Senior Scholars Commission); and Speaker and members of Majlis Al-Shura on the royal trust bestowed on them through renewing their memberships to their agencies, wishing them all success. Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz Al Saud chaired the Cabinet's session which was held at Al-Yamamah Palace today.

title: Four Royal Orders Issued
content: Four royal orders were issued today.:

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet approved the accession of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to the membership of The Hague Conference on Private International Law; and approved the organization of the Council of Family Affairs. The Cabinet approved an agreement Tunisia on cooperation in the field of defense, which was signed in Riyadh on 11/3/1437 H.; approved a cooperation arrangement with Brazil in the field of sports, which was signed in Brasilia on 25/6/1436H.; approved the organizational arrangements of the Center for Achievement and Rapid Intervention; and approved a number of arrangements described in detail in Resolution No. 2770 dated 03/06/1436H regarding the rearrangement of services supervised by the Ministry of Finance. The Cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's strong denunciation and condemnation of the terror explosion that ripped the Afghani capital of Kabul, and the terror armed attack which took place in the German city of Munich, leaving tens of innocents killed or injured, expressing the condolence and sympathy of the Kingdom to each of the Republic of Afghanistan and Federal Republic of Germany on the terror victims, wishing the injured quick recovery and calling for concerted international efforts to fight this dangerous plague which is categorically rejected by all divine religions and international norms. The Cabinet welcomed the statement issued by the 25th session of the joint ministerial council between the Gulf Cooperation Council and the European Union in Brussels, underscoring the importance of continuing enhancing relations between them amid the regional challenges to develop into firm and effective bases for regional and international stability and security as well as the strategic importance of close coordination between the two sides. The Cabinet reviewed a number of reports on the developments of issues regionally and internationally, as well as the international efforts regarding a number of issues at the international arena and terror combating. The Cabinet welcomed the convening of the 2nd meeting of the International Coalition for Fighting Daesh held at Andrews Airbase, near the Washington, DC.?é?? Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, led the Saudi delegation to the meeting. At the outset of the session, the Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques briefed the Cabinet on the outcome of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques audience held for King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa, King of Bahrain. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif chaired the Cabinet's session held today at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah.

title: Saudi Arabia Announces Budget for 2016???Ć?"December 28
content: The focus will continue to be on education, health, social services, municipal services, security services, infrastructure and transportation. Expenditures on education and health and social affairs continued to receive the largest shares of the ???Ć?Äbudget. The general budget allocations for major sectors are as follows: education: SR 191.69 billion ($51.11 billion), representing 22.8 percent of FY 2016 total appropriations; health and social development: SR 104.86 billion ($27.96 billion), representing 12.48 percent; municipality services: SR 21.25 billion ($5.67 billion); security services: SR 213.37 billion ($56.9 billion), 25.4 percentage of the budget; infrastructure and transportation: SR 23.90 billion ($6.37 billion), 23.9 percentage of the budget; economic resources: SR 78.12 billion ($20.83 billion); public administration: SR 23.84 ($6.36 billion); and budget support provision: SR 183 billion ($48.8 billion), representing 21.78 percentage of the budget. The Cabinet today approved the state's budget for the new Fiscal Year ???Ć?Ä???Ć?437/1438H (2016). The budget totaled SR 840 billion ($224 billion), a projected a deficit of SR 326.2 billion ($86.99 billion).

title: Saudi Air Defense Forces Intercept a Missile Launched from Yemen
content: The Command of Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen announced today that Saudi Air Defense Forces intercepted a Scud missile launched from Yemen yesterday toward the city of Najran. The command said that the missile was launched at 11 pm from Sana???Ć?Öa and that coalition aircrafts destroyed the missile launch pad.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: On domestic affairs, the Cabinet discussed numerous items, including its appreciation of the Royal Order on forming the first circle for Banking Disputes Committee, the approval of a memorandum of understanding on cooperation in the field of tourism between the Saudi Commission for Tourism and National Heritage and Egypt's Ministry of Tourism, and the discussion and signing of a draft agreement on cooperation in the field of defense between the governments of Saudi Arabia and Tunisia. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's welcome of the signing of the peace agreement in Sukhairat, Moroccan, between various Libyan parties under the auspices of the United Nations. The Cabinet denounced the non-compliance and breach of truce of Houthi militias after the announcement of a cease-fire during peace talks between the Yemeni parties. The Cabinet commended the United Nations Security Council Resolution 2254 on Syria, which sets up the political process to resolve the crisis on the basis of the principles of Geneva Declaration 1 and Vienna 1 and 2 Conferences based on the establishment of a transitional body for governing with full security and political authorities. The Cabinet commended the outcomes of the second meeting of Saudi-Egyptian Coordination Council and the King's directives to increase Saudi investments in Egypt and support transport movement in Suez Canal by Saudi ships. The Cabinet also expressed the Kingdom's solidarity with the Government of Nigeria in confronting terrorism. The Cabinet confirmed the joint statement on the formation of an Islamic alliance to fight terrorism; militarily, intellectually and in the media in coordination with international organizations, and important countries, which includes a group of Islamic states that make up the majority of the Islamic world. The King began by briefing the Cabinet on the telephone call received from the King of Bahrain, the telephone call made to the President of Nigeria, and the results of his talks with the Chairman of Presidency Council of Bosnia and Herzegovina. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh on Monday afternoon.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet was finally briefed on local reports including preparations for the upcoming municipal elections. On domestic issues, the Cabinet commended the approval of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques of launching King Salman Program for Human Resources Development aiming to raise the quality of a government employee's performance and work productivity. The Cabinet condemned the terrorist bombing and assassination of Governor of Aden, as well as the terrorist attack inside a social service institution in the city of St Bernardino, California, calling for concerted international effort to fight terrorism. The Cabinet commended the Kingdom's keenness to solve the Syrian crisis politically and welcomed the Vienna II group???Ć?Ös request to host a conference in Riyadh for Syrian opposition of moderate Syrian opposition to participate in an expanded meeting of the Syrian opposition in Riyadh on December 8- 10, 2015. The Cabinet was briefed on the results of the participation of King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid in the 6th annual conference on effective partnership and exchange of information on humanitarian programs in Yemen. The Cabinet praised the signing of the first meeting's minutes of Saudi-Egyptian Coordination Council in Riyadh including the formation of working teams to support the Council to complete reviewing initiatives, draft agreements, memorandums of understanding and executive programs emanating from Cairo Declaration. The Cabinet highlighted the Kingdom's call in 21st Conference of the States Parties to United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change in Paris on the importance of working to launch the initiative for research and studies of clean energy (technical innovation), encourage support for investments and increase them in these areas. The Cabinet reviewed the Kingdom's emphasis in the Conference of States Parties to the Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons at its 20th session in The Hague on the importance of implementation of Chemical Weapons Convention. It also called for an investigation in Syria based on the UN Security Council Resolution No. 2235 in 2015 on the formation of a joint committee between the Organization and the UN to investigate the use of chemical weapons in Syria. The King began by briefing the Cabinet on his meeting with former King Juan Carlos of Kingdom of Spain then the Cabinet welcomed the leaders of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) for Arab States in their summit at 36th session scheduled to be held in Riyadh, commending achievements of the GCC states. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah palace in Riyadh this afternoon.

title: Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session
content: Finally, the Cabinet discussed a number of local issues and commended the efforts of the Ministry of Interior, represented by Anti-Narcotics National Committee, in the implementation of the national project for the prevention of narcotics Nebras. The Cabinet authorized the President of King Abdullah City for Atomic and Renewable Energy, or his deputy, to discuss a draft agreement on cooperation in the field of peaceful usage for atomic energy the United Kingdom. The Cabinet commended on the King's speech delivered at the working dinner session of the leaders and heads of delegations of the participating countries, denunciating the terrorist bombings that took place in Lebanon and France, emphasizing the international community's efforts to root out world terrorism and terrorists, especially those who take justifications from the teachings of Islam. The Cabinet emphasized the importance of convening the Group of Twenty Summit in view of the world's witnessing of events and critical economic and political circumstances requiring the unification of the global position to confront these issues. The Vice Custodian also briefed the Cabinet on the results of meetings of the Custodian and Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques with a number of leaders of countries who participated in the Summit.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet stressed the importance of the 6th Ministerial meeting of the Carbon Sequestration Leadership Forum (CSLF). The Forum is expected to receive about 250 ministers, officials and experts in the field of energy, environment and sustainable development, and is to be help in Paris on 30 November-11 December 2015 with the obligation for clean energy and reduction of greenhouse gas emissions. On domestic affairs, the Cabinet expressed its strong condemnation of the terrorist bombing of Al-Mash'had Mosque in Najran, stressing that these terrorist attacks only increase the citizens' unity and cohesion. The Cabinet then expressed its sincere condolences to the governments and people of Afghanistan and Pakistan on the victims of the devastating earthquake that struck the two countries last week, also expressing its deep condolences to the Russian Federation's leadership and people on the victims of the Russian plane that crashed over Sinai. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's condemnation of the aggression of Israeli occupation authorities and settlers against Palestinians and Islamic holy sites, and called on the international community to act quickly. The Cabinet was then briefed on the final declaration on the results of the Syria Talks in Vienna as agreed by 17 countries participating in the talks.?é?? The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh on Monday afternoon. King Salman began by briefing the Cabinet on his phone calls with King Abdullah II of Jordan, U.S. President Barack Obama, and President Vladimir Putin of Russia, as well as the results of his talks with Somali President Hassan Sheikh Mahmoud and President of Chad Idriss Deby Itno. The Cabinet was also briefed on King Salman???Ć?Ös meeting with British Foreign Secretary Philip Hammond.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: On domestic affairs, the Cabinet praised Saline Water Conversion Corporation's winning two awards from the World Water Summit, the first in recognition of being the best Saline Water Conversion Company in the world due to its developmental tools adopted by it in the management of its works and operational instruments, and the second award for its desalination plant at Ras Al-Khair due to the modern and leading technologies as well as the unprecedented global operational levels in the efficiency of the plant's design, stressing that the top position of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's superiority in this important strategic industry embodies the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques' support for this vital and important sector. The Cabinet was then briefed on visits by Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir to Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore and Austria. The Cabinet was then briefed on the latest regional and international developments and the quadrilateral meeting with US Secretary of State John Kerry, Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov, and Turkish Foreign Minister Feridun Sinirlioglu to discuss the Syrian conflict. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon. King Salman briefed the Cabinet on his message to Egyptian President Abdel Fattah Al-Sisi, his telephone conversations with Russian President Vladimir Putin, Turkish Recep Tayyip Erdogan, and Afghan President Ashraf Ghani Ahmadzai, as well as the results of his meetings with Bahraini Prime Minister Prince Khalifa bin Salman Al-Khalifa, Ethiopian Prime Minister Hailemariam Desalegn, and US Secretary of State John Kerry.

title: Statement Following the Conclusion of Pensacola Naval Air Station Attack Investigation
content: Finally, close to a million Saudis have come to the US for their education, training and other reasons over several decades. The overwhelming majority of them are law-abiding citizens who view themselves as unofficial ambassadors of the kingdom. Many consider the United States as a second home and treat their American colleagues, classmates and neighbors as an extended part of their family. Thousands of them took to social media to condemn the attacker and to extend their condolences to Americans, as did our leadership and our ambassador to the United States. Saudi Arabia has been at the forefront of the international community???Ć?Ös effort to counter terrorist groups for many years.?é?? The kingdom has used every means at its disposal to counter the men, mindset and money that allow Al Qaeda, ISIS and other terrorist groups to recruit followers and to threaten communities and even entire nations.?é?? In addition, the terrorists who have struck the US and many other nations have also targeted Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös people, leadership, military personnel and even our holiest religious institutions in Mecca and Medina on multiple occasions. Violent extremists continue to pose a serious challenge to the security of the United States, Saudi Arabia and most countries around the world. Terror groups have recruited thousands of people from dozens of countries. The kingdom has worked closely with the US to counter this global threat. It is worth noting that the military training that the US provides to Saudi military personnel has enabled Saudi soldiers, pilots and sailors to fight along their American counterparts and against our common foes. The close cooperation between the two nations on intelligence matters and issues related to counter terrorism has saved the lives of many in the US, Saudi Arabia and elsewhere and has made the world a safer place.?é?? Approximately 28,000 Saudis have undergone military training in the US over the course of several decades without incident. Saudi Arabia has fully cooperated with American investigators looking into the motivations of the attacker. Saudi Arabia will continue to cooperate with US authorities should they require additional information. In addition, the Kingdom has determined that 21 cadets have demonstrated conduct unbecoming of an officer in the Saudi Royal Air Force or Royal Navy and therefore have been unenrolled from their training program in the U.S. military. They will be returning to the Kingdom. The disturbed and radicalized individual who carried out this terrible attack acted alone. He does not represent the hundreds of thousands of Saudis who have lived, studied and trained in the United States over the past several decades, nor does his heinous act represent the values of Saudi Arabia. title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet finally adjourned after reviewing a number of local issues on its agenda. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's strong condemnation of the terrorist bombings that took place in northern Sinai in Egypt and the terrorist attack on a camp for displaced people in Anbar province in Iraq, expressing its condolences to the Egyptian and Iraqi governments, reconfirming the Kingdom's solidarity and stand with Egypt and Iraq and its firm positions against terrorism and extremism. The Cabinet reviewed the joint statement issued by Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Bahrain, and Egypt after receiving the Qatari reply from Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, Emir of Kuwait, renewing its thanks and appreciation to the Kuwaiti Emir for his endeavors and efforts to resolve the crisis with the Qatari government. The Statement was initially directed at the Qatari government to correct its track seeking to destabilize the countries in the region and intervene in their affairs. The Cabinet praised the security forces for thwarting a terrorist act during the holy month of Ramadan to target the Grand Holy Mosque in Makkah and its visitors. It also condemned the terrorist attacks that targeted security patrols in Qatif Governorate which resulted in martyring two security men and injuring others. The Cabinet stressed that security authorities in the Kingdom are capable of confronting such criminal plans and anyone else who tampers with the Kingdom's safety and stability. The Cabinet also expressed thanks to the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) in its fight to contain the cholera epidemic in Yemen and the provision of $66.7 million to the World Health Organization (WHO) and The United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) in this regard. The Cabinet commended the King's approval to adopt a public health policy, reflecting the keenness of the leadership over the health of its citizens. The Cabinet also expressed its appreciation to Germany, represented by Chancellor Angela Merkel for the good organization and management of the activities of the G20 leaders' summit, which discussed numerous issues including international efforts in combating terrorism and extremism. The Cabinet also appreciated the welcome of leaders of the G20 summit countries for the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's hosting G20 Summit in 2020. The Cabinet valued the King's invitation to hold a Saudi-African Summit in the Kingdom at the end of this year 2017 or beginning of next year 2018, as the invitation was conveyed by the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir to the President of the 29th ordinary African Summit held in Ethiopia. At the outset of the session, the King welcomed Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz on the occasion of his selection as Crown Prince and his appointment as Deputy Prime Minister.?é?? King Salman also expressed his appreciation to Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz for all his efforts exerted in the service of the homeland.?é?? In addition, King Salman welcomed Prince Abdulaziz bin Saud bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Minister of Interior, wishing him success in his new post. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah on Monday afternoon.

The artifacts were previously hosted by the Smithsonian Museum in Washington, D.C., the Carnegie Museum in Pittsburgh, and the Museum of Fine Arts in Houston. The Exhibition has also been shown in France, Spain, Russia and Germany. Afterwards, Prince Sultan attended a dinner to celebrate the occasion. The opening ceremony and dinner were attended by Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir; the Kingdom???Ć?Ös Permanent Representative to the United Nations Ambassador Abdullah Al-Mo???Ć?Öallemi; and a number of other officials. During his own speech, Prince Sultan characterized the Exhibition as window allowing viewers to witness the ancient history and prosperous present of a friendly country that is the cradle of Islam. He commended deep, historic relations between the Kingdom and the United States since the meeting of the late King Abdulaziz and former U.S. President Franklin Roosevelt following the World War II. Prince Sultan highlighted the Kingdom???Ć?Ös important position in the global economy and the positive role it has played at the regional and international levels. He concluded, ???Ć?£The masterpieces exhibitions represent an important element in the larger project, which is the King Abdullah Project for the Care of Civilizational Heritage, recently approved by the Cabinet in the Kingdom [that] aims at the rehabilitation of the national heritage.???Ć?The Museum???Ć?Ös Director gave a speech in which he expressed pride in hosting the event, which will introduce visitors to the civilizational heritage of the Arabian Peninsula. The ceremony was also addressed by the Kansas City Mayor and the President of the Board of Trustees of the Museum, Shirley Bush Helzberg, who praised bilateral relations and cooperation between the Kingdom and the United States. President of the Saudi Commission for Tourism and Antiquities Prince Sultan bin Salman bin Abdulaziz and Kansas City Mayor Sly James opened the ???Ć?£Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös Archeological Masterpieces Through the Ages???Ć?Exhibition at the Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art in Kansas City, Missouri last night. As many as 250 artifacts will be displayed during the show???Ć?Ös two-month run.

title: Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet approved the appointments of Dr. Saeed Al-Qarni as Secretary General of the Higher Committee for Administrative Organization at the Institute of Public Administration (15th rank); Ali Yousef as Minister Plenipotentiary at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs; Majed Al-Qahtani as Minister Plenipotentiary at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs; and Abdullah Al-Amer as Security Advisor at the Ministry of Interior (14th rank). Addressing its remaining agenda items, the Cabinet approved the controls required to be taken into account when preparing and studying draft regulations of government agencies; approved a memorandum of understanding in the field of tourism between the Saudi Commission for Tourism and Antiquities (SCTA) and the Azerbaijani Ministry of Culture and Tourism; approved the Kingdom???Ć?Ös contribution to increase the capital of the Islamic Development Bank (IDB) by 764,720 shares with a nominal value per share of 10,000 Islamic Dinars Special Drawing Right (SDR) (one Islamic Dinar is equivalent to one SDR of the International Monetary Fund); authorized the Minister of Communications and Information Technology to sign a draft memorandum of understanding between the Saudi Ministry of Communications and Information Technology and the Finnish Ministry of Housing and Communications for cooperation in the field of communications and information technology; and approved the renewal and appointment of several members of the Board of Directors of the General Organization for Social Insurance (GOSI). Finally, the Cabinet was reassured regarding the efforts of the Ministry of Health to contain coronavirus (MERS) in coordination with national and international agencies and organizations. Turning to the domestic arena, the Cabinet hailed the Saudi Basic Industries Corporation (SABIC) for winning eleventh place among 100 companies from emerging economies for its efforts in the field of transparency and anti-corruption, as ranked by the International Transparency Organization (ITO). It commended the 5th International Exhibition and Conference on Higher Education, which enjoyed the participation of more than 430 universities and higher education institutions and more than 1,000 exhibitors from thirty-six states. The ministers also noted the celebration by the Radio and Television Commission of the 50th anniversary of the start of television broadcasts in the Kingdom and welcomed the activities of the Principles Forum Set by Islamic Sharia held by the Ministry of Justice. Then, the Cabinet reviewed the latest regional and international developments and discussed prospects for cooperation between the Kingdom and numerous friendly countries. The ministers were briefed on the statement issued by the General Secretariat of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) states following the meeting of GCC Foreign Ministers in Riyadh, in which the member states agreed on the importance of implementing the mechanisms of the Riyadh Declaration. The Council also congratulated Algerian President Abdelaziz Bouteflika on his re-election to a fourth presidential term, wishing Algeria steady progress and prosperity. Crown Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah this afternoon. At the outset, the Cabinet congratulated the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz on winning the Cultural Personality Award for the year 2014, awarded by the Sheikh Zayed Book Prize, which recognized the monarch???Ć?Ös humanitarian and cultural achievements at home and abroad.

title: Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet approved the appointments of Dr. Naif Al-Otaibi as Undersecretary for Petroleum Affairs at the Ministry of Petroleum and Mineral Resources (15th rank); Faisal Al-Zaban as Undersecretary for Planning and Follow-up at the Ministry of Transport (14th rank); Abdullah Al-Saleh as Director-General of Investment Development at the Ministry of Municipal and Rural Affairs (14th rank); Ahmad Al-Qadhi as Director General of Administrative and Financial Affairs at the Ministry of Interior; Abdul-Ilah Dalak as Director General of Administrative and Financial Affairs at the General Presidency for Youth Welfare (14th rank); Yahya Khaulani as Director General of Legal Affairs at the Ministry of Petroleum and Mineral Resources (14th rank); and Zuhair Kateb as Director General of the Department of Roads and Transport in Eastern Province at the Ministry of Transport. Addressing its weekly agenda items, the Cabinet approved the amendment of Article (15) of the Military Colleges Regulation, issued by Royal Decree No. (M/50); approved the Headquarters Agreement between the Saudi government and the Monetary Council of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC); authorized the Minister of Interior to sign a draft agreement on cooperation regarding the mutual recognition of driver???Ć?Ös licenses with Tunisia; approved two agreements on the prevention of double taxation and tax evasion with Ethiopia and Luxembourg; authorized the Minister of Transport sign a draft agreement on cooperation with Algeria in the field of maritime transport; and approved a memorandum of understanding between the Saudi Ministry of Health and the Portuguese Ministry of Health on cooperation in the health fields. Turning to domestic affairs, the Cabinet said that the awarding of the 36th King Faisal International Prize exemplifies the Kingdom???Ć?Ös appreciation of culture and science. It also commended the Award and Grant of Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz for the Study and Research of the History of the Arabian Peninsula, which promotes knowledge of Saudi history. After reviewing the latest regional and international developments, the Cabinet commended the Kuwait Declaration and the decisions of the 25th Arab Summit. It stressed the importance of strengthening Arab solidarity in order to achieve progress and prosperity for the Arab peoples. The Cabinet also welcomed the signing of the Comprehensive Peace Agreement between the government of the Philippines and the Islamic Moro Liberation Front (MILF), which will hopefully lead to peace and stability in the southern Philippines. Finally, the ministers expressed hope that the recommendations made at the 3rd Nuclear Security Summit in The Hague will lead to further progress in global efforts to enhance nuclear safety. They noted the initiatives put forth by the Kingdom to prevent nuclear terrorism and reiterated their call for a Middle East zone free of weapons of mass destruction (WMD). Then, the Cabinet was briefed on the results of the visit of U.S. President Barack Obama to Saudi Arabia and his talks with the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz. The ministers commended the deep bilateral relations between the U.S. and the Kingdom and called for enhancing ties in a mutually beneficial way. Crown Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz began the Cabinet session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon by congratulating Second Deputy Prime Minister Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz on his new appointment as Deputy Crown Prince and praying for his success.

title: 36th King Faisal International Prizes Awarded
content: After the ceremony concluded, Crown Prince Salman and the winners attended a dinner banquet. Then, Secretary General of the King Faisal International Prize Dr. Abdullah Al-Saleh Al-Othaimeen announced the winners. The Prize for Service to Islam was awarded to Dr. Ahmed Lemu of Nigeria. The Prize for Islamic Studies was awarded to Professor Abdulwahab Abou Sulaiman of Saudi Arabia. The Prize for Arabic Language and Literature was awarded to Dr. Abdullah Ibrahim of Iraq. The Prize for Medicine was awarded to Professor Yuk Ming Dennis Lo of China and the United Kingdom. The Prize for Mathematics was awarded to Professor Gerd Faltings of Germany. Upon arrival at the Prince Sultan Grand Hall at the Faisaliah Center, the Crown Prince was greeted by Minister of Education Prince Khaled Al-Faisal bin Abdulaziz, who serves as Director General of the King Faisal Foundation and Chairman of the King Faisal International Prize Commission. The ceremony was also attended by Deputy Crown Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz; Nigerian Vice President Namadi Sambo; and a number of other senior officials. On behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, Crown Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz patronized the awards ceremony for the 36th King Faisal International Prize in Riyadh this evening.

title: Crown Prince Opens 29th Janadriyah National Heritage and Culture Festival
content: The events were also attended by Minister of Education Prince Khalid Al-Faisal; Prince Khalid bin Fahd bin Khalid; Second Deputy Prime Minister Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz; Prince Mohammed bin Saad bin Abdulaziz; Prince Saud bin Saad bin Abdulaziz; Prince Faisal bin Abdullah bin Mohammed; Riyadh Governor Prince Khalid bin Bandar bin Abdulaziz; President of the General Commission for Tourism and Antiquities Prince Sultan bin Salman bin Abdulaziz; President of the Royal Commission for Jubail and Yanbu Prince Saud bin Abdullah bin Thinayan; Advisor to the King Prince Dr. Bandar bin Salman bin Mohammed; Deputy Riyadh Governor Prince Turki bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz; Deputy Defense Minister Prince Salman bin Sultan bin Abdulaziz; Chief of the Crown Prince???Ć?Ös Court Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz; Prince Saud bin Salman bin Abdulaziz; and Prince Bandar bin Salman bin Abdulaziz. Upon his arrival, the Crown Prince was received by Minister of the National Guard Prince Meteb bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz. He then met with several guests of honor, including Bahraini Crown Prince Salman bin Hamad Al-Khalifa. After watching the first round of camel races, the Crown Prince handed out prizes to the winners. Then, he and his entourage attended a dinner party. On behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, Crown Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz patronized the opening of the 29th Janadriyah National Heritage Festival today. The annual event, organized by the National Guard, showcases the culture and history of the Kingdom to visitors from around the world.

title: Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet approved the appointments of Nasser Al-Houti as Ambassador at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs; Naif Al-Rashid as Minister Plenipotentiary at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs; Sultan Al-Sultan as Assistant Undersecretary for Security Affairs at the Tabuk Governorate (14th rank); Fahd Al-Huthaili as Assistant Undersecretary for Consumer Affairs at the Ministry of Commerce and Industry (14th rank); and Ahmad Al-Nassar as Director General of the Reports Office at the General Auditing Bureau (14th rank). Addressing its weekly agenda, the Cabinet authorized the Crown Prince to sign a draft agreement on defense cooperation with the Ukraine; authorized the President of the Board of Directors of the King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives (KAFRA) to sign a draft memorandum of cooperation with the Kuwaiti Center for Research and Studies; authorized the President of Youth Welfare to sign a draft cooperation agreement with Kyrgyzstan in the field of youth and sports; and approved the adoption of the final account of the Saudi Fund for Development (SFD) for fiscal year 1432/1433 H. Finally, the Council commended the national consensus reached at the Yemeni National Dialogue Conference. It congratulated Yemen on this historic achievement, wishing its people stability, security, and continued growth. The Cabinet repeated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös strong condemnation of the cowardly terrorist bombings that have recently afflicted Egypt, claiming many innocent lives with the intention of destabilizing that country???Ć?Ös security and stability. It expressed confidence in the ability of the Egyptian government and people to put an end to these un-Islamic, criminal acts. Turning to international affairs, the Cabinet was briefed on the proceedings of the Geneva II Conference, which opened last Wednesday in the Swiss city of Montreux. It reiterated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös call for the formation of a transitional Syrian government, free of the leaders of the Assad regime. The ministers expressed hope that a new government will allow the Syrian people to achieve self-determination away from external interferences, preserving the sovereignty, independence and unity of Syria. The Council expressed pride in the numerous programs held to celebrate Madinah as the capital of Islamic culture for 1434 H. [2013]. It welcomed the results of the 8th Islamic Conference of the Ministers of Culture of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC), particularly the adoption of the draft operational plan for the Initiative of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz for Dialogue Among Followers of Religions and Cultures. Crown Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz began the Cabinet meeting at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon by discussing several reports on local affairs and regional and international developments.

title: Winners of 36th King Faisal International Prize Announced
content: The 36th King Faisal International Prize announcement ceremony was attended by a number of princes, ministers, officials and members of the diplomatic corps accredited to the Kingdom. The Service of Islam category was won by Dr. Ahmad Abu Bakr Limo of Nigeria. The Islamic Studies category was won by Professor Abdulwahab bin Ibrahim Abu Suleiman of Saudi Arabia. The Arabic Language and Literature category was won by Dr. Abdullah Ibrahim Allawi Albosabah of Iraq. The Medicine category was won by Professor Yuk Ming Dennis Lo, a Chinese and British national. Finally, the Science category was won by Professor Gerd Faltings of Germany. Minister of Education Prince Khalid Al-Faisal bin Abdulaziz, who serves as Chairman of the King Faisal International Prize (KFIP) Commission and Executive Director of the King Faisal Foundation, attended a ceremony announcing the winners of the Prize in Riyadh this evening. KFIP Secretary General Dr. Abdullah bin Salih Al-Othaimeen revealed the winners of this year???Ć?Ös Prize, which is offered in five different categories.

title: Prince Sultan Inaugurates Saudi Archeological Masterpieces Expo in Houston
content: Prince Sultan delivered a speech in which he declared, ???Ć?£The history of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia confirms the scope of stability and prosperity witnessed over years, which is a confirmation of the present stability and prosperity achieved by the Kingdom under the leadership of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud, and his wise directives for the constant progress of the Kingdom with confident steps and determination.???Ć?President Saudi Commission for Tourism and Antiquities Prince Sultan bin Salman bin Abdulaziz opened the ???Ć?£Saudi Archeological Masterpieces Through Ages???Ć?Exhibition at the Museum of Fine Arts in Houston, Texas yesterday evening. The exhibit is on the third leg of its tour across the United States.

title: Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet approved the appointments of Fahd Al-Jarallah as Undersecretary for Documentation Affairs at the Ministry of Justice (15th rank); Abdulaziz Al-Ameer as Undersecretary for Central Services at the Ministry of Finance (15th rank); Dr. Abed Al-Saoudon as Undersecretary for Companies Affairs at the Ministry of Petroleum and Mineral Resources (15th rank); Seraj Al-Ghamdi as head of the Khamis Mushait Principality at the Ministry of Municipal and Rural Affairs (14th rank); Abdulaziz Al-Thunyan as Civil Engineer Consultant at the Ministry of Municipal and Rural Affairs (14th rank); Abdullah Al-Sharif as head of the Notary Office at the Ministry of Justice (14th rank); and Saleh Al-Aboudi as Assistant Undersecretary for Financial and Accounts Affairs at the Ministry of Finance (14th rank). Addressing its remaining agenda items, the Cabinet authorized the Crown Prince to sign a draft agreement with Poland for cooperation in the field of defense; approved establishment of diplomatic relations with Trinidad and Tobago at the level of non-resident ambassador; authorized the Minister of Finance to sign draft agreements with Algeria and Kyrgyzstan regarding the avoidance of double taxation and tax evasion; and approved the appointment of Dr. Rashid Al-Zahrani as a member of the Trustees Council of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques???Ć?Prize for Honoring Inventors and the Talented, as a representative of the Technical and Vocational Training Corporation. Finally, the Cabinet listened to a report from Interior Minister Prince Mohammed bin Nayef bin Abdulaziz regarding the results of the field implementation of residence (Iqama) regulations and wished all success to the security bodies. Domestically, the Cabinet thanked the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah for issuing a number of royal decrees approving a set of legal regulations as part of the King Abdullah Project for the Improvement of the Judicial System. The decrees pertained to systems of religious litigation procedures, criminal procedures and pleading procedures before the court of grievances. The Cabinet welcomed the approval by the United Nations General Assembly of a draft resolution submitted by Saudi Arabia on behalf of 66 member states calling for the protection of human rights in Syria. It also condemned the recent terrorist bombings in Beirut, rejecting terrorism in all its manifestations. Then, the Cabinet reviewed a number of international and domestic events, conferences and meetings in which the Kingdom participated, including the 3rd Africa-Arab Summit; the 19th United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change; the ???Ć?£Image of The Other???Ć?Conference held by the King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz Center for Interreligious and Intercultural Dialogue; the 35th King Abdulaziz International Competition for the Holy Quran Memorization, Intonation and Interpretation; the 21st International Islamic Fiqh (Jurisprudence) Academy Conference; and the 2nd Saudi International Conference for Information Technology. Next, the Crown Prince briefed the Cabinet on the audiences held by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz last week with Kuwaiti Emir Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah and Qatari Emir Tammim bin Hamad Al-Khalifa, as well as the Crown Prince???Ć?Ös own meeting with Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas. Noting that the Kingdom has carefully reviewed the agreement reached between Iran and the P5+1 on November 24, the Cabinet characterized the deal as a primary step towards a comprehensive solution to the Iranian nuclear program. It viewed the development as potentially positive, as long as good intentions are proved and further steps lead to a Middle East free of weapons of mass destruction, but with a right to the peaceful use of nuclear energy. Crown Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon. The Council began by reviewing a number of reports on developments in the regional and international arenas.

title: SACTA President Meets with Smithsonian Delegation
content: President of the Saudi Arabian Commission for Tourism and Antiquities (SACTA) Prince Sultan bin Salman bin Abdulaziz met in Jeddah recently with a delegation from the U.S. Smithsonian Foundation. The Smithsonian officials, who are in charge of research centers and museums, are scheduled to visit the Kingdom???Ć?Ös central and eastern regions. During the meeting, Prince Sultan and the delegation discussed ways of promoting cooperation between the SACTA and the U.S. institution in the fields of research and museums. Last year, the Smithsonian Museum in Washington, D.C. hosted the ???Ć?£Saudi Archeological Masterpieces through the Ages???Ć?Exhibition.

title: Prince Sultan Bin Salman Opens Saudi Archeological Exhibition in Pittsburgh
content: During his own remarks, Ambassador Al-Jubeir commended U.S.-Saudi relations, highlighted his country???Ć?Ös achievements under the leadership of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, and noted the Saudi monarch???Ć?Ös leading role in facilitating dialogue between civilizations. After cutting a ceremonial ribbon, Prince Sultan toured the Exhibition and delivered a speech. He noted that the pieces introduce the historical heritage of the Kingdom and demonstrate its openness to world civilizations. Governor Corbett expressed his pride in the selection of Pittsburgh to host the Exhibition, praising the friendship and cooperation between the Kingdom and the United States. President of the Saudi Commission for Tourism and Antiquities (SCTA) Prince Sultan bin Salman bin Abdulaziz and Pennsylvania Governor Tom Corbett opened the Saudi Archaeological Masterpieces through the Ages Exhibition in Pittsburgh this evening. The event was attended by Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir. The Carnegie Museums are hosting the exhibit, which is on the second leg of its U.S. tour. Over 227 relics highlighting the history of the Arabian Peninsula are on display.

title: Saudi Embassy Hosts Annual Passport DC Event
content: Passport DC is organized by Cultural Tourism DC, an independent nonprofit coalition of more than 230 community-based organizations. The month-long event kicked off today with the Around the World Embassy Tour, in which more than forty embassies put on cultural events for the public. Visitors to the Embassy had the opportunity to participate in several activities, including dressing up in traditional Saudi clothing, receiving henna paintings on their hands and having their names written in Arabic calligraphy. In addition, guests were treated to Saudi cuisine and watched a traditional dance performed by a group of students. ???Ć?£The Embassy is pleased to participate in Passport DC for the fifth consecutive year,???Ć?Saudi Ambassador Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said in a statement. ???Ć?£This event gives us the opportunity to share our traditions with local visitors and residents in hopes of providing a greater cultural understanding of Saudi Arabia.???Ć?The Saudi Embassy in Washington, D.C. participated in the sixth annual Passport DC event today, opening its doors to the public and displaying Saudi culture through exhibits, performances and activities.

title: Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet approved the appointments of Fawzi Hakim as Deputy Governor of the Saudi Authority for Standardization, Metrology and Quality (15th rank); Abdulmohsen Al-Yousef as Adviser for Quality at the Saudi Authority for Standardization and Metrology and Quality (15th rank); Khalid Al-Dosari as Mayor of the Qatif Governorate at the Ministry of Municipal and Rural Affairs (14th rank); Noor Baqader as Director General of Educational Information at the Ministry of Education (14th rank); and Ali Al-Osaimi as Director General of Financial Affairs at the Ministry of Interior (14th rank). Addressing its remaining agenda items, the Cabinet amended the service system of officers issued by Royal Decree No. (M/43), dated 8/28/1393, and the service system of servicemen issued by Royal Decree No. (M/9), dated 3/24/1397; approved the System of Practicing Veterinary Medical Occupations in Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) States, adopted by the GCC Supreme Council in its 32nd session; approved the safety regulations for ships with small cargoes, adopted by the GCC Supreme Council in its 32nd session; authorized the Minister of Water and Electricity to sign a draft Memorandum of Understanding with Egypt in the field of electrical interconnection; and assigned the Ministry of Commerce and Industry with a list of powers regarding the imposition of sanctions and fines for municipal violations. On the domestic front, the Cabinet praised the National Guard for organizing the 28th Janadriyah National Festival for Heritage and Culture. They welcomed the guests of the Festival, which showcases Saudi culture and provides writers and intellectuals a forum to express their views. Then, the Cabinet reviewed a number of reports on regional and international events, including the latest developments in the Palestinian territories and Syria. The ministers affirmed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös interest in further developing its relations with Lebanon and expressed hope that the assignment of Tammam Salam to form a new government will contribute to Lebanese stability and prosperity. Crown Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz began the Cabinet meeting at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon by expressing, on behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, his appreciation to all the foreign leaders and officials who offered their condolences on the death of Prince Badr bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud.

title: 28th Janadriyah Festival Opens Doors to Visitors
content: The Janadriyah Festival will close its doors on April 19. This year, visitors will encounter approximately three hundred examples of traditional crafts, more than twenty local folkloric troupes, and over sixty exhibits set up by governmental and private bodies. One of Festival???Ć?Ös central events is the camel race, which will include approximately 1,200 participants. The annual event was launched in 1405 H. [1985] with the support of Commander of the National Guard Prince Miteb bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz, who serves as Chairman of the Festival???Ć?Ös Higher Committee. The 28th Janadriyah National Festival for Heritage and Culture opened its doors today, receiving 300,000 visitors. During its twenty-seven year history, the Festival, organized by the National Guard, has presented the history, culture, and heritage of the Kingdom in a two million square meter venue. It has hosted nearly seventy-eight lectures, two hundred and twenty-nine cultural symposia, and fifty-six poetic events attended by more than 5,000 writers and intellectuals from the Kingdom and abroad, in addition to printing three hundred and forty-nine books on Saudi history and heritage.

title: Saudi Arabia to Host G20 Summit in 2020
content: Head of the Saudi Delegation to the G20 Summit in Hamburg, Dr. Ibrahim Al-Assaf, announced today that of Saudi Arabia will host the G20 Leaders' Summit in 2020 under the directives of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz.

title: Saudi Arabia to Participate Tomorrow in G20 Summit in Hamburg
content: Saudi Arabia will participate in the Group of Twenty (G20) meeting in Hamburg in Germany.?é?? On behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, Dr. Ibrahim Al-Assaf, Minister of the State and Member of the Cabinet, will lead the Kingdom's delegation to the summit.

title: Joint Saudi-Ukrainian Military Transport Aircraft Made Its Maiden Flight
content: Major General (retired) Ali Al-Ghamdi, President of the Aviation Technology Company, said the Saudi industrial rate for the Antonov AN-132 will start with 35 percent and will gradually reach 100 percent. The Antonov AN-132 is a joint venture between the King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology (KACST) and Ukraine's Antonov State Company.?é?? The joint venture is part of the Kingdom???Ć?Ös Vision 2030 to localize 50 percent of military spending. The joint Saudi-Ukrainian military transport aircraft Antonov AN-132 today made a successful maiden flight over the Ukraine.?é?? The plane was piloted by retired Saudi Air Force Major General Mohammed Ayash and a crew of Ukrainian pilots and technicians.

title: GCC Ministerial Council Concludes 142nd Session in Riyadh
content:
