As part of a plan to expand alternative energy projects in the Kingdom, the Saudi Electricity Company and King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology (KACST) on May 18 kicked off the first project by installing solar panels at two schools in Riyadh.

title: Al-Naimi Expects Oil Market to Balance and Prices to Stabilize
content: The minister dismissed criticism of the Kingdom???Ć?Ös role in the drop in oil prices. ???Ć?£During periods of rapid price movement, up or down, there is often a frenzy of commentary ascribing various bizarre theories and motives ???Ć?about collusion or conspiracy ???Ć?to OPEC and to major producers, most notably Saudi Arabia. With the recent price drop, OPEC and Saudi Arabia have yet again been maliciously ???Ć?and unfairly ???Ć?criticized for what is, in reality, a market reaction. Some speak of OPEC???Ć?Ös ???Ć?Öwar on shale???Ć? others claim ???Ć?śOPEC is dead.???Ć?Theories abound. They are all wrong.???Ć?Minister Al-Naimi added that ???Ć?£going forward, I hope and expect supply and demand to balance and for prices to stabilize." Saudi Arabian Oil Minister Ali al-Naimi said he expects the oil market will stabilized. In a speech to the German-Arab Friendship Association in Berlin today, Minister Al-Naimi said Saudi Arabia is committed to helping balance the oil market, but that it is a global issue. ???Ć?£Achieving market stability remains our goal. We will never be able to curb volatile oil market investment cycles, but perhaps we can work to moderate them for the benefit of all producers. It is vital that all producing countries ???Ć?OPEC and non-OPEC ???Ć?continue to focus on long-term common objectives of ensuring oil market stability and a sustainable future for both oil producers and consumers. Going forward, I hope and expect supply and demand to once again start to balance, and for prices to stabilize.

title: KSrelief Provides Yemen $615 million in Assistance
content: Since its launch, the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) has implemented 139 projects at a cost of $615,839,175 across Yemen in a number of areas, including emergency relief food and medical supplies; project to prevent disease outbreaks and reduce child mortality; promote agricultural development and improve water supplies.?é?? KSrelief also provided support?é??for Yemeni refugees in Djibouti and Somalia. The projects were implemented with support with national and international agencies.

title: KSrelief Continues Distributing Food Baskets and Medical Supplies in Yemen
content: During Ramadan, KSrelief provided $75 million and 550 tons of medical supplies to prevention and eradicate cholera in Yemen. This comes as part of more than 133 relief projects implemented by the Riyadh-based Center. Also noteworthy that during the holy month of Ramadan, the Center provided 550 tons of medicines and medical supplies for cholera control. The Center also distributed 45 tons of medicines, medical supplies and equipment to government hospitals in Aden, Taiz, Marib, Jouf and Sayoun. The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) in Yemen continues distributing 17,174 food baskets in the governorate of Al-Baida, as well as 20,900 food baskets in the governorate of Maareb; benefiting 10,3044 people.

title: KSrelief Distributes 50,000 Food Baskets in Hadramout
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) launched last Sunday the distribution of 50,000 food baskets in 16 districts in Hadramout Governorate to benefit 300,000 people.?é?? This assistance comes within multiple projects being carried out by the Center in Yemen.

title: KSrelief Distributes Relief Aid in Yemeni Governorates
content: The assistance provided included 650 tents, 17,700 blankets and 2,950 carpets and rugs.?é?? This assistance comes within multiple projects being carried out by the Center in Yemen. The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) distributed various relief aid in the Yemeni governorates of Aden, Lahj and Hadramout.

title: KSrelief Delivers 72 Tons of Dates to WFP in Chad
content: The shipment is part of the Kingdom's contribution to the WFP, and came within the Kingdom's humanitarian role to support other countries. The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) has delivered 72 tons of dates to the World Food Program (WFP) in Chad.

title: KSrelief Distributes Over 19,000 Food Baskets in Myanmar
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) competed the distributed more than 19,000 food baskets in the Rakhine Arakan in Myanmar. The aid is expected to benefit over 116,000 people.

title: Saudi Arabia Donates $66.7 Million to Combats Cholera in Yemen
content: Saudi Arabia has announced a donation of $66.7 million to the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF), the World Health Organization (WHO) and their partners in response to the cholera outbreak in Yemen.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Council of Ministers renewed the Kingdom's condemnation of the terrorist acts that took place in Egypt and Somalia, and the terrorist assault on worshippers leaving the Finsbury Park Mosque in London yesterday, as well as the attack on security forces inn Champs-Elys?ā??es in Paris yesterday, expressing the Kingdom's firm rejection of all forms of terrorism and extremism. On the recent Qatif terrorist attack, the cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's keenness on fighting terrorism, protecting its homeland security. It also noted the efforts of the naval forces in confronting three boats that entered the Kingdom's territorial waters. Moreover, it considered the bombing of the three trucks belonging to King Salman Relief and Humanitarian Action Center while preparing for distribution of food aid in the city of Marib as a threat to humanitarian work by the Houthis. King Salman briefed the council on the results of his meeting with Pakistani President Mamnoon Hussain, Yemeni President Abdurbo Mansour Hadi, his talks with the Iraqi Prime Minister Dr. Haider Al-Abbadi and the phone calls received from Russian President Vladimir Putin, British Prime Minister Teresa May and Moroccan Minister of Foreign Affairs and Cooperation Nasser Bourita. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the meeting held by the Council of Ministers, on Monday evening at Al-Safa Palace in Makkah.

title: KSrelief's First Shipments of Anti-Cholera drugs Arrived in Yemen
content: The first shipment anti-cholera drugs donated by the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) to treat the cholera epidemic arrived in Aden.?é?? The aid contains 550 tons of medicines and medical supplies, and is part of the medical convoy that the Center recently conducted to address the cholera epidemic in Yemen.

title: KSrelief to Distribute Breaking-Fast Meals in Somalia, Yemen, and Myanmar
content: The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSrelief) launched on Monday a program to distribute 569,340 breaking-fast meals in Somalia during the holy month of Ramadan to ease the sufferings of the Somali people affected by drought in the previous years. The Center also launched a breaking-fast program in Abyan Governorate in Yemen to be distributed to 234,000 beneficiaries and also started a project in Myanmar. This project is an extension of 202 humanitarian and relief projects offered by the Center, which were implemented through 103 partners in 37 countries on four continents.

title: KSrelief Signs Two Fasting Programs in Syria and Myanmar
content: The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSrelief) signed two projects with Turkey's Humanitarian Relief Foundation (IHH) to provide fasting meals in Syria and Myanmar during the holy month of Ramadan.?é?? The project will serve more than 296 thousand beneficiaries.

title: KSrelief Signs Project to Assist Earthquake and Flood Victims in Tajikistan
content: The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center (KSrelief) inspected the projects implemented in Tajikistan to assist earthquake and flood victims. It also delivered 50 tons of dates as assistance to the government of Tajikistan.

title: KSrelief delivers Saudi gift of 100 tons of dates to Burkina Faso
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Action (KSrelief) handed over on Thursday, a Saudi gift of 100 tons of dates to Burkina Faso.

title: KSrelief Delivers 70 Tons of Dates in Sudan
content: The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSrelief) has delivered 70 tons of dates to the World Food Program (WFP) in Sudan.

title: KSrelief Delivers 98 Tons of Dates to the World Food Program in Burkina Faso and Benin
content: The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center (KSrelief) delivered over 48 tons of dates to the World Food Program (WFP) in Burkina Faso and 50 tons of dates to the Republic of Benin yesterday.

title: KSrelief signs Project to Combat the Cholera Epidemic in Yemen
content: The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSrelief) signed in Riyadh today a $8.3 million program with the World Health Organization (WHO), to combat the cholera epidemic in Yemen.

title: KSrelief Provided Yemen over $600 Million in Assistance
content: Since its launch, the Riyadh-based King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSrelief) has spent over $602 million on assistance projects in 130 governorates. ?é??Those include projects included humanitarian aid, shelter, environmental and sanitation programs, and support for agriculture and water programs.

title: KSrelief Delivers 50 Tons of Dates to Benin
content: The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSrelief) delivered 50 tons of dates to the Benin on yesterday as a gift given by the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

title: KSrelief Aims to Eliminate Cholera Epidemic in Yemen
content: The Center also cooperates with international organizations such as the World Health Organization and UNICEF and the International Medical Corps. The Center has an ongoing contact with the rest of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) members through the GCC Aid Coordination Office and the members of the Arab coalition to implement urgent programs and develop a plan to contain the epidemic and to protect the Yemeni people from the risks and consequences. In cooperation with the United Arab Emirate???Ć?Ös Ministry of Health, the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center (KSrelief) has set up an urgent response team to develop rapid intervention programs, diagnosis, treatment and control and work on prevention of the cholera epidemic.

title: KSrelief's Clinics Provide Services to Yemeni Refugee in Djibouti
content: King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Work (KSRelief) clinics in Djibouti provided medical services to 2,560 Yemenis.?é?? On overage, the clinics treat about 900 cases.

title: KSRelief Delivers 1,200 Tons of Dates to WFP in Jordan, Bangladesh, Algeria and Senegal
content: The Riyadh-based King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Work (KSRelief) has provided today 1,200 tons of dates World Food Program (WFP) in?é??Jordan, Bangladesh, Algeria and Senegal.?é?? KSRelief delivered 400 tons of dates the WFP in Jordan, 550 tons to the WFP in Bangladesh, 200 tons to the WFP in Algeria, and 100 tons to the WFP in Senegal.

title: Kingdom Donates $10 Million for Somalia
content: Prince Mohammed bin Nawaf bin Abdulaziz, Saudi ambassador to the United Kingdom announced today at the International Conference in Support of Somalia, in London, a $10 million donation.?é?? Prince Mohammed said the $10 million donation brings the Kingdom???Ć?Ös assistance to Somalia to $1.2 billion.

title: King Salman Relief Center Delivers 878 Tons of Dates to Pakistan
content: The Riyadh-based King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Work (KSRelief) has provided 878 tons of dates to the World Food Program (WFP) in Pakistan.

title: KSRelief Delivers 100 Tons of Dates to Mauritania
content: The Riyadh-based King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Work (KSRelief) has provided 100 tons of dates to Mauritania.

title: Kingdom Hosting Some 900 Thousand Yemeni and Syrian Displaced Person
content:
Dr. Al-Rabeeah expressed unease over the difficulty of delivering humanitarian aid through the Houthi-controlled Port of Hodeidah, which he accused confiscating and blocking of passage of aid.?é?? Dr. Al-Rabeeah added that little aid that the Houthis allow to pass is stolen.?é?? He accused the Houthis of using humanitarian aid as a political weapon againt the civilian population for political gains and urged humanitarian relief organizations to take advantage of other safe crossings to ensure delivery of aid, especially in areas controlled by the Houthis. In addition to providing assistance to Syrian and Yemenis, KSRelief is also active in 37 countries.?é?? To date, KSRelief has provided over $700 million in humanitarian assistance globally, including Iraq and Somalia. 
Dr. Al-Rabeeah stressed that the Kingdom is providing humanitarian assistance all Yemenis through KSRelief???Ć?Ös 127 centers throughout the country, including areas under Houthis control, in collaboration with 81 international and local partners. 
?é?? Speaking at a press conference in?é??Ottawa, Canada,?é??Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabeeah the Supervisor General of the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center (KSRelief) added that they Yemeni and Syrians are granted free medical services, free movement, the right to seek work and allowed to enroll their children in public schools.?é?? Currently 114,000 Syrians and 285,000 Yemeni children are reenrolled in public schools in the Kingdom.?é?? Dr. Al-Rabeeah added that KSRelief is also supporting Yemeni refugees in Djibouti and Somalia. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is host to over 600,000 Yemeni and over 290,000 Syrian refuges, which the Kingdom refers to as guests.

title: Royal Court: King's Efforts on Al-Aqsa are Culminated With Success
content: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia affirms the right of Muslims in Al-Aqsa Holy Mosque and the performance of their worship in it with ease and tranquility. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman stressed the need for the return of calm at Al-Aqsa Mosque and its surroundings, the respect for the sanctity of the place, and that Muslims must return to enter the mosque and perform worship in it with all security, tranquility and peace effective from today. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia emphasizes the importance of achieving a just and comprehensive peace for the Palestinian cause in accordance with the Arab peace initiative, the vision of a two-state solution and the relevant resolutions of the international legitimacy. In reference to the events that took place at Al-Aqsa Holy Mosque during the last period, the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has held necessary contacts with a number of leaders of the world over the past few days.?é?? The Government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia has also contacted the Administration of the United States of America to exert its efforts for not closing Al-Aqsa Mosque in front of Muslims, not preventing them from performing their duties and prayers in it, and canceling restrictions being imposed on the entry to the Mosque. These efforts have been culminated with success today and in a way that contributes to restore stability and tranquility to worshipers and preserve their dignity and security. The Royal Court issued today the following statement:

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Meets with Commander of the CENTCOM
content: The Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammed bin Salman met at his office at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah this evening with the Commander of the US Central Command General Joseph Fotel.?é?? During the meeting, they reviewed areas of bilateral cooperation, particularly in the military aspect, as well as the joint efforts in fighting terrorism and combating extremism, in addition to the latest developments in the region.

title: King Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet stressed the statement issued by the emergency session of the Arab League on the latest Israeli developments and violations in Al-Quds and Al-Aqsa Mosque, reiterating the Kingdom's condemnation and denunciation of the measures taken by Israeli authorities at Al-Aqsa Mosque and its closure before worshippers, and its call on the international community to shoulder its responsibilities towards the continuation of the Israeli aggression against the Palestinian people. The Cabinet also renewed the Kingdom's full support taken by Kuwait towards the Iranian diplomatic mission after a judicial ruling on the so-called "Abdali Cell" and the participation of Iranian parties in helping and supporting the cell's members. The Cabinet welcomed the commendation included in the International Monetary Fund Agency???Ć?Ös (IMF) report regarding the trends of the Kingdom's national economy and the progress achieved so far in implementing the Kingdom's Vision 2030, described by the Fund as a "Bold Reform" program. The Cabinet appreciated the security agency???Ć?Ös killing of three wanted terrorists in Al-Qatif governorate who were involved in many terrorist crimes; and the seizure of their possession of explosive materials, weapons and ammunition, reiterating that the security authorities are able to deter anyone who seeks to harm the security and stability of the Kingdom. The Cabinet discussed a number of topics on local affairs and reviewed reports on latest developments at regional, Arab and international arenas and expressed its appreciation to the King for issuing a number of royal orders to promote some of the state's most efficient figures. At the outset of the session, the King briefed the Cabinet on the results of his meeting with Sudanese President Omar Hassan Al-Bashir, his talks with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan, and the phone call made to President Emomali Rahmon of Tajikistan. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah this afternoon.

title: King Salman Holds Talks With Turkish President Hold Talks in Jeddah
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz met today at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah Turkey's President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.?é?? The talks addressed bilateral relations as well as the developments in the region in addition to the efforts being exerted to counter terrorism.?é?? Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense held a separate meeting with President Erdogan.

title: Crown Prince Meets Russian President Envoy
content: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, met in his office in Jeddah on Wednesday evening with Russian President's special envoy on Syria Alexander Lavrentiev. During the meeting, they discussed bilateral relations and developments in the region.

title: Foreign Minister Meets with EU Officials
content: Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir held a number of meetings in Brussels today with EU Counter-terrorism Coordinator Gilles de Kerchove, Deputy Secretary General for Political Affairs - Political Director for European External Action Service Jean-Christophe Belliard and European Parliament Member Rachida Dati.?é?? During the meetings, issues of common interest were discussed.

title: Kingdom Expresses Support for Kuwait???Ć?Ös Against Iranian Diplomatic Mission
content: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed the Kingdom's support of all the measures taken by Kuwait against Iran's diplomatic mission in Kuwait.?é?? This comes as a court verdict was issued condemning members of Al -Abdali terrorist cell, who were found to have received aid and support from Iran.

title: King Salman Receives Sudanese President
content: President Al-Bashir arrived in Jeddah earlier in the day to perform Umrah. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah today Sudanese President Omar Hassan Al-Bashir.?é?? During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two countries and the latest developments in the region.?é?? Later, Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, met with President Al-Bashir in Jeddah.

title: Kingdom Continues Efforts to Lift Sanction on Sudan
content: Al-Jubeir added that the Kingdom is working with Sudan to improve Sudan's relations with the United States, lift sanctions imposed on Sudan and lift Sudan from the list of state sponsors of terrorism. In this regard he said, "We believe that Sudan has achieved significant progress to reach lifting these sanctions and lift the name of Sudan from the list of state sponsors of terrorism, and we continue in our efforts to coordinate with Sudan and others in the region to find a solution to this situation, so that Sudan can return to its normal state and focus on development, economy and prosperity.???Ć?Speaking at a joint press conference with Sudanese Foreign Minister Ibrahim Ghandour, Minister Al-Jubeir said, "The two countries are also key partners in the Islamic alliance to confront terrorism and extremism, and partners in the coalition to support legitimacy in Yemen, and the two countries are partners in working to counter terrorism and extremism ..." Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said that the talks between the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman and Sudanese President Omar Hassan Al-Bashir dealt with issues of the region, bilateral relations and confronting the scourge of terrorism and extremism.

title: Foreign Ministers of Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Egypt and Kuwait meet with US Secretary of State
content: Saudi Arabia's Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir, Egypt's Minister of Foreign Affairs Sameh Shukri, Bahrain's Minister of Foreign Affairs Sheikh Khalid bin Ahmed bin Mohammed Al Khalifa, UAE's Minister of State for Foreign Affairs Dr. Anwar bin Mohammed Gargash and Kuwait's Minister of State for Cabinet Affairs Sheikh Mohammed Abdullah Al-Mubarak Al-Sabah met in Jeddah today with US Secretary of State Rex Tillerson.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed all aspects of the crisis with the State of Qatar.

title: Crown Prince Meets with US Secretary of State
content: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, met in Jeddah today with US Secretary of State Rex Tillerson.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed means of enhancing bilateral relations as well as the developments in the Middle East and joint efforts in the fight against terrorism and extremism.?é?? The meeting was also attended by Prince Khalid bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Saudi Ambassador-designate to the US; and Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet finally adjourned after reviewing a number of local issues on its agenda. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's strong condemnation of the terrorist bombings that took place in northern Sinai in Egypt and the terrorist attack on a camp for displaced people in Anbar province in Iraq, expressing its condolences to the Egyptian and Iraqi governments, reconfirming the Kingdom's solidarity and stand with Egypt and Iraq and its firm positions against terrorism and extremism. The Cabinet reviewed the joint statement issued by Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Bahrain, and Egypt after receiving the Qatari reply from Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, Emir of Kuwait, renewing its thanks and appreciation to the Kuwaiti Emir for his endeavors and efforts to resolve the crisis with the Qatari government. The Statement was initially directed at the Qatari government to correct its track seeking to destabilize the countries in the region and intervene in their affairs. The Cabinet praised the security forces for thwarting a terrorist act during the holy month of Ramadan to target the Grand Holy Mosque in Makkah and its visitors. It also condemned the terrorist attacks that targeted security patrols in Qatif Governorate which resulted in martyring two security men and injuring others. The Cabinet stressed that security authorities in the Kingdom are capable of confronting such criminal plans and anyone else who tampers with the Kingdom's safety and stability. The Cabinet also expressed thanks to the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) in its fight to contain the cholera epidemic in Yemen and the provision of $66.7 million to the World Health Organization (WHO) and The United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) in this regard. The Cabinet commended the King's approval to adopt a public health policy, reflecting the keenness of the leadership over the health of its citizens. The Cabinet also expressed its appreciation to Germany, represented by Chancellor Angela Merkel for the good organization and management of the activities of the G20 leaders' summit, which discussed numerous issues including international efforts in combating terrorism and extremism. The Cabinet also appreciated the welcome of leaders of the G20 summit countries for the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's hosting G20 Summit in 2020. The Cabinet valued the King's invitation to hold a Saudi-African Summit in the Kingdom at the end of this year 2017 or beginning of next year 2018, as the invitation was conveyed by the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir to the President of the 29th ordinary African Summit held in Ethiopia. At the outset of the session, the King welcomed Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz on the occasion of his selection as Crown Prince and his appointment as Deputy Prime Minister.?é?? King Salman also expressed his appreciation to Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz for all his efforts exerted in the service of the homeland.?é?? In addition, King Salman welcomed Prince Abdulaziz bin Saud bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Minister of Interior, wishing him success in his new post. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah on Monday afternoon.

title: Saudi Arabia to Host G20 Summit in 2020
content: Head of the Saudi Delegation to the G20 Summit in Hamburg, Dr. Ibrahim Al-Assaf, announced today that of Saudi Arabia will host the G20 Leaders' Summit in 2020 under the directives of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz.

title: Saudi Arabia to Participate Tomorrow in G20 Summit in Hamburg
content: Saudi Arabia will participate in the Group of Twenty (G20) meeting in Hamburg in Germany.?é?? On behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, Dr. Ibrahim Al-Assaf, Minister of the State and Member of the Cabinet, will lead the Kingdom's delegation to the summit.

title: In a Joint Press Conference, Foreign Ministers of Saudi Arabia, Egypt, UAE, Bahrain Condemn Qatar's Support for Terrorism, Extremist Groups
content: He made these remarks during a joint press conference held here today, indicating that despite the four countries' patience, Qatar had not shown any interest in its brothers or surroundings.?é?? ???Ć?£Doha has no interest in its brethren, nor its surroundings. It is interested in extremism, instigation, subversion and terrorism," Sheikh Al-Nahyan added.?é?? He noted that the goal of all countries of the world is to provide better opportunities to their respective peoples to grow, prosper and lead a stable and secure life. He regretted the fact that for the past two decades, Doha took the path of destruction and chaos. For his part, Sheikh Abdullah bin Zayed Al-Nahyan, Minister of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation said that the current state of separation from Qatar will continue until it changes its course. The foreign minister raised what he termed as some sort of tolerance for extremists in Europe where they are free to work under the pretext of freedom of opinion. He expressed belief that some European countries started to be aware that some terrorists and extremists are practicing hatred address which directly leads to violence. He concluded that such policies should be reconsidered. Minister Al-Jubeir said it is impossible to give room for a non-tolerance policy. He explained that we don't accept that Qatar, a GCC and Arab League ally, to host an air base from which aircraft fly to attack Daesh (ISIS) and at the same time it harbors and finances terror groups and networks and ignores those who recruit terror-prospect youths.?é?? He denied taking the announced measures to harm Qatar. Instead, we want to help it as this goes in the benefit of Qatar, the region, and the world. He said that Saudi Arabia is on the world's anti-terror front platform, recalling that it has hosted an international terror combat conference in which more than 50 countries took part, issuing a recommendation to establish an international center for combating terrorism under the umbrella of the United Nations. He disclosed that terrorists who started their attacks against the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia long ago, were aiming at controlling Makkah and Al-Madinah, Muslims two major holy cities, to address Muslims of the world from there. He urged everybody "to help us in this war". The minister stated that Saudi Arabia has adopted a new approach to address the issue of terrorism intellectually, citing changes in education, media and information, public dialogue and rehabilitation programs, cultural programs and establishing anti-terror and extremism centers abroad.?é?? He stressed the need for an international cooperation, in terms of money and mentality, to fight the phenomenon as imperative conditions for success. Minister Al-Jubeir lauded the determination and will of the four countries in their fight against terrorism, citing that the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia has succeeded in overturning the situation from the defensive to the offensive and pre-emptive. Also we have built successful relations with some countries of the world, enabling the Kingdom to unveil several terror plots against foreign countries.?é?? Minister Al-Jubeir cited the cargo plane bomb plot.?é?? He said the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia have imposed a money-laundering monitor system which helped discover terror-oriented bank transactions, barred charities from sending money abroad, enacted terror money transfers, and indicted many terrorists who took part in terrorist acts abroad. Some of them were imprisoned for terror and extremism financial support accusations, the minister of foreign affairs said. Those who want to organize terrorism, spread hatred and interfere in the affairs of others, these things are rejected by the international community and not only the Arab quartet, which have taken these actions because they have suffered for many policies carried out by Qatar, he added. On the Turkish position, he said that Ankara told us that it is neutral, and we hope that it will continue to be neutral, pointing that the crisis is related to the Gulf Cooperation Council member states. For any country dealing with Iran, the result is very negative, he asserted, as there is no interest in dealing with Iran, indicating that Iran is a fringe state and is considered almost out of international law and an isolated country in the world, so it is not surprising that Iran is trying to get closer to Qatar, Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir commented. On the situation of the support of both Turkey and Iran to Qatar, which finances and harbor terrorism.?é?? Minister Al-Jubeir said that Iran is the first state sponsoring terrorism in the world, for it supports any state sponsoring terrorism in the world. As for the coming procedures, there is continuous consultation, he indicated, and we have the sovereign right to take any action that is consistent with international law.?é?? ???Ć?£We will consider these matters and consult before announcing them,???Ć?he stressed. Al-Jubeir pointed out that there is political and economic boycott, and it will continue until Qatar changes its policies for the better. This is not only the requirement of the four countries, but it is the demand from the international community, as a whole," he laid emphasis. In response to a question on the existence of economic measures to be taken after the rejection of the requests of the four countries, he said that the joint statement of the four countries is clear, and this statement coincided with the response we received from Kuwait, but is not a response to it, adding that Saudi Arabia and the three sister countries have taken action against Qatar. ???Ć?£We have the sovereign right to make decisions consistent with international law,???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said.?é?? He said the measures taken by Bahrain, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, and the UAE against Doha is because of its support for terrorism and interference in the affairs of the region and spreading hatred.?é?? The minister stressed the need to stop such efforts by Qatar. Saudi Minister of Foreign Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said that the boycott of the State of Qatar will continue until Doha adjusts its position.?é?? He added that additional steps will be taken in time after consultation with Egypt, Bahrain and the UAE. Foreign ministers of Saudi Arabia, Egypt, the United Arab Emirates and the Kingdom of Bahrain affirmed, in a joint press conference held at the Liberation Palace in Cairo today the necessity of combating terrorism and drying up its sources, stressing that countries that support extremism and terrorism will not be tolerated.

title: Crown Prince Receives Phone Call from British Prime Minister
content: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman received today a telephone call from the British Prime Theresa May. The two sides stressed the bilateral relations, in the light of the Kingdom Vision 2030 and the historical ties binding the two friendly countries.?é?? They also reiterated the significance of fighting terrorism and extremism in the region. The British Prime Minister also asserted that Qatar has to clearly commit itself to that and to cooperate with the Gulf states.

title: King Salman Receives Phone Call from US President Trump
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received today a phone call from US President Donald Trump.?é?? During the telephone call, they reviewed the historical relations between the two countries and discussed latest developments in the region and the world. They also discussed joint efforts to combat terrorism, the drying up of its sources and confronting the countries that stand behind financing terrorism, as well as enhancing the joint action between the two countries to combat extremism.

title: Saudi Foreign Minister: Demands on Qatar to Stop Funding Terrorism are Non-Negotiable
content: ???Ć?Ä???Ć?£It was an issue that has been building up, and then a decision was made that enough is enough. ???Ć?ÄZero tolerance,???Ć?he said. ???Ć?The Foreign Minister reiterated that the decision to cut off diplomatic ties with Qatar was made ???Ć?Äafter taking into account the history of its behavior, including harboring known terrorists and ???Ć?Äfunding extremist groups throughout the region.???Ć????Ć?Ä???Ć?£It???Ć?Ös very simple. We made our point. We took our steps. And it???Ć?Ös up to [Qatar] to amend [its] ???Ć?Äbehavior. And once they do, then things will be worked out. But if they don???Ć?Öt, they will remain ???Ć?Äisolated,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. ???Ć?£If Qatar wants to come back into the GCC pool, they know ???Ć?Äwhat they have to do.???Ć????Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said that Saudi Arabia has expressed its grievances and it is now up to Qatar ???Ć?Äto make amends, and he said Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös demands are non-negotiable. Specifically, Saudi ???Ć?ÄArabia has demanded that Qatar end its practice of harboring known terrorists, prohibit funding ???Ć?Äfrom within its borders to Al-Qaeda and Daesh (ISIS), and shut down its news network, Al-???Ć?ÄJazeera, which has been inciting violence throughout the region. ???Ć????Ć?Ä???Ć?£We hope that reason will prevail and that our brothers in Qatar will do the right thing and ???Ć?Ärespond to the demands of the international community to cease these activities. Because we ???Ć?Äthink we can???Ć?Öt be on both sides of this issue. You cannot fight against ISIS, you cannot commit ???Ć?Äto participate in the global center against extremism, you cannot commit to participate in a ???Ć?Äfinancial center to combat terror financing and at the same time allow these things to go on,???Ć?he ???Ć?Äsaid.???Ć????Ć?Ä???Ć?£This idea that you can fund extremist groups, that you can pay ransom to terrorist groups like ???Ć?ÄAl-Qaeda and ISIS, that you can send $300 million to the Shi???Ć?Öite militias in Iraq with most of it ???Ć?Äending up with the Quds Force in Iran, is not acceptable,???Ć?he said in a press briefing at the Saudi ???Ć?ÄEmbassy in Washington today. ???Ć?£I think most countries in the world would agree with the ???Ć?Ädemand to stop this.???Ć????Ć?(WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?Saudi Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir called on ???Ć?ÄQatar today to end its support for terrorism and extremism in the Middle East. ???Ć?

title: Saudi Arabia Condemns Brussels Train Station Attack
content: The Ministry of Foreign Affairs today strongly condemns the attack which took place in the train station in Brussels, Belgium.

title: Saudi-Iraq Joint Statement
content:
He also expressed his thanks and appreciation for the warm reception and generous hospitality accorded to him and the accompanying delegation. The Prime Minister commended the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques and the Government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia for their care of the Two Holy Mosques and services to pilgrims, Umrah performers and visitors. Prime Minister Haider Al-Abadi expressed the Iraqi Government's appreciation to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia for its support for the efforts being exerted in the reconstruction of Iraq. Emanating from the keenness to strengthen their relations, the two countries agreed to establish a coordination council between them to upgrade relations to the desired strategic level and open new horizons of cooperation in various fields including political, security, economic, developmental, commercial, investment, tourism and cultural domains, activate the partnership between the private sector in the two countries, and follow up the implementation of what has been concluded of agreements and memorandums of understanding to achieve their common objectives. The two countries confirmed their condemnation of all actions affecting the security and stability of the two countries and the region, stressing the need for rejecting the spirit of hatred, violence, sectarian discrimination and fomenting of ideology, affirming their keenness to strengthen their brotherly relations to achieve their common interests and the interests of the two brotherly peoples, emphasizing the importance of enhancing peace and security in the region. The two countries expressed their determination to continue their successful efforts to combat terrorist organizations, especially terrorist Daesh (ISIS) organization whose terrorist actions have reached secure people in the two countries. 
Within the framework of the constructive spirit prevailed in the talks between the two sides, they discussed current situations and political and security challenges facing Arab and Islamic nations, agreed to intensify the joint action to confront these challenges, particularly combating extremism and fighting terrorism in all its forms and manifestations, and emphasized the importance of drying up the sources of terrorism and its financing and abiding by agreements and commitments that bind States in this regard. 
?é?? During the two sessions, the two sides commended what is binding the two countries and their brotherly peoples of the ties of religion, brotherhood, neighborliness, bonds and common destiny, expressing their happiness over what has been recorded by the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the Republic of Iraq of a qualitative leap in relations between them, stressing the importance of the regular exchanges of visits between officials of the two countries as well as businessmen to explore opportunities to support economic and trade relations and develop them to prospects for the interest of the two countries. Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense also held a session of talks with Prime Minister Haider Al-Abadi. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques and the Prime Minister held a session of talks attended by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, and a number of senior officials of the two countries, during which they exchanged views on the prospects and ways of developing bilateral relations and intensifying cooperation in all areas for the service of the two brotherly peoples and the common interests existing between the two countries, especially in political, security, economic, trade, investment and energy fields, and discussed regional and international issues of common concern. At the invitation of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, the Prime Minister of Republic of Iraq Haider Al-Abadi paid an official visit to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia on Monday, 24 Ramadan 1438 AH (19 June 2017). The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the Republic of Iraq issued today the following joint statement:

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Council of Ministers renewed the Kingdom's condemnation of the terrorist acts that took place in Egypt and Somalia, and the terrorist assault on worshippers leaving the Finsbury Park Mosque in London yesterday, as well as the attack on security forces inn Champs-Elys?ā??es in Paris yesterday, expressing the Kingdom's firm rejection of all forms of terrorism and extremism. On the recent Qatif terrorist attack, the cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's keenness on fighting terrorism, protecting its homeland security. It also noted the efforts of the naval forces in confronting three boats that entered the Kingdom's territorial waters. Moreover, it considered the bombing of the three trucks belonging to King Salman Relief and Humanitarian Action Center while preparing for distribution of food aid in the city of Marib as a threat to humanitarian work by the Houthis. King Salman briefed the council on the results of his meeting with Pakistani President Mamnoon Hussain, Yemeni President Abdurbo Mansour Hadi, his talks with the Iraqi Prime Minister Dr. Haider Al-Abbadi and the phone calls received from Russian President Vladimir Putin, British Prime Minister Teresa May and Moroccan Minister of Foreign Affairs and Cooperation Nasser Bourita. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the meeting held by the Council of Ministers, on Monday evening at Al-Safa Palace in Makkah.

title: Kingdom Condemns Terror Attack in London
content: The Foreign Ministry today condemned the terrorist attack that took place on worshipers leaving the Finsbury Mosque in London yesterday.?é?? In a statement, the Foreign Ministry offered its condolences to the families of the victims and UK government and people, and expressed his country's firm rejection of all forms of terrorism and extremism.

title: King Salman Holds Talks With Iraqi Prime Minister
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held talks with the Iraqi Prime Minister Dr. Haider Al-Abbadi at Al-Safa Palace in Makkah today.?é?? During the meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two brotherly countries and discussed regional developments.

title: King Salman Receives Turkish Foreign Minister
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Safa Palace in Makkah this evening Turkish Foreign Minister Mevlut Cavusoglu.?é?? During the meeting, they reviewed the bilateral relations between the kingdom and Turkey and discussed developments in the region.

title: Kingdom Rejects Turkish Offer to Establish Military Bases in Saudi Arabia
content: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia today rejected an offer by Turkey to set up a military base on Saudi territory.?é?? In a statement carried the Saudi Press Agency, the Kingdom said it ???Ć?£cannot allow Turkey to establish military bases on its territories???Ć?and noted that the Kingdom has no need for this since its armed forces and military capabilities are at the best level and it has military presence in a number of countries, including the Incirlik Air Base.

title: Statement Following the Conclusion of Pensacola Naval Air Station Attack Investigation
content: Finally, close to a million Saudis have come to the US for their education, training and other reasons over several decades. The overwhelming majority of them are law-abiding citizens who view themselves as unofficial ambassadors of the kingdom. Many consider the United States as a second home and treat their American colleagues, classmates and neighbors as an extended part of their family. Thousands of them took to social media to condemn the attacker and to extend their condolences to Americans, as did our leadership and our ambassador to the United States. Saudi Arabia has been at the forefront of the international community???Ć?Ös effort to counter terrorist groups for many years.?é?? The kingdom has used every means at its disposal to counter the men, mindset and money that allow Al Qaeda, ISIS and other terrorist groups to recruit followers and to threaten communities and even entire nations.?é?? In addition, the terrorists who have struck the US and many other nations have also targeted Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös people, leadership, military personnel and even our holiest religious institutions in Mecca and Medina on multiple occasions. Violent extremists continue to pose a serious challenge to the security of the United States, Saudi Arabia and most countries around the world. Terror groups have recruited thousands of people from dozens of countries. The kingdom has worked closely with the US to counter this global threat. It is worth noting that the military training that the US provides to Saudi military personnel has enabled Saudi soldiers, pilots and sailors to fight along their American counterparts and against our common foes. The close cooperation between the two nations on intelligence matters and issues related to counter terrorism has saved the lives of many in the US, Saudi Arabia and elsewhere and has made the world a safer place.?é?? Approximately 28,000 Saudis have undergone military training in the US over the course of several decades without incident. Saudi Arabia has fully cooperated with American investigators looking into the motivations of the attacker. Saudi Arabia will continue to cooperate with US authorities should they require additional information. In addition, the Kingdom has determined that 21 cadets have demonstrated conduct unbecoming of an officer in the Saudi Royal Air Force or Royal Navy and therefore have been unenrolled from their training program in the U.S. military. They will be returning to the Kingdom. The disturbed and radicalized individual who carried out this terrible attack acted alone. He does not represent the hundreds of thousands of Saudis who have lived, studied and trained in the United States over the past several decades, nor does his heinous act represent the values of Saudi Arabia. Following the conclusion of the investigation by US law enforcement authorities into the shooting incident at Pensacola Naval Air Station on December 6, 2019 involving a Royal Saudi Air Force cadet, the official spokesperson of the Embassy of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia in Washington would like to stress the following points:

title: King Salman Deputizes Crown Prince to Administer Affair of State in his Absence
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz issued today a issued a royal decree deputizing Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman to administer the state's affairs and take care of the interests of the people during the period of our absence from the Kingdom.

title: Prince Abdulrahman bin Abdulaziz Passed Away, Royal Court announces
content: The Royal Court announced today the death of Prince Abdulrahman bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud.?é?? Funeral prayers for his soul will be performed at the Grand Mosque in Makkah on Friday following Isha (evening) prayer.

title: Prince Mohammed bin Naif Pledges Allegiance to Prince Mohammed bin Salman as Crown Prince
content: On the occasion of his selection as the Crown Prince at Al-Safa Palace in Makkah on Wednesday June 20, 2017; Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz pledged allegiance to Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, wishing him success in his new position.

title: King Salman Names New Ministers, Governors, Ambassador to the US
content: Hail Province Governor Prince Saud bin Abdulmohsin has been replaced by Prince Abdulaziz bin Saad and Prince Faisal bin Khalid bin Sultan replaced Prince Mishal bin Abdullah as governor of the Northern Border Province. Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman was named minister of state for energy affairs, Prince Khalid bin Salman was appointed ambassador to the United States, Abdullah bin Amer Al-Sawaha was appointed Minister of Communications and Information Technology, and Dr. Awwad bin Al-Awwad was appointed Minister of Culture and Information. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today issued a number of royal orders, including the appointments of a new ambassador to the United States and ministers of Culture and Information, and Communications and Information Technology.?é?? King Salman removed Civil Service Minister Khaled Al-Arj and ordered an investigation of possible?é??abuse of?é??office. The royal orders also?é??included the appointment of two new governors and a number of deputy governors. King Salman ordered?é??the restoration of?é??financial allowances for civil servants and military personnel that had been suspended under earlier austerity measures.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet lauded efforts security forces in pursuing terrorists and anyone who tries to undermine the Kingdom's security and threaten its citizens and residents.?é?? The Cabinet commended the security forces for their recent success in raiding a terrorist hideout north of Al-Awamiya in Qatif Governorate and foiling their terror plans. The Council of Ministers renewed the Kingdom's condemnation terrorist attack in Parachinar, Pakistan and expressed its condolences to the families of the victims, Pakistan and its people. The Council of Ministers condemned the declaration by the Israeli occupation authorities to establish a 2,000 new settlement on 241 acres of Palestinian land in the West Bank for the first time in 20 years. The Council reaffirmed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös positions towards the Palestinian cause, including its condemnation of all actions taken by the Israeli occupation authorities and the Kingdom???Ć?Ös repeated calls to the international community to compel Israel to cease all arbitrary and racist measures against the Palestinian people. King Salman also briefed the Cabinet on the results of his meetings with Arab leaders on the sidelines of the summit expressing his pleasure over the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's hosting of the 29th Arab Summit in Riyadh. King Salman began the weekly session by expressing his deep thanks and appreciation to King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan for the warm welcome and generous hospitality accorded to him and the accompanying delegation during his visit to Jordan.?é?? King Salman also expressed his appreciation to the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the King, the Government and people for their great efforts exerted to ensure the success of the 28th Arab Summit Conference. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace this afternoon.

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet reiterated the condemnation of terrorist attacks which took place at the military hospital in Kabul, Afghanistan and a military base in Mali. The Cabinet stressed the decisions of the session, confirming the full commitment and adherence to the Arab Peace Initiative without change, and the need to enable the Palestinian people to exercise their inalienable rights. The Cabinet was then briefed on the latest developments on the Arab and Islamic arenas. The Cabinet lauded the decisions adopted by the 147th session of the Council of Arab Foreign Ministers held in Cairo. In this context, the Cabinet expressed appreciation to the Arab foreign ministers for thanking the Saudi-led Arab Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen in its support of Yemen's legitimacy and people, the resumption of the peace process, and endeavors to stop war and restore the Yemeni state.?é?? The Cabinet also appreciated the humanitarian efforts of the King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSRelief) in providing assistance to the Yemeni people.?é?? The Cabinet also welcomed the Council of Arab Foreign Ministers???Ć?Ös statements unilaterally condemning the coup leaders in Yemen, their violations against the people of Yemen as well as the Iran???Ć?Ös continual interventions that violate the security and stability of Yemen. Crown Prince Mohammed briefed the Cabinet on the contents of the message sent by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah of Kuwait, the telephone call King Salman had with President Joko Widodo of Indonesia, and his meetings in Indonesian.?é?? Crown Prince Mohammad also briefed the cabinet on his meeting with New Zealand???Ć?Ös Foreign Minister Murray MuCully. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif chaired the Cabinet's weekly session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.

title: Crown Prince Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet also approved a scientific cooperation agreement with Belarus; authorized King Saud University to discuss a MoU for academic cooperation with Shanghai University in China.?é?? The Cabinet also authorized the Minister of Culture and Information, or his designate, to discuss with Indonesia and Malaysia a program to exchange news between the Saudi Press Agency and Indonesian and Malaysian news agencies. The Cabinet finally adjourned after discussing a number of topics including cooperation agreements between the Kingdom and each of Kyrgyzstan and Russia in combating crime and protection of classified information; approved the memorandum of understanding with Afghanistan on cooperation in judiciary and legal affairs and the memorandum of understanding with Japan in the field of social development. The Cabinet reviewed the latest developments at the regional and international arenas including the resumption of Syrian negotiations in Geneva under the auspices of the United Nations. The Cabinet expressed hope that the negotiations would bring about a comprehensive ceasefire and a political solution to the Syrian crisis based on Geneva Declaration I and the UN Security Council Resolution 2254. The Cabinet was briefed on the outcomes of the visit of Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir to Iraq, where he held talks with a number of Iraqi officials, reiterating the affirmation of the ties binding the two countries. Crown Prince Mohammad briefed the Cabinet on the outcomes of the talks that the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman held in Riyadh during the week with President Mohammed Abdullah Farmajo of Somalia and President Prokopios Pavlopoulos of Greece. Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammed bin Naif Saud chaired the Cabinet session Monday afternoon at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet authorized the General Commission for Statistics to conduct the population general census for 2020. The Cabinet approved the incentives and privileges offered to donors who purchase antiquities or masterpieces or special collections displayed in national museums; approved the regulatory provisions of the General Authority for Sports; The Cabinet approved the security cooperation agreement with Djibouti signed in Riyadh on 10/07/1437 AH; approved the memorandum of understanding (MoU) between the Ministry of Interior???Ć?Ös Saudi Financial Investigations Administration (SAFA) and the Financial Investigation Unit (FIU) of India as regards cooperation on the exchange of the investigations related to money laundering, terror financing and related crimes, signed in Riyadh on 25/06/1437 AH; approved MoU for cooperation in the field of renewable energy between King Abdullah City for Atomic and Renewable Energy and China???Ć?Ös National Power Management, signed in Riyadh on 09/04/1437H. The Cabinet finally adjourned after discussing a number of domestic issues. The Council strong condemnation of the murder of Russia's Ambassador to Turkey, Andrey Karlov and the terror attack in Berlin, Germany, that resulted in the death of 12 people.?é?? The Cabinet also the double terrorist bombing of a market in Baghdad and the attack in Istanbul.?é?? The Cabinet offered its condolences to the families of the victims. The Cabinet also reiterated the Kingdom's appreciation to U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry???Ć?Ös statement on the final solution of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, as well as the adoption by Security Council Resolution 2334, which condemns Israeli settlement and calls for stopping it in the occupied Palestinian territories. The Cabinet also discussed the outcome of the 97th OPEC meeting in Cairo and commitment by OPEC members to honor the November 2016 agreement on reducing oil production. The Cabinet welcomed Security Council Resolution 2328 on the deployment of international observers in Aleppo to supervise the evacuation of civilians. The Cabinet was briefed on a number of reports including the emergency meeting of the Executive Committee of Foreign Ministers of Organization of Islamic Cooperation that the massacres being committed in Aleppo, Syrian, can be classified as a war crime and crimes against humanity. King Salman briefed the Cabinet on the results of his two phone calls with King Abdullah II bin Al-Hussein of Jordan and President Abed Rabbo Hadi of Yemen, as well as his two meetings with Palestinian President Mahmud Abbas and Chairman of the Board of Directors of Al-Quds Fund Muneeb Al-Masri, in addition to the messages received from King of Jordan; Emir of Kuwait and President of Comoros. The Cabinet appreciated King Salman???Ć?Ös order to organize a popular campaign for the Syrian people relief of the Syrian people.?é?? King Salman???Ć?Ös directive to allocate SR 100 million ($26.67 million) for the campaign and his personal SR 20 million ($5.33 million) donation.?é?? The Cabinet also express its appreciation to the SR 10 million ($2.67 million) donation by Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif and the SR 10 million ($2.67 million) Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman???Ć?Ös SR 8 million ($2.13 million) to alleviate the sufferings of Syrian people, especially the displaced from Aleppo. The Cabinet began by congratulating King Salman on the second anniversary of his ascension to the throne.?é?? The Cabinet then commended the State???Ć?Ös general budget for the fiscal year 2017; for its sufficient strength to cope with economic and financial challenges. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh on Monday afternoon.

title: Kingdom Issues 2017 Budget, Projects Major Deficit Reduction
content: Saudi Arabia is a member of the G20 and ranks as the 29th most competitive economy in the world, according to World Economic Forum???Ć?Ös 2016-2017 Global Competitiveness Report. Revenue is projected to reach SAR 692 billion ($184.5 billion) in 2017, a 31 percent increase from initial projections. Oil revenues are expected to increase by 46 percent, and non-oil revenues are estimated to grow by 6.5 percent. Moreover, the budget deficit is expected to reach SAR 198 billion in 2017, reflecting 7.7 percent of the GDP. Combined, these efforts will move the Kingdom closer to its Vision 2030 goal of balancing the budget by 2020. In reaching these goals, the 2017 budget expenditures will focus primarily on the following key sectors: ???Ć?£Our economy is firm and it has sufficient strength to cope with the current economic and financial challenges,???Ć?said King Salman. ???Ć?£We have sought through this budget and its programs to improve the efficiency of capital and operational expenditures in the state, strengthen the situation of public finances, enhance their sustainability, give priority to developmental and service projects and programs that serve citizens directly, contribute to activating the role of the private sector and increase its contribution to the Gross Domestic Product.???Ć? is estimated to reach SAR 890 billion ($237.3), an 8 percent increase from 2016, and is projected to reflect a record 33 percent decrease in the Kingdom???Ć?Ös national deficit. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz issued today a royal decree outlining Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös 2017 budget. The

title: Saudi King Says Nation Is Committed to Regional Security and Economic Development
content: ???Ć?£I am optimistic about a better tomorrow,???Ć?King Salman said. ???Ć?£These conditions that we are now experiencing are not more difficult than their previous ones. We will overpass them to a better and brilliant future.???Ć?King Salman noted that economic reforms implemented to date have focused on diversifying the nation???Ć?Ös economy and re-distributing resources to expand job opportunities. Saudi Arabia has ???Ć?£come out of [the past three years] with a strong economy and continuous growth; we have set out our reforms today to look forward to the future,???Ć?he noted. The strength of these structural reforms fall under Vision 2030, Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös economic transformation plan. The region???Ć?Ös stability directly impacts the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic development, and ensuring both remains fundamental, King Salman stressed. ???Ć?£Our domestic policy is based on pillars of maintaining security, stability and prosperity to achieve the development that meets the needs of the present and preserves the right of future generations.???Ć????Ć?£We in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia believe that the security of our dear neighbor Yemen is the security of the Kingdom, and will not accept any interference in its internal affairs,???Ć?he stated. King Salman pledged to uphold Yemen???Ć?Ös right to security. Yemen, he said, cannot be the ???Ć?£corridor???Ć?for countries trying to undermine the security of the Kingdom, nor can Saudi Arabia allow other nations to undermine its sovereignty and security. Among the core foreign policy priorities, King Salman vowed to continue cooperating with the international community in combatting the extremism that threatens its borders and the region at large. Political solutions to international crises can safeguard values of tolerance and coexistence, paving the way for nations to ensure peace and achieve development, King Salman said. In his annual address to the Consultative Council, King Salman presented an optimistic outlook for the nation???Ć?Ös foreign and domestic policies. He said that Saudi Arabia has played an active and constructive role in the region and in important global undertakings with the international community is proceeding with ???Ć?£firm steps???Ć?to maintain its active role in the region and around the world. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz reinforced today that Saudi Arabia remains fully committed to acting vigorously to ensure its security and the security of the region while moving forward on the Kingdom???Ć?Ös economic development and reforms.

title: Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet has also reviewed a number of issues on its agenda, including the annual report of the general organization for social insurance on a previous fiscal year. The Cabinet issued relevant instructions towards them. The cabinet also approved the appointment of Jamal bin Ali Al-Kishi and Eng. Omar bin Nabil Al-Khudairi as two members of the private sector in the Board of Directors of the Saline Water Conversion Corporation for a period of three years. The Cabinet approved an amendment to Table (2) regarding the joint committees list attached to the Cabinet's decision No. 2 dated 03/01/1428H, so that the Saudi side at the Saudi-Indonesian Joint Committee is chaired by the Minister of Foreign Affairs, and that the Committee shall meet every six months. The Cabinet concluded by approving a memorandum of understanding the Peoples Republic of China on enhancing joint cooperation on the silk road economic belt and the initiative of naval silk road for the 21st century and cooperation on productive energy which was signed in Riyadh on 9/4/1437 H;?é?? authorized the Minister of Islamic Affairs, Call and Guidance, or his deputy, to negotiate a memorandum of understanding in the field of Islamic Affairs with Mauritania, sign it, and forward the final version for taking further procedures; approved a general cooperation agreement Mauritius which was signed in New York on 12/12/1436H; authorized the Minister of Foreign Affairs, or his deputy, to negotiate with Norway and New Zealand draft memoranda of understanding on political consultations, sign them, and submit the final versions for taking further authentication;?é?? authorized the Acting Secretary General of King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives, or his deputy, to discuss with the United Arab Emirate a draft memorandum of cooperation between King Abdulaziz Foundation for Research and Archives Saudi Arabia and the National Archive of the Ministry of Presidential Affairs of the United Arab Emirates, sign it, and submit the signed final version. Also praised was the Kingdom's participation in the international conference on the preservation of endangered cultural heritage, held in Abu-Dhabi in the attendance of Prince Khalid Al-Faisal, Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque and Governor of Makkah Region, who led the Kingdom's delegation on behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosque. The Cabinet lauded the holding of a number of cultural activities, citing the opening by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques of Trade Roads in the Arab peninsula exhibition, the masterpieces and antiquities of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia across history, held at King Abdulaziz international cultural center in Dhahran, and the king's okay of the exhibition's tour of east Asia following its successful nine tours of Europe and America. To mark the occasion of the International Day for Solidarity with the Palestinian people, the Cabinet reiterated the Kingdom's call for the world community to remain committed towards the rights of Palestinians and retard Israel from its procrastinating policies against them. The Cabinet praised the 7th Bosphorus Summit.?é?? The Cabinet hailed the cooperation agreements in the fields of investment signed by Saudi Arabia and Turkey during the Summit. The Cabinet also praised the cooperation and contribution of the main oil producing countries outside OPEC to the restoration of stability and balance of international petroleum market. The Cabinet was briefed on the outcome of OPEC ministerial meeting which was held last week in Vienna. The Cabinet praised the outcome of the meeting, the decisions that were taken as well as the clear cooperation among all OPEC countries to restore stability in the international oil market by reducing excess supply. The Cabinet commended the outcome of the 35th Meeting of GCC Interior Ministers, expressing hope that the outcome of the meeting will contribute to enhancing the efforts of GCC countries in the field of security. The Cabinet reviewed a number of reports on regional, Arab and international developments, and looked forward to the outcome of meetings the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques will hold with leaders of the United Arab Emirates, the State of Qatar, the Kingdom of Bahrain and the State of Kuwait during his tour that emanates from his keenness to communicate with Gulf Cooperation Council leaders to serve the interests of their peoples and to enhance ties of brotherhood among GCC countries. Crown Prince Mohammad welcomed Minister of Labour and Social Development Dr. Ali bin Nasser Al-Ghafis in his new portfolio, wishing him success. He also thanked the outgoing officials and members for their efforts. The Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques congratulated the Kingdom's Grand Mufti; members of the Senior Scholars Commission; full-time sheikhs, members of the Standing Committee for Fatwa (affiliated to the Senior Scholars Commission); and Speaker and members of Majlis Al-Shura on the royal trust bestowed on them through renewing their memberships to their agencies, wishing them all success. Deputy Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz Al Saud chaired the Cabinet's session which was held at Al-Yamamah Palace today.

title: Royal Order Appoints Al-Sadhan as Royal Court Advisor
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz issued a royal order appointing Abdulrahman bin Mohammed Al-Sadhan as an advisor at the Royal Court, at ministerial rank, with immediate effect.

title: King Salman Recomposes Majlis Al-Shura (Consultative Council)
content: The Speaker of Majlis Al-Shura is instructed to carry out this royal order issued by King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud. The newly reformed Majlis Al-Shura is to consist of these following members: King Salman appointed Sheikh Dr. Abdullah bin Mohammed bin Ibrahim Al-AsSheikh as Speaker of the Majlis Al-Shura, with Dr. Mohammed bin Amin bin Ahmed Al-Jiffri asDeputy Speaker, Dr. Yahya bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz Al-Samaan as the Assistant to the Speaker of Majlis Al-Shura. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz issued a royal order to recompose the formation of Majlis Al-Shura for four years beginning of 3/3/1438 (Dec. 2, 2016).
