title: 34 Positive Confirmed Reported Cases of Corona
content: In its weekly statement, the Ministry of Health said that it recorded 34 positive confirmed cases of respiratory Middle East syndrome virus (Corona) during the past week, including 33 cases in Riyadh and one case in Najran.

title: Beneficiaries of Mohammad bin Naif Center to Spend Eid Holiday with their Families
content: A spokesman of the Ministry of Interior announced today that in implementation of the directives of Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, the beneficiaries of the branches of the Mohammad bin Naif Center for Counseling and Care in Riyadh and Jeddah will be allowed to spend the blessed Eid Al-Fitr with their families. The spokesman said the directive will be implemented effective from Thursday, 22 Ramadan until 5 Shawwal 1436 AH. (July 9-21, 2015).

title: Saudi Health Centers Provide Medical Assistance to some 1,800 Patients a Week in Zaatari Camp
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support Syrian Brothers funded medical centers in Zaatari refugee camp in Jordan treats 1,800 Syrian patients per week during Ramadan, Medical Director of the Saudi Specialized Clinics Dr. Muhammad Ismail Zoubi said today. The campaign provides health services, including psychological support, to Syrian refuges inside Syria and in neighboring countries.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: Minister of Culture and Information Al Toraifi said that the Cabinet was briefed on the topics on its agenda, including topics in which the Shura Council participated in their study, and on the reports of both its General Committee and Experts' Bureau regarding them, and decided the following: approved an amendment the Civil Defense Regulation; amendment the name of the Saudi Commission for Tourism and Antiquities (SCTA) to the Saudi Commission for Tourism and National Heritage; approved the two mutual memorandums between the United Nations Secretary-General and the Foreign Minister regarding the actions that can be taken by the Kingdom with respect to diplomatic bags of the United Nations. The Cabinet praised the efforts of Prince Sultan bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, chairman of the board of directors of the Society for Children with Disability and all those interested in charitable and volunteering works as well as all contributors and supporters of such charitable deeds for what has been achieved by the Society. Turning to domestic issues, the Cabinet was briefed on the results of the 22nd Annual Meeting of Governors of Regions. The meeting, under the chairmanship of Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of the Interior, discussed plans to boost development and enhance services for citizens. This year???Ć?Ös agenda focused on modernization and development of Provincial System, ways to measure the performance of governors and chiefs of administrative centers, and the state of unplanned communities. The Cabinet extended its thanks and appreciation to King Salman for patronizing laying of the foundation stone ceremony of Khair Makkah charitable investment project allocated for Society for Children with Disability, and contributing SR 50 million ($13.33 million) for the project. The Cabinet was briefed on the results of the official visit by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, to France and his meeting with President Francois Hollande and senior French officials.?é?? The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös condemnation of the continuing acts of violence, discourse of hatred, incitement and racial discrimination against the Muslim community in Myanmar. The Cabinet stressed that the humanitarian tragedy being experienced by Rohingya Muslims and their exposure to an ethnic cleansing campaigns require urgent action from the international community to call on the Government of Myanmar to adopt a transparent and comprehensive policy to stop all practices of persecution and deportation, and to speed the recognition of Rohingya Muslims minority without any discrimination or ethnic classification. The Cabinet re-emphasized in this regard, the Kingdom's rejection, condemnation and denunciation of all forms of violence and terrorism, and its solidarity and stand with sisterly and friendly countries in confronting terrorist acts to maintain their security and stability. The Cabinet also condemned today's terrorist bombing in Cairo which killed Egypt???Ć?Ös Prosecutor General Hisham Barakat. The Cabinet expressed its deepest condolences and sympathy to the government of Egypt and people, and to the family of the deceased, praying to Allah to have mercy on him and to inspire his family patience and solace. Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel Al Toraifi said following the session that the Cabinet reviewed the latest regional and international developments. The Kingdom re-affirming it condemnation of the terrorist bombings at the Imam Al-Sadeq Mosque in Kuwait, the attack at the Tunisian seaside resort of Sousse, and the terrorist attack at a chemical plant near Lyon, France. The Cabinet stressed that such terrorist acts are contrary to all Islamic values and principles and expressed its condolences to the governments and people of Kuwait, Tunisia, and France, and the families of the victims and wished a speedy recovery for the injured. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah on this evening. At the outset of the session, King Salman briefed the Cabinet on the telephone conversation he had with President Nursultan Nazarbayev of Kazakhstan, and his meeting with the Secretary General of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Dr. Abdul Latif bin Rashid Al-Zayani.

title: Separation Surgery on Yemeni Conjoined Twins to Start Friday
content: As an extension of the humanitarian initiatives adopted by the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia towards the people of Yemen, and on the directives of Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, separation surgery on Yemeni conjoined twins will be performed Friday. The surgery is expected to last up to 12 hours with the participation of nearly 30 specialists from the medical departments of anesthesia, surgery of children, urology, orthopedic, plastic, nursing, technicians and paramedics, adviser at the Royal Court, General Supervisor of King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid and head of the medical and surgical team, Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabiah said.

title: Saudi Clinics Treat some 350 Patients a Day at Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan
content: Saudi Specialist Clinics provided medical services to some two thousand displaced Syrians at Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan last week. The clinics treats an average of 300-350 patients a day. The clinics???Ć?pharmacies dispense on average over 1,300 prescriptions a day.

title: Foreign Ministry Issues Travel Warning to Iran
content: The Ministry also urges the Iranian government to shoulder its responsibilities towards accelerating the results of the investigation with full transparency, along with the provision of the medical care for the injured and the provision of the necessary protection for Saudi nationals in Iran. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs today warned Saudi citizens of the risks of traveling to Iran following the death of four and the injury to 29 Saudi citizens from poisoning in the Iranian city of Mashhad. Due to the mysterious circumstances surrounding the poisoning and lack of transparency in the investigation, the Foreign Ministry calls upon Saudi citizens traveling to Iran to take the utmost caution to preserve their safety and security as well as their families from any harm.

title: Foreign Ministry Summons Iranian Ambassador After Poisoning of Saudis
content: Ambassador Nugali added that Kingdom???Ć?Ös charge d???Ć?Öaffaires in Tehran visited the victims and to offer support. The Saudi Foreign Ministry summoned Iran's ambassador today after four Saudis died from poisoning in the northeast Iranian city of Mashhad. Ministry spokesman Ambassador Osama Nugali said in a statement that the Kingdom expressed deep concern over the death of the four children and the injury of 28 other Saudis, and urged the Iranian authorities to conduct a thorough investigation and reveal all circumstances surrounding the poisoning. Ambassador Nugali also said the Kingdom demanded that Iran cooperate with the Saudi embassy in Tehran and the Consulate General in Mashhad in order to allow Saudi embassy staff to follow up the medical condition of the patients and to provide all the necessary facilities and care needed by the Saudi citizens in Iran as well as to provide the necessary protection for them.

title: Interior Ministry Bans Citizens from Traveling to Ebola-Affected Countries
content: The Ministry of Interior has announced a travel ban against citizens to countries affected by Ebola. General Director of Public Relations and Media at the Ministry of Interior Maj. Gen. Dr. Mohammed bin Abdullah Al-Marul said that a High Directive has been issued banning Saudi citizens from traveling to Guinea, Sierra Leone and Liberia. Emergency travel to these countries will be considered on a case-by-case basis by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs in coordination with the Ministry of Health. Those permitted to travel, will be subject to preventive measures upon their arrival from the affected countries.

title: Royal Court: King's Efforts on Al-Aqsa are Culminated With Success
content: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia affirms the right of Muslims in Al-Aqsa Holy Mosque and the performance of their worship in it with ease and tranquility. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman stressed the need for the return of calm at Al-Aqsa Mosque and its surroundings, the respect for the sanctity of the place, and that Muslims must return to enter the mosque and perform worship in it with all security, tranquility and peace effective from today. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia emphasizes the importance of achieving a just and comprehensive peace for the Palestinian cause in accordance with the Arab peace initiative, the vision of a two-state solution and the relevant resolutions of the international legitimacy. In reference to the events that took place at Al-Aqsa Holy Mosque during the last period, the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has held necessary contacts with a number of leaders of the world over the past few days.?é?? The Government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia has also contacted the Administration of the United States of America to exert its efforts for not closing Al-Aqsa Mosque in front of Muslims, not preventing them from performing their duties and prayers in it, and canceling restrictions being imposed on the entry to the Mosque. These efforts have been culminated with success today and in a way that contributes to restore stability and tranquility to worshipers and preserve their dignity and security. The Royal Court issued today the following statement:

title: Embassy Hosts Annual Iftar
content: Saudi Deputy Chief of Mission to the U.S. Sami Al-Sadhan this evening hosted the traditional Iftar for Arab and Muslim ambassador at the Saudi Embassy in Washington. The event was also attended by Muslim leaders and members of the Saudi community in Washington.

title: Crown Prince Orders Distribute of Ramadan Breakfast Baskets to the Needy in Somalia
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior launched Ramadan 1438 Project for Somalia to feed the needy.?é???é??Regional Director of the Saudi National Campaign for Relief of Somali People Sa'ad Al-Swayed said that group has allocated over 40,000 Ramadan baskets worth about $3.5 million for distributed in Somalia.

title: OIC, WML  Condemn Terrorist Attacks on Egypt's Coptic Church
content: Both Islamic organizations reaffirmed their resolute stand against terrorism in all its forms and manifestations. In a statement, the Secretary General of MWL Sheikh Dr. Mohammed Al-Isa said attacks against places of worship go against the authentic Islamic religion and its teachings of tolerance, but fail to undermine the unity of the Egyptian people. The Jeddah-based Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) and Makkah-based Muslim World League condemned?é?? the terrorist attacks on Mari Girgis (St. George's) Church in Tanta, Egypt, as a heinous crime against all Egyptians.

title: King Salman Leaves Indonesia on His Way to Japan
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today left Indonesia on his way to Japan on an official visit.

title: OIC Strongly Condemns Terrorist Attacks in Burkina Faso
content: Dr. Othaimeen has expressed his concern vis-?ā??-vis the persistence of such acts at the common borders of Burkina Faso and Mali and has called for better counter-terrorism coordination in the sub-region, encouraging the implementation of the joint force between Burkina Faso, Mali and Niger in order to fight insecurity in the area of Liptako-Gourma. In a statement, OIC Secretary General, Dr. Yousef A. Othaimeen, indicated that those people who use Islam in a fraudulent way to justify their terrorist acts are criminals who must be pursued and brought to justice for punishment. He pointed out that the principles of Islam are completely opposed to hatred and violence. The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has strongly condemned the Tuesday's terrorist attacks by Ansarul Islam of Ibrahim Malam Dicko against two police stations in Soum Province in northern Burkina Faso, where a civilian was wounded and some buildings and vehicles were burned.

title: Saudi Minister of Hajj and Umrah Meets Iranian Hajj Officials
content: Minister of Hajj and Umrah Dr. Mohammad Saleh Bin Taher Benten met in Riyadh today Chairman of Iranian Hajj and Visit Organization Dr. Hameid Mohammadi to discuss Iranian pilgrims affairs arrangements for the this year???Ć?Ös Hajj season.

title: Deputy Islamic Affairs Minister Receives Visiting USCIRF Delegation
content: At the end of the meeting, the members of USCIRF delegation praised the efforts being exerted by the Ministry of Islamic Affairs in the fight against extremism and terrorism, wishing to strengthen bilateral relations between the Commission and the Ministry to serve the principle of freedom. During the meeting, Dr. Al-Sudairy explained the overall rights and freedoms being guaranteed by Islam, the tolerance of Islam and its great values in all aspects of life, including the guarantee of freedom and peaceful coexistence among the followers of other religions. Deputy Minister of Islamic Affairs, Call and Guidance Dr. Tawfiq Abdulaziz Al-Sudairy received in Riyadh today a delegation from United States Commission on International Religious Freedom (USCIRF).

title: King Salman to Cover Cost of Jordanian With Special Needs Who Wish to Perform Umrah
content: ?é?? The Saudi Embassy in Amman today launched the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz?é??Umrah program for people with special needs.?é?? The program is in collaboration with the Handicapped Child Care Charity Association in Irbid, Jordan and the Medical Educational Center.?é??

title: Turkish Prime Minister Arrives in Medinah
content: Turkish Prime Minister Ahmet Davutoglu arrived today in Madinah to visit the Prophet's Mosque. During his arrival at Prince Mohammed bin Abdulaziz International Airport, he was received by Undersecretary of Al-Madinah Province Abdul Mohsen Al-Muneef.

title: King Salman Calls Indonesian President
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques made today a telephone call to Indonesian President Joko Widodo. The King expressed sincere condolences on the victims of the terrorist attacks witnessed by Jakarta city yesterday and left several people dead and scores injured.

title: Islamic Relief Organization Conducts More than 500 Eye Surgeries in Morocco
content: The Jeddah-based the International Islamic Relief Organization (IIRO) of Muslim World League (MWL) has conducted 529 cataract surgery, including replacing lens with artificial lens. The medical team also performed eye examinations and distribution of glasses to a large numbers of patients from various towns and villages of Morocco. The medical team perform on average about 100 operations a day.

title: Supreme Magistracy Council Calls Islamic Military Alliance a Blessing
content: ???Ć?£The Islamic Alliance announces to the whole world that terrorism has no religion, and all have suffered from it, which requires decision-making process on the ground aiming at combating terrorism and responding militarily in accordance with the programs and mechanisms to support the joint efforts of countries allied to stand united against this serious challenge that surrounds the nations, undermines the security and threatens their stability. The Secretary-General and spokesman of the Council, Sheikh Salman bin Mohammed Al-Nashwan said the development of terrorism and its mechanisms, forms, manifestations, the multiplicity of its sources, and different sources make it imperative to take such a blessed step to fight it, drain its resources and eliminate sources of funding. General Secretariat of Supreme Magistracy Council has called the formation of the Islamic Military Alliance to fight terrorism a blessed step towards the fight against terrorism and its extremist thought.

title: Kingdom Forms 34 Nation Islamic Military Alliance Against Terrorism
content: He also asserted that the Islamic military alliance will coordinate with globally important countries and international organizations in this action, pointing out that the alliance will fight terrorism at the military, intellectual and media levels, in addition to the remarkable security effort currently existing. ???Ć?£We will confine terrorist organizations whatever might be their classification. Of course, in connection with operations in Syria and Iraq, we cannot carry out these operations but only through coordination with the legitimacy in both of them and the international community,???Ć?the deputy crown prince said. Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed said that alliance will not only target Daesh, but all terrorist organizations. The Deputy Crown Prince added: ???Ć?£We have a number of countries suffering from terrorism, including Syria, Iraq, Sinai, Yemen, Libya, Mali, Nigeria, Pakistan, and Afghanistan, and this requires very strong efforts to fight it. Undoubtedly through this alliance, there will be a coordination to fight it through these efforts.???Ć?The Deputy Crown Prince pointed out that an operations room of the alliance will be established in Riyadh to coordinate efforts to fight terrorism. He added, ???Ć?£today, every Islamic country is fighting terrorism individually. The coordination of efforts is very important; and through this room, means and efforts will be developed for fighting terrorism all over the Islamic world.???Ć?Speaking at a press conference in Riyadh, Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman said, ???Ć?£the alliance includes a group of Islamic states that make up the majority of the Islamic world, and this emanates from the keenness of the Muslim world to fight this disease which affected the Islamic world first, before the international community as a whole. ???Ć?Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense today announced the formation of a 34-nation Islamic military alliance to fight terrorism and be a partner in the global war on terrorism.

title: Saudi Arabia Dispels Rumors of Plans to Build Mosques in Germany
content: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia today denied rumors ???Ć?Äcirculating in social media in Germany and other news outlets that Saudi Arabia is planning to ???Ć?Äbuild 200 mosques in Germany. Ambassador Osama Nugali, manager of the Media Department ???Ć?Äat the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, said in a statement that the ???Ć?£the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia as ???Ć?Äthe Custodian of Two Holy Mosques and the birthplace of ???Ć?ÄIslam is committed to supporting ???Ć?ÄMuslim communities around the world. Building mosques in ???Ć?Äother countries is arranged in close ???Ć?Äcoordination with the governments of those countries in order ???Ć?Äto facilitate the ability of Muslim ???Ć?Äcitizens and residents to practice their faith.???Ć?However, with regard to reports that Saudi Arabia ???Ć?Äplans to build 200 mosques in Germany, Ambassador ???Ć?ÄNugali said, ???Ć?£This issue is not under ???Ć?Äconsideration and no discussions have taken place with the ???Ć?ÄGerman government about it. ???Ć?ÄTherefore, the reports are without basis or truth.???Ć?

title: Kingdom Call for Emergency OIC Meeting on Al-Quds
content: The Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has welcomed the request for an emergency meeting to discuss the Israeli violations of Al-Quds and the occupation forces???Ć?brutal aggression on Al-Aqsa Holy Mosque.

title: King Salman Holds Meeting with Indonesian President
content: The talks addressed bilateral relations between the two countries and means of enhancing them in various fields. The two leaders also discussed the latest developments on Islamic, regional and international arenas. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz held official talks with Indonesia's President Joko Widodo at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah.

title: King Salman Receives Arab Ambassadors to the United States and American Muslim Leaders
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received today at his residence the Arab ambassadors to the United States and leaders of American Muslim community. The audiences was attended by Minister of State Dr. Musaed Al-Aiban; Minister of Finance Dr. Ibrahim Al-Assaf; Minister of Commerce and Industry Dr. Tawfiq Al-Rabia; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi; Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir; Minister of Health Khalid bin Abdulaziz Al-Falih; and the Chief of General Intelligence Khalid n Ali Al-Homaidan, Chief of General Intelligence.

title: UN Unveiled Re-Installation of Kiswa
content: Ambassador Abdallah Yahya A. Al-Mouallimi, Permanent Representative of Saudi Arabia to the United Nations and UN Secretary-General Ban Ki-moon yesterday unveiled the reinstallation of the Kiswa of Holy Kaaba at UN Headquarters in New York. The Kiswa was a gift to the UN by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques in 1983. The Kiswa was re-installed following the renovation of the Headquarters complex.

title: SCTH Renovates Historic Abu Gabal Mosque in Amlaj
content: The Saudi Commission for Tourism and National Heritage (SCTH) began renovating Abu Gabal archaeological mosque located in the historical area in Amlaj Province. The renovation of the mosque is being implemented according to its old style of building with mud and stones extracted from the sea and some of the mountains surrounding the province.

title: $8.5 Million to Improve Makkah's Entrance
content: The Mayor of Makkah Dr. Osama bin Fadhl Al-Bar said that a project has been concluded to improve and beautify the entrance of the holy city of Makkah. The project will be finalized within two and a half years at a total cost of $8.5 million.

title: Kingdom Announces Friday as Eid Al-Fitr
content: The Saudi Supreme Court announced this evening that tomorrow, 1 Shawwal 1436 (July 17, 2015), Eid Al-Fitr.

title: OIC Condemns Terrorist Attacks in Sinai
content: Madani reiterated the OIC's consistent position to fight terrorism in all its forms and manifestations, regardless of its source. He stressed the OIC's full commitment to continue to work through constant consultation with Member States and the OIC organs and institutions to confront the scourge of terrorism effectively within the framework of the OIC adopted resolutions. OIC Secretary General Iyad bin Ameen Madani expressed the OIC's full solidarity with the Egyptian government and people in facing up to terrorism. Madani added, that he is confident that such terrorist acts would only consolidate the determination of the Egyptian government and people to stand together and unite in the fight against terrorism and for peace and stability. The Jeddah-based Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) condemned the terrorist attacks on military checkpoints in Egypt's North Sinai today in which dozens of people were killed, including civilians.

title: Council of Senior Scholars Condemns Terrorist Bombing in Kuwait
content: General Secretariat of the Council of Senior Scholars strongly condemned the heinous terrorist bombing at Al-Imam Al-Sadiq Mosque in Kuwait. In a statement, the council stressed that this terrorist attack violated all sanctities of religion and that the perpetrators of this crime and their supporters have committed great sins according to the consensus of Muslim Scholars.

title: Kingdom Calls Upon Myanmar to Stop Rohingya Muslims Persecution
content: Address the U.N. Human Rights Council on the circumstances of Rohingya Muslims, Saudi delegate Khalid Manzalawi stressed the Kingdom's condemnation of the continuing acts of violence against the minority Rohingya Muslims in Myanmar and Nepranytau???Ć?Ös refusal recognized them as citizens under the pretext that they are illegal immigrants from Bangladesh. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia expressed deep concern over the humanitarian experienced by Rohingya Muslims in Myanmar and their recent plight of what is called the crisis of refugee boats in the South East Asia in this regard. The Kingdom also expressed its appreciation to all states that have contributed to the rescue of Rohingya refugees, particularly Malaysia and Indonesia.

title: Four Royal Orders Issued
content: Four royal orders were issued today.:

title: IPU: Saudi Women Participate in Politics at a High Rate
content: Commenting on the news, Faisal bin Hasan Trad, Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös Permanent Representative to the UN in Geneva, attributed the achievement to the decision made earlier by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz allotting 30 seats for women in the membership of the Shura Council. Saudi Arabia occupies an advanced world position in terms of female political participation, it was announced in Geneva today. A statement issued by the International Parliamentary Union (IPU) noted that Saudi women participated in government at a rate of 19.9 percent, one of the higher positions internationally. The IPU cited a newly released world map illustrating the level of female participation in politics.

title: 24th Arab Summit Issues Doha Declaration
content: Finally, the declaration touched on several additional topics. It expressed support for the national dialogue initiated by Bahraini King Hamad bin Eissa Al-Khalifa; stressed the importance of cooperation in countering terrorism and encouraging dialogue between civilizations; praised the signing of agreements between Djibouti and Eritrea in 2010 and between Sudan and South Sudan in 2012; called for greater Arab assistance for Darfur; condemned the treatment of the Muslim Rohingyas in Burma; and welcomed the ongoing political progress in Somalia. The declaration urged member countries to work towards economic integration by establishing a free-trade zone across the Arab world. It stressed the importance of carrying out political, economic, social, financial and trade reforms in order to create the conditions for the establishment of a joint Arab market. In particular, it called for the reform of the Arab League system and the passage of legislation guaranteeing the rights of Arab women in society. Addressing other Arab countries, the statement emphasized the importance of maintaining the sovereignty, independence and territorial integrity of Libya, Yemen and Lebanon and rejected any form of foreign interference into the internal affairs of those countries. Furthermore, it condemned Iran for its continued occupation of three islands belonging to the UAE. On the crisis in Syria, the statement condemned in the strongest terms the dangerous military escalation being pursued by the Assad regime, characterized by the use of heavy weapons, warplanes, and scud missiles to commit mass murder against the civilian population. It welcomed the assumption by the Syrian National Coalition of Syria???Ć?Ös seat in the Arab League and its various bodies. While emphasizing the importance of reaching a political solution, the statement maintained the right of every member state to provide aid ???Ć?including military support ???Ć?to the Syrian people to assist in their self-defense against the Assad regime. It urged all countries and international organizations to provide aid to help ease the ongoing humanitarian crisis. Regarding the Palestinian cause, the statement welcomed the initiative proposed by Qatari Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani to establish a $1 billion fund (with $250 million to be provided by Qatar) to finance projects that conserve the Arab and Islamic identity of Al-Quds [Jerusalem] and aid the Palestinian people suffering under the Israeli occupation. It called upon the international community to work towards the ultimate goal of establishing an independent Palestinian state based on the pre-June 1967 borders with East Al-Quds as its capital. Furthermore, it demanded that Israel withdraw from all occupied Arab land, including Syria???Ć?Ös Golan Heights, condemned illegal Israeli settlement activity, and rejected Israel???Ć?Ös characterization of itself as a Jewish state. The statement expressed the Arab League???Ć?Ös commitment to reconstructing the Gaza Strip and reiterated its call to end the blockade of the territory. Finally, it urged all Palestinian factions to work towards national unity and commended Egypt and Qatar for their tireless efforts to help foster reconciliation. The 24th Arab League (AL) Summit concluded its deliberation in Doha today with the issuance of a final statement addressing a wide array of regional issues. The meeting included the participation of Crown Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz and other leaders from across the Arab world. AL Deputy Secretary-General Ahmed bin Helli read the Doha Declaration to the gathering.

title: Minister of Culture and Information Receives U.S. official
content: Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Abdulaziz bin Mohieddin Khoja received Undersecretary of the U.S. Department of State for Political Affairs Wendy Sherman in Riyadh today. During the meeting, the two sides held cordial talks and reviewed bilateral relations.

title: Foreign Minister Proposes Arab League Reform
content: Such a team, he said, will discuss the circumstances that have led to Arab inaction and come up with plans to promote broad-based cooperation. Prince Saud noted that any reforms will be based on the Arab League covenant and charter, which have already been approved by the member states. Furthermore, he said, the restructuring process should be transparent and aimed at achieving the aspirations of the Arab people. Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal headed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös delegation to the 139th ordinary session of the Foreign Ministers of the Arab League Council in Cairo today. During his remarks, Prince Saud presented a proposal to form a neutral, independent working group made up of representatives from various Arab states to develop ideas for increasing the effectiveness of the Arab League.

title: Royal Orders Amend Shura Council System and Form New Chamber
content: According to the royal order, the new Shura Council will be formed with Sheikh Abdullah bin Mohammed bin Ibrahim Al-Asheikh as speaker. The other will members include: The royal order mandates that no less than twenty percent of the Council???Ć?Ös members will be women. It states, ???Ć?£Women, selected as members of the Shura Council, will enjoy full rights of membership, be committed to their duties, responsibilities and assume their jobs.???Ć?The directive adds that separate entrances, seating areas, and facilities will be allocated for women. Royal orders were issued today amending the Shura Council system and forming a new chamber for four years, beginning at the expiration of the term of the current Shura Council. According to the royal order, the Shura Council will consist of a Speaker and 150 members, chosen by the King according to their knowledge, experience and profession. Another royal order ???Ć?£will designate the members???Ć?rights, duties and other things.???Ć?The rights of the Shura Council to form subcommittees and ad hoc committees to study specific issues are also detailed.

title: Islamic Solidarity Summit Releases Final Communiqu?ā??
content: In the economic and social sphere, the communiqu?ā?? welcomed the increase in trade between OIC member states and advocated studying the possibility of establishing free trade zones. It stressed the importance of cooperation in the fight against poverty, unemployment, illiteracy and disease, as well as in efforts to achieve industrial development and food security. The Summit recommended a clearly defined plan for the advancement of science, technology and innovation and higher education, including progress in ???Ć?£the peaceful use of technology under the auspices of the International Energy Agency (IAEA) in order to support sustainable development.???Ć?Finally, the communiqu?ā?? called for supporting development in Africa, reaffirming the commitment to achieving the Millennium Development Goals in OIC member states. Addressing the problem of extremism, the Summit condemned all forms of immoderation and deviant thought as contradictory to the Islamic values of understanding, tolerance, dialogue, and pluralism. It took a firm stand against terrorism and praised the efforts made by the government of Saudi Arabia in the establishment of the International Counterterrorism Center and the King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud International Center for Dialogue between the Followers of Religions and Cultures. On the issue of reform, the communiqu?ā?? stressed that change must come from within the Muslim world and should center on the development of scientific advances and the achievement of national unity and equality. The Summit also expressed deep concern over the situation in the Mali and Sahel region, condemned the oppression of the Rohingya Muslims in Myanmar, and demanded the withdrawal of Armenian troops from Azerbaijani territories. Additionally, it expressed its ???Ć?£solidarity and full support for Sudan, Somalia, Afghanistan and Jammu and Kashmir, Iraq, Yemen, the Ivory Coast, the Union of Comoros and the Republic of Turkish-Cyprus in addressing the challenges facing these countries.???Ć?Regarding Syria, the communiqu?ā?? stressed the need to preserve the unity, sovereignty and independence of Syria, while strongly condemning the Syrian government for killing thousands of unarmed civilians. It called for the immediate cessation of violence, the release of detainees, and the provision of humanitarian aid to those in need. However, it stated, ???Ć?£In light of the failure of Syrian authorities to reach practical results in the implementation of the initiative of U.N.-Arab Envoy to resolve the Syrian crisis, the Arab initiative, and the decisions of the Executive Committee of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation and in view of the intransigence of the Syrian authorities and their commitment to resolving the situation through a military solution, the Summit decides to suspend the membership of the Syrian Arab Republic in the Organization of Islamic Cooperation and all its subsidiary organs and to enforce the recommendation of the Executive Committee meeting at the ministerial level in this regard on June 24, 2012.???Ć?In the political sphere, the communiqu?ā?? affirmed that the situation in Palestine is the central issue of the Islamic world. It demanded an end to the Israeli occupations and the establishment of an independent Palestinian state based on the pre-1967 borders and with Al-Quds [Jerusalem] as its capital. Furthermore, it condemned Israel???Ć?Ös attempts to alter the character of Al-Quds and commended the efforts of Saudi Arabia and other countries to defend that city???Ć?Ös Islamic holy sites. The 4th Extraordinary Islamic Solidarity Summit issued a final communiqu?ā?? yesterday evening following two days of deliberations in Makkah. The participants, including leaders and delegations from Muslim countries around the world, stressed that cooperation and unity are imperative in solving the many crises that plague the Islamic world. They advocated the establishment of ???Ć?£practical programs in political, educational, economic and social fields???Ć?that will enable Muslims to positively associate with one another, undertake joint actions, and reject political discord and sectarian strife.

title: King Abdullah Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet approved the appointments of Jamal Al-Mulhem as Undersecretary of the Mayor for Reconstruction and Projects at the Eastern Province Governate (15th rank); Sultan Al-Sudairi as Assistant Undersecretary for Security Affairs at the Jazan Governorate (14th rank); Suleiman Al-Tuweijiri as Director General of Customs of the King Fahd Causeway at Saudi Customs (14th rank); Saad Al-Sayyari as Director General of the Minister???Ć?Ös Office at the Ministry of Telecommunications and Information Technology (14th rank); Amer Al-Dossari as Director General of the Assir Branch of the General Presidency for the Commission of the Promotion of Virtue and Prevention of Vice (14th rank); and Abdullah Al-Qurashi as Director General of the Financial Affairs Department at the General Secretariat of the Cabinet (14th rank). Addressing its remaining agenda items, the Cabinet approved the Arab Agreement to Combat Information Technology Crimes; authorized the President and the head of the Board of Directors of the Saudi Commission for Wildlife to sign a Memorandum of Understanding on the Preservation and Management of Sea Cows; and approved the appointment of Saleh Al-Rasheed as Acting Director General of the Industrial Cities Commission and Technology Fields on the Board of Directors of the Saudi Industrial Development Fund for the remainder of his predecessor???Ć?Ös term. The Cabinet then discussed a number of domestic issues, including the Forum on Family Cases in Islamic courts, which was organized by the Ministry of Justice as part of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Project to develop the judiciary sector. Conversely, the Cabinet welcomed the U.N. Security Council resolution authorizing the deployment of an international observer mission to oversee a ceasefire in Syria. It underscored the importance of expediting the implementation of the ceasefire agreement in a way that ends the violence and guarantees the delivery of humanitarian aid to civilians. The ministers also expressed disapproval over the visit made by Iranian President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad to the United Arab Emirates???Ć?island of Abu Musa, which is currently occupied by Iran. They characterized the act as a violation of the UAE???Ć?Ös sovereignty and detrimental to the efforts being made to find a peaceful settlement to the issue through direct negotiations or international arbitration. After reviewing a number of reports on regional and international developments, the Cabinet renewed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös condemnation of the terrorist bombing that took place last week in the Bahraini village of Al-Aker, injuring seven policemen. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz began the weekly Cabinet meeting at his residence in Rawdhat Khuraim today with a briefing on his latest consultations with foreign leaders, including talks he held with Turkish Prime Minister Recep Tayyeb Erdogan.

title: Minister of Foreign Affairs Meets with Canadian Foreign Minister
content: The Canadian Foreign Minister also met today with President of the Saudi Human Rights Commission Dr. Bandar Al-Aiban. During their discussion, Dr. Al-Aiban reviewed the efforts exerted by the Saudi government to promote human rights and establish justice and equality at home and abroad. He also highlighted the grave human rights violations being committed against unarmed civilians in Syria and Palestine and called for international action to address these situations. Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal received Canadian Foreign Minister John Baird in Riyadh today. During the meeting, they discussed bilateral relations and regional and international issues of common concern.

title: Minister of Justice Receives World Bank Delegation
content: Minister of Justice Dr. Mohammed bin Abdulkarim Al-Essa received in Riyadh today a delegation from the World Bank headed by Julian Valkn and Joyce Ibrahim. During the meeting, the two sides reviewed judicial procedures in the Kingdom and the achievements of the Project of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz for the Development of the Judiciary.

title: Justice Minister Meets with U.S. Supreme Court Chief Justice
content: Minister of Justice Dr. Mohammed bin Abdulkareem Al-Isa met in Washington yesterday with U.S. Supreme Court Chief Justice John Roberts. During the meeting, Dr. Al-Isa briefed Chief Justice Roberts on efforts to develop the judiciary in the Kingdom within the framework of the Judiciary Development Project of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz. He noted that the Saudi judiciary provides strong guarantees that enhance the confidence bestowed upon it as a protector of rights and freedoms.

title: GCC Leaders Conclude 32nd Session of Supreme Council
content: The summit was attended by Bahraini King Hamad bin Isa Al-Khalifa; Kuwaiti Emir Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah; Qatari Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al-Thani UAE Vice President Sheikh Mohammed bin Rashid Al-Maktoum; Oman???Ć?Ös Sultan Qaboos bin Saeed; and many other senior GCC officials. Finally, the statement called for the greater integration of GCC youths into society by intensifying communication and cooperation through the employment of educational, media, cultural, sports and scouting activities. The Declaration noted that the process of development and comprehensive reform must be accelerated in each GCC state, in order to achieve greater participation for citizens, while maintaining security, stability, and social welfare. Furthermore, national unity must be consolidated by ensuring the equality of all men and women and confronting foreign attempts to create sectarian division. The document welcomed the proposal of King Abdullah, made during his opening speech, to form a GCC Union. It called for the highest degree of economic integration between GCC countries, encompassing a customs union, monetary union and common market. It also advocated the deepening of defense and security cooperation and the activation of GCC diplomacy to serve national, Arab and Islamic causes at the international level. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz and other leaders and heads of delegations of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) states concluded the meetings of 32nd session of the GCC Supreme Council at Dir???Ć?Öiya Palace in Riyadh yesterday. Afterwards, GCC Secretary General Dr. Abdullatif bin Rashid Al-Zayani read a final statement, referred to as the Riyadh Declaration.

title: Annual SAMA Report Reflects Strength of Saudi Economy
content: During another meeting, Dr. Al-Jasser presented the report to Crown Prince Nayef bin Abdulaziz. The presentation was attended by Minister of Finance Dr. Ibrahim Al-Assaf; Minister of Defense Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz; Commander of the National Guard Prince Miteb bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz; SAMA Deputy Governor Dr. Abdulrahman Al-Homaidi; and other senior SAMA officials. In terms of restructuring the economy and reforming regulations, Dr. Al-Jasser noted that Saudi Arabia was ranked 12th in ease of doing business out of 183 countries by the Business Performance Report issued by the World Bank for the year 2012. He also praised the government???Ć?Ös prudent oil policies, which have helped protect the Kingdom from the financial crisis and reduced the public debt from 104 percent of GDP in 1999 to approximately seven percent in 2011. Outlining the challenges ahead, Dr. Al-Jasser cited the need to create more jobs, diversify the economy away from oil dependency, and reduce the soaring domestic consumption of oil and gas. Addressing the future, Dr. Al-Jasser predicted that growth in the Kingdom will accelerate, largely due to unprecedented government spending on development projects. He also cited a number of initiatives that will improve quality of life and lower living expenses. These include: the establishment of the Ministry of Housing; the construction of 500,000 residential units for the poor worth SR 250 billion [$66.66 billion] over five years; the increase in housing loans from SR 300,000 [$79,995] to SR 500,000 [$133,349]; the fixing of basic salaries for citizens at SR 3,000 [$800] per month; the distribution of SR 2,000 [$533] monthly for every job-seeker; the increase in the Saudi Credit and Savings Bank???Ć?Ös capital by SR 30 billion [$8 billion]; the greater number of social insurance beneficiaries allowed in each family, from eight to fifteen; the rise in the government???Ć?Ös bursary to charities by 50 percent, bringing it to SR 450 million [$120.02 million]; the granting of soft vacancies at universities for students from poor families; the appropriation of SR 16 billion [$4.27 billion] for the expansion of hospitals and health dispensaries; and the increase in financing for private hospitals from SR 50 million [$13.33 million] to SR 200 million [$53.33 million]. Instead, Dr. Al Jasser reported, the Kingdom enjoyed its eleventh consecutive year of growth, at a rate of 4.1. percent, with the non-oil economy growing at 4.9 percent. A budget surplus of 5.2 percent of Gross Domestic Product (GDP) was achieved, he added. Furthermore, the Kingdom saw its twelfth consecutive balance of payments surplus, equaling SR 250.3 billion [$66.74 billion]. Dr. Al-Jasser reported that the inflation rate increased slightly from 5.1 percent in 2009 to 5.3 percent in 2010, falling to 5.2 percent by October 2011. He attributed the rise to the soaring costs of food and rent. Delivering a speech in which he summarized his agency???Ć?Ös findings, Dr. Al-Jasser congratulated King Abdullah for implementing policies that have led to continuous growth. Despite the global financial crisis that has dragged down leading economies, Dr. Al-Jasser noted that the Kingdom has avoided serious repercussions. Specifically, he noted the lack of the public and private indebtedness that has devastated other nations. Governor of the Saudi Arabian Monetary Agency (SAMA) Dr. Mohammed Al-Jasser delivered the 47th annual SAMA report on economic conditions in the Kingdom to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz during a meeting in Riyadh today. The report covers fiscal year 1431/1432 H. (2010), as well as the first quarter of the current year.

title: Forbes: King Abdullah Ranks Among the Seven Most Powerful People in the World
content: The U.S. financial magazine Forbes has once again ranked the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz among the seven most powerful personalities in the world. Explaining the ranking, Forbes hailed the Saudi monarch for embarking on reforms and adopting moderate stances on a wide array of issues. Specifically, it noted the decision to allow women the right to run for office and to vote in municipal elections. The magazine also noted that the King has spent more than $130 billion to fund social projects and has initiated the King Abdullah Project to Modernize General education.

title: King Abdullah Chairs Cabinet Session
content: Finally, the Cabinet approved the appointments of Dr. Abdullah Ansari as Director General of Legal Affairs and International Cooperation at the Ministry of Interior (15th rank); Ahmad Al-Zahrani as Director General of the Makkah branch of the Ministry of Justice (15th rank); Abdulaziz Mefleh as Administrative Consultant at the Ministry of Justice (15th rank); Dr. Riyadh Najm as Undersecretary for Media Affairs at the Ministry of Culture and Information (15th rank); and Hamad Al-Sadoun as Scientific Research Expert at King Abdulaziz City for Sciences and Technology (KACST) (14th rank). Addressing its remaining agenda items, the Cabinet authorized the President of the Saudi Red Crescent Authority to sign a memorandum of understanding between the Saudi Red Crescent Authority and the Turkish Red Crescent Society; authorized the Minister of Higher Education to sign a draft memorandum of scientific and educational cooperation between the Saudi Ministry of Higher Education and the Hungarian Ministry of National Resources; and approved the final accounts of the Saudi Ports Authority for fiscal year 1430/1431AH. On the international front, the ministers reviewed regional and global developments, including the events surrounding the opening of the 66th session of the United Nations General Assembly in New York. They welcomed the launching of the Center for Combating Terrorism, which was proposed by King Abdullah in 2005, and supported the application submitted by Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas asking for full U.N. membership for a new Palestinian state. Next, the Cabinet listened to reports on events that took place inside the Kingdom last week, including the International Conference on the Takfeer Phenomenon, Its Causes, Effects and Treatment in Madinah and the Okaz cultural market in Taif. For its part, the Cabinet hailed the speech delivered on Sunday by King Abdullah to the opening session of the third year of the Majlis Al-Shoura???Ć?Ös fifth term. In particular, they welcomed the King???Ć?Ös decision to allow women to serve in the Majlis Al-Shoura and to permit them to participate in future municipal elections. The ministers also praised the Shoura Council???Ć?Ös domestic and foreign policies and endeavors. At the outset of the weekly Cabinet session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh today, the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz expressed his appreciation for the numerous good wishes sent from leaders around the world on the occasion of Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös 81st National Day. He asked Allah Almighty to perpetuate the security and stability of the Kingdom, allowing it to continue serving the Arab and Islamic worlds. Then, the King gave a briefing on the recent meeting he held with Yemeni President Ali Abdullah Saleh, during which he stressed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös support for its neighbor.

title: White House Welcomes Expansion of Saudi Women???Ć?Ös Political Participation
content: King Abdullah???Ć?Ös announcement has been met with praise from governments around the world. In an a statement issued today, the White House welcomed the announcement of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz that Saudi women will be appointed to the Majlis Al-Shoura (consultative council) and will be allowed to participate in future municipal elections. ???Ć?£We welcome Saudi King Abdullah???Ć?Ös announcement today that women will serve as full members of the Shoura Council in the next session, and will have the right to participate in future elections,???Ć?said the White House statement. It added, ???Ć?£These reforms recognize the significant contributions women in Saudi Arabia make to their society and will offer them new ways to participate in the decisions that affect their lives and communities.???Ć?

title: Majlis Al-Shoura Approves Judicial Reforms
content: In a statement following the session, Secretary General of the Majlis Al-Shoura Dr. Mohammad bin Abdullah Al-Ghamdi said that the council is following the intentions of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz to reinvigorate the judiciary and to promote and support judicial reforms financially as well as morally. Presided over by Speaker Abdullah bin Mohammad bin Ibrahim Al-Asheikh, the Majlis also approved judicial and administrative formations for courts, new judicial districts and notaries. During its meeting in Riyadh today, the Majlis Al-Shoura (Consultative Council) tasked the Ministry of Justice with drawing up regulations and setting safeguards to prevent the marriage of underage girls.

title: Cabinet Approves Creation of National Anti-Corruption Commission
content: The Cabinet has issued a decision, No. 165, dated 28/05/1432 H., approving the organization of a National Anti-Corruption Commission. The aims of the Commission are to uphold integrity, to promote the principle of transparency, and to fight against financial and administrative corruption in all its forms and manifestations. Its duties will include:

title: Saudi Arabia's Address to the U.N. General Assembly
content: Finally, Prince Saud called for reorm at the United Nation, specifically empowering the General Assembly, rather than allowing the Security Council a monopoly on important decision-making. In the statement, Prince Saud expressed grief at the devastating floods in Pakistan, outlining the major Saudi humanitarian relief effort and requesting that other nations to contribute as much as possible. He also insisted that states honor their pledges in the effort to achieve the Millennium Development Goals, which aim to significantly reduce global poverty by 2015. The Foreign Minister noted that Saudi Arabia has been at the forefront of global humanitarian efforts, donating $99.75 billion in assistance to more than 95 different states between 1973 and 2009. Touching on other regional issues, Prince Saud called for a nuclear weapons-free zone in the Middle East, calling on Iran to cooperate with the International Atomic Energy Agency and Israel to sign the Nuclear Nonproliferation Treaty (NPT). Addressing the Middle East peace process, the Saudi Foreign Minister once again called on Israel to accept the Arab Peace Initiative and to end all settlement building activities in the Occupied Territories. He expressed hope that the current U.S.-mediated peace talks would bear fruit, calling it a ???Ć?£moment of truth???Ć?and a ???Ć?£crossroads???Ć?and warning that ???Ć?£the consequences of failure this time are too catastrophic to imagine.???Ć?Prince Saud stressed, ???Ć?£Respect for United Nations resolutions and the principles of international law is the only way to resolve chronic international conflicts and defuse focal points of tension, thereby preventing terrorists from exploiting feelings of despair and frustration brought about by subjection to injustice, aggression and occupation.???Ć?Saudi Arabia has called for the strengthening of global cooperation to confront terrorism, which is at odds with the principles of tolerance, peace and respect promoted by Islam, Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal said in a statement issued in New York on the occasion of the 65th session of the General Assembly of the United Nations. Prince Saud outlined the many contributions the Kingdom has made in the fight against terrorism, including King Abdullah???Ć?Ös interfaith dialogue initiative; the 2005 Counterterrorism Conference in Riyadh; the establishment of a successful terrorist rehabilitation program; and various other military, cultural, educational and economic efforts.

title: Kingdom Aims for the Top in Global Competitiveness
content: Another example of this support is the ???Ć?£Saudi Fast Growth 100,???Ć?a new national program designed to promote entrepreneurship and innovation. Professor Michael Porter of the Harvard Business School notes, ???Ć?£Entrepreneurship is alive and well in Saudi Arabia. The Saudi Fast Growth 100 companies, led by dynamic men and women, represent the leading edge of a new approach to Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös competitiveness. These entrepreneurs are shattering our perception of what???Ć?Ös really going on in Saudi Arabia.???Ć?The U.S.-Arab Tradeline, a publication of the National U.S.-Arab Chamber of Commerce, describes in its Spring 2010 issue the numerous ways in which the Saudi government and private sector are supporting business and educational programs aimed at creating a more dynamic economy. From the founding of the King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST) to the creation of the National Entrepreneurship Center, the Saudi government has been taking major steps to promote education and business creation. With entrepreneurship and innovation expanding rapidly in the Kingdom in the past decade, Saudi Arabia has set a goal for itself: to be among the top ten most competitive nations in the world by the end of 2010. That goal is not far from being achieved: In 2005, Saudi Arabia was ranked 67th in the Doing Business report published by the World Bank; by 2009, it had climbed the ranks to the 13th spot.

title: 5th Anniversary of King Abdullah???Ć?Ös Reign Celebrated
content: For his part, Iraqi President Jalal Talabani praised the role of King Abdullah in serving Saudi Arabia and all of the world???Ć?Ös Muslims. He declared that the efforts exerted by the King to enhance dialogue among the followers of religions are appreciated by all and welcomed the Saudi monarch???Ć?Ös concerted efforts to strengthen bilateral relations. Rector of King Fahd University of Petroleum and Minerals Dr. Khalid bin Saleh Al-Sultan pointed out that during King Abdullah???Ć?Ös reign, higher education has received a great deal of attention. He noted that the state???Ć?Ös budget for education and training has risen significantly and the number of universities in the Kingdom has increased exponentially. Furthermore, he highlighted the launch of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Program for Foreign Scholarships and the establishment of the King Abdullah University for Science and Technology (KAUST). The Grand Mufti of Saudi Arabia, Sheikh Abdulaziz bin Abdullah bin Mohammed Al-Asheikh, commended the King for promoting national dialogue and for pushing to develop and improve religious institutions. Najran Governor Prince Misha???Ć?Öal bin Abdullah bin Abdulaziz noted that the King has implemented several massive development projects across the country, including many that contribute to the comfort and convenience of pilgrims and visitors to the Holy Places. Foreign Minister Prince Saud Al-Faisal lauded the King???Ć?Ös wise handling of regional and international relations ???Ć?actions that have raised the Kingdom???Ć?Ös global stature, while contributing to stability at home. In particular, he welcomed the King???Ć?Ös efforts to promote Arab and Islamic reconciliation. In an article written for the occasion, the Secretary General of the Cabinet, Abdulrahman bin Mohammed Al-Sadhan, pointed to King Abdullah???Ć?Ös launching of his inter-faith and cross-cultural dialogue initiative as a major milestone. In addition, he praised the establishment of the King Abdullah City for Atomic and Renewable Energy (KACARE). The 5th anniversary of the accession to the throne by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz was celebrated throughout the Kingdom today. Senior Saudi officials and foreign leaders offered their good wishes to the King and highlighted the major achievements made by Saudi Arabia during his reign. King Abdullah???Ć?Ös policies, they noted, have led to great strides in the fields of education, health, social affairs, security, foreign relations, energy, business, communications and national development.

title: U.S.-Saudi Business Opportunities Forum Opens in Chicago
content: Trade between Saudi Arabia and the U.S. stood at SR193.3 billion [$51.54 billion] in 2008, and direct U.S. exports to the Kingdom are projected to rise to SR 63 billion [$16.8 billion] in 2010. Abdulrahman Al-Juraisi, the Council???Ć?Ös Vice Chairman, noted that the U.S. was ???Ć?£one of the Kingdom???Ć?Ös most important trade partners.???Ć?He added, ???Ć?£The business sectors in both countries have a significant role in boosting economic relations.???Ć?According to Omar Bahalwa of the Committee for International Trade at the Council of Saudi Chambers, the first day of the gathering will focus on the stability of the Saudi economy, infrastructure opportunities, education, industry, and financial issues, while on the second day workshops will be held exploring commercial investment opportunities in the Kingdom. Discussions will center on energy, petrochemicals, electricity, water, infrastructure, knowledge, financial markets, trade and investment, and agriculture. ???Ć?£The Kingdom will present to Americans at the forum investments in a wide range of fields, worth around a trillion Saudi Riyals,???Ć?Bahalwa said. The forum, which will include the signing of five main agreements and a number of sub-agreements, will focus on the small to intermediate-scale sectors, technology transfers, and the exchange of job opportunities, says a report published today by the Saudi Gazette. Minister of Petroleum and Mineral Resources Ali Al-Naimi delivered a speech in which he stressed the importance of the energy relationship between the Kingdom and the United States. The gathering is also being attended by Minister of Finance Dr. Ibrahim Al-Assaf, Minister of Commerce and Industry Abdullah Alireza and Saudi Ambassador to the U.S. Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir, ???Ć?£The Saudi-U.S. relationship has grown and deepened over the past seven decades,???Ć?said Saudi Ambassador Al-Jubeir. ???Ć?£This conference will highlight the opportunities available to citizens of both countries as a result of the strong economic ties between them.???Ć?The U.S.-Saudi Business Opportunities Forum, a two-day event organized by the Council of Saudi Chambers of Commerce and Industry, the U.S. Saudi Arabian Business Council and a number of notable figures from the Saudi and American business communities, opened today in Chicago. Representatives from the Kingdom are on hand to present a diverse selection of investment opportunities to the American audience.

title: Ambassador Meets I-SWEEEP 2010 Participants
content: I-SWEEEP 2010 brought together over 1,000 young scientists from 70 countries to Houston, Texas April 14-19. Albader Humid received an Honorable Mention Awards for his safe iron project; Abdulrahman Alfozan and Abdulmohsen Alblushi received silver medals for their Smart Fire Detector and Solar Panel Launch Support & Release Mechanism for the Castor Space System, respectively; and Abdulaziz Alghunaim received a bronze medal for his ???Ć?£Action Shields - A Helpful Solution to Save Lives in Building Emergencies. Saudi Arabia was represented by four participants, all of whom were honored. Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel A. Al-Jubeir today met with members of the Saudi delegation and a participant in the 2010 International Sustainable World Project Olympiad, known as I-SWEEEP.?é??

title: Prince Salman Receives Former Secretary of State James Baker
content: Riyadh Governor Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz received former U.S. Secretary of State James Baker and former U.S. Ambassador to the Kingdom Robert Jordan in Riyadh yesterday. During the meeting, they discussed Saudi-U.S. relations and other topics of common interest. The meeting was also attended by Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Special Advisor to Prince Salman.

title: Second Private Radio License Granted
content: The Minister of Culture and Information today awarded the second private FM radio broadcast license to Extreme Creativity Trade Holdings Limited Company at an amount of SR 66 million ($17.6 million).

title: Statement Following the Conclusion of Pensacola Naval Air Station Attack Investigation
content: Finally, close to a million Saudis have come to the US for their education, training and other reasons over several decades. The overwhelming majority of them are law-abiding citizens who view themselves as unofficial ambassadors of the kingdom. Many consider the United States as a second home and treat their American colleagues, classmates and neighbors as an extended part of their family. Thousands of them took to social media to condemn the attacker and to extend their condolences to Americans, as did our leadership and our ambassador to the United States. Saudi Arabia has been at the forefront of the international community???Ć?Ös effort to counter terrorist groups for many years.?é?? The kingdom has used every means at its disposal to counter the men, mindset and money that allow Al Qaeda, ISIS and other terrorist groups to recruit followers and to threaten communities and even entire nations.?é?? In addition, the terrorists who have struck the US and many other nations have also targeted Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös people, leadership, military personnel and even our holiest religious institutions in Mecca and Medina on multiple occasions. Violent extremists continue to pose a serious challenge to the security of the United States, Saudi Arabia and most countries around the world. Terror groups have recruited thousands of people from dozens of countries. The kingdom has worked closely with the US to counter this global threat. It is worth noting that the military training that the US provides to Saudi military personnel has enabled Saudi soldiers, pilots and sailors to fight along their American counterparts and against our common foes. The close cooperation between the two nations on intelligence matters and issues related to counter terrorism has saved the lives of many in the US, Saudi Arabia and elsewhere and has made the world a safer place.?é?? Approximately 28,000 Saudis have undergone military training in the US over the course of several decades without incident. Saudi Arabia has fully cooperated with American investigators looking into the motivations of the attacker. Saudi Arabia will continue to cooperate with US authorities should they require additional information. In addition, the Kingdom has determined that 21 cadets have demonstrated conduct unbecoming of an officer in the Saudi Royal Air Force or Royal Navy and therefore have been unenrolled from their training program in the U.S. military. They will be returning to the Kingdom. The disturbed and radicalized individual who carried out this terrible attack acted alone. He does not represent the hundreds of thousands of Saudis who have lived, studied and trained in the United States over the past several decades, nor does his heinous act represent the values of Saudi Arabia. Following the conclusion of the investigation by US law enforcement authorities into the shooting incident at Pensacola Naval Air Station on December 6, 2019 involving a Royal Saudi Air Force cadet, the official spokesperson of the Embassy of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia in Washington would like to stress the following points:

title: Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Meets with Commander of the CENTCOM
content: The Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammed bin Salman met at his office at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah this evening with the Commander of the US Central Command General Joseph Fotel.?é?? During the meeting, they reviewed areas of bilateral cooperation, particularly in the military aspect, as well as the joint efforts in fighting terrorism and combating extremism, in addition to the latest developments in the region.

title: Prince Khalid bin Salman Officially Begins Assignment as Saudi Ambassador to the U.S.
content: Prince Khalid is the 10th?é??Ambassador of Saudi Arabia to the U.S. since 1945. Prince Khalid graduated from the King Faisal Air Academy in Riyadh and was ???Ć?Äcommissioned a second lieutenant in the RSAF. He received pilot ???Ć?Ätraining in the United States at Lackland Air Force Base in San Antonio, Texas and Columbus Air Force Base in Columbus, Mississippi. He also studied advanced electronic warfare in France.???Ć?Most recently, Prince Khalid was a senior civilian advisor in the Ministry of Defense. Previously, he was an officer in the Royal Saudi Air Force (RSAF) where he flew F15-S combat missions in Syria and Yemen.?é?? As a result of a back condition that prevented him from flying, Prince Khalid served as a tactical intelligence officer at ???Ć?Äthe King Abdulaziz Air Base in Dhahran.?é?? ???Ć?£My first priority is to build on the outcomes of the Riyadh Summit to foster closer cooperation on our collective security and to expand our economic partnership,???Ć?said the Ambassador.?é?? ???Ć?£I also want to reintroduce Saudi Arabia to the United States. Under our new leadership, Saudi Arabia is continuing to change and modernize. I want to engage Americans in new ways to introduce and describe the important economic and social reforms now underway.???Ć????Ć?£I am honored and excited to represent the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to the United States of America. Close friends and long-time allies, now more than ever, our two countries are aligned in shared interests and common purpose,???Ć?said the Ambassador. ???Ć?£We are expanding trade and investment, increasing cultural exchanges, and fighting terrorism side by side.???Ć?(WASHINGTON, DC) -?é?? HRH Prince Khalid bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al-Saud officially began his tenure as the Ambassador of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to the United States today with the presentation of his credentials to President Donald J. Trump at a White House ceremony.

title: Foreign Ministers of Saudi Arabia, UAE, Bahrain, Egypt and Kuwait meet with US Secretary of State
content: Saudi Arabia's Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir, Egypt's Minister of Foreign Affairs Sameh Shukri, Bahrain's Minister of Foreign Affairs Sheikh Khalid bin Ahmed bin Mohammed Al Khalifa, UAE's Minister of State for Foreign Affairs Dr. Anwar bin Mohammed Gargash and Kuwait's Minister of State for Cabinet Affairs Sheikh Mohammed Abdullah Al-Mubarak Al-Sabah met in Jeddah today with US Secretary of State Rex Tillerson.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed all aspects of the crisis with the State of Qatar.

title: Crown Prince Meets with US Secretary of State
content: Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, met in Jeddah today with US Secretary of State Rex Tillerson.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed means of enhancing bilateral relations as well as the developments in the Middle East and joint efforts in the fight against terrorism and extremism.?é?? The meeting was also attended by Prince Khalid bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Saudi Ambassador-designate to the US; and Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet finally adjourned after reviewing a number of local issues on its agenda. The Cabinet expressed the Kingdom's strong condemnation of the terrorist bombings that took place in northern Sinai in Egypt and the terrorist attack on a camp for displaced people in Anbar province in Iraq, expressing its condolences to the Egyptian and Iraqi governments, reconfirming the Kingdom's solidarity and stand with Egypt and Iraq and its firm positions against terrorism and extremism. The Cabinet reviewed the joint statement issued by Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Bahrain, and Egypt after receiving the Qatari reply from Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, Emir of Kuwait, renewing its thanks and appreciation to the Kuwaiti Emir for his endeavors and efforts to resolve the crisis with the Qatari government. The Statement was initially directed at the Qatari government to correct its track seeking to destabilize the countries in the region and intervene in their affairs. The Cabinet praised the security forces for thwarting a terrorist act during the holy month of Ramadan to target the Grand Holy Mosque in Makkah and its visitors. It also condemned the terrorist attacks that targeted security patrols in Qatif Governorate which resulted in martyring two security men and injuring others. The Cabinet stressed that security authorities in the Kingdom are capable of confronting such criminal plans and anyone else who tampers with the Kingdom's safety and stability. The Cabinet also expressed thanks to the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSrelief) in its fight to contain the cholera epidemic in Yemen and the provision of $66.7 million to the World Health Organization (WHO) and The United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) in this regard. The Cabinet commended the King's approval to adopt a public health policy, reflecting the keenness of the leadership over the health of its citizens. The Cabinet also expressed its appreciation to Germany, represented by Chancellor Angela Merkel for the good organization and management of the activities of the G20 leaders' summit, which discussed numerous issues including international efforts in combating terrorism and extremism. The Cabinet also appreciated the welcome of leaders of the G20 summit countries for the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's hosting G20 Summit in 2020. The Cabinet valued the King's invitation to hold a Saudi-African Summit in the Kingdom at the end of this year 2017 or beginning of next year 2018, as the invitation was conveyed by the Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir to the President of the 29th ordinary African Summit held in Ethiopia. At the outset of the session, the King welcomed Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz on the occasion of his selection as Crown Prince and his appointment as Deputy Prime Minister.?é?? King Salman also expressed his appreciation to Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz for all his efforts exerted in the service of the homeland.?é?? In addition, King Salman welcomed Prince Abdulaziz bin Saud bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Minister of Interior, wishing him success in his new post. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah on Monday afternoon.

title: Saudi Foreign Minister and US Secretary of State Confirm Strategic Partnership

It is unacceptable that they would be allowed to seize power and get away with it.???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir expressed an outlook for a peaceful resolution to the conflict in Yemen and called on the Houthi militias to end all separatist activities. ???Ć?£If Iran wants to be a normal country and wants others to treat it like a normal country, it has to act in accord with international law and the values and the mores of the international system that have existed for centuries,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. Minister Al-Jubeir criticized Iran for conducting destabilizing actions throughout the region, including in Yemen, Syria and Lebanon. The foreign minister said the world should judge Iran by its actions, not words. The two leaders outlined a renewed commitment to defense cooperation. Earlier today, the two countries signed a defense agreement worth more than $100 billion that will upgrade Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös military capabilities, allowing it to better protect its borders and aid in the global fight against terrorism. Secretary Tillerson praised the deal???Ć?Ös economic impact, stating that it would generate ???Ć?£hundreds of thousands???Ć?of American jobs. ???Ć?£If we can change the conversation in the Islamic world from enmity towards the U.S. to partnership with the U.S. and if we can change the conversation in the U.S. and in the West from enmity towards the Islamic world to one of partnership, we would have truly changed our world and we would have truly drowned the voices of extremism and we will have drained the swamp from which extremism and terrorism emanates,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. title: Foreign Minister: US President???Ć?Ös Visit Reflects Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös Pivotal Role in the Arab and Islamic Nations
content: "This visit will enhance, we believe, cooperation between the US and Arab and Islamic countries in combating terrorism and extremism. It will has enormous interests in the region and world," Al-Jubeir concluded. Al-Jubeir said Trump's visit to Saudi Arabia would include a bilateral summit, a meeting with the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) leaders and another with leaders of Arab and Islamic countries. The Kingdom has a key role in pushing the peace process forward. The Kingdom has huge investments in the US economy.?é?? The Kingdom is a big trading partner with the United States, which means existence of enormous economic and financial interests between the two countries. To achieve the goals set by President Trump, whether in restoring the US role, defeating ISIS, containing Iran, promoting peace or enhancing investment, trade and prosperity, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is the key to these issues. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is among the US most important allies in the Arab and Islamic world. The Kingdom is one of the most important international partners with regard to economy, trade and investment. "The US President's visit to the Kingdom is historic by all standards. The Saudi Arabia is the birthplace of Islam and serves the Two Holy Mosques. Terrorism and extremism cannot be defeated without the Kingdom. We are the closest partners in the fight against terrorism and extremism. The Kingdom is at the forefront of countries against Iran's negative and illegal activities.?é?? The Kingdom is at the forefront of countries fighting the terrorist ISIS organization. The Kingdom has provided the Arab Peace Initiative. "President Trump desires to restore the US role in the world which is welcomed by the US allies. He also expressed his desire to destroy the ISIS terrorist organization and the terrorist groups. President Trump also seeks to address the Iranian illegal practices in the region. All these matters are welcomed by the US allies in the region. So, we are allies with the US administration in these issues," Al-Jubeir added. The Foreign Minister stressed that Saudi Arabia is major player in combating terrorism and extremism.?é?? He said that the Kingdom has provided the Arab Peace Initiative which is a key reference to a Palestinian-Israeli peaceful solution where President Trump is seeking to end this conflict.?é?? He described the US President's visit to the Kingdom as historic by all standards. ???Ć?£The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is the first partner for the United States of America in the Arab and Islamic nations.?é?? The Kingdom is the first partner for the United States in combating terrorism and extremism as well as eradicating Daesh (ISIS) and Al-Qaeda. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is at the forefront of countries addressing the Iranian negative interferences in the regional affairs. This is an important issue for the United States. Therefore, the partnership with the Kingdom is very important," Minister Al-Jubeir said.
