content: Other key appointments also announced today included: Khaled bin Abdulaziz Al-Falih as minister of health and chairman of Saudi Aramco; Adel bin Mohammed Fakeih as minister of economy and planning; Mufrej bin Saad Al-Haqbani as minister of labor; and Hamad bin Abdulaziz Al-Suwailem as chief of the Royal Court at the rank of minister. Prince Saud Al-Faisal was relieved as foreign minister at his request for health reasons. He was appointed state minister, cabinet member, adviser and special envoy to King Salman, and supervisor on foreign affairs. Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir was appointed as the new foreign minister Prince Mohammed bin Salman was appointed as deputy crown prince and second deputy prime minister. He will retain his positions as minister of defense and president of the Council of Economic and Development Affairs. Crown Prince and Deputy Prime Minister Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz was relieved of his post at his request. Prince Mohammad bin Naif was appointed crown prince and deputy prime minister. He will retain his positions as minister of interior and president of the Council of Political and Security Affairs. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today issued a number royal orders, including the appointments of a new crown prince, deputy crown prince and foreign minister.

title: King Salman Calls for Economic Strength and Stability in Major Policy Address
content: King Salman urged the people of Saudi Arabia to stand united and said that Saudi Arabia would continue its efforts to achieve comprehensive, integrated and balanced development for all regions. ???Ć?£Ever since the Kingdom???Ć?Ös founding, the government has been making continuous efforts for development and modernization, without deviating from religious principles and social values while protecting the rights of all citizens,???Ć?said King Salman. King Salman also emphasized the need for the Kingdom to prioritize national security stating, ???Ć?£Security is everyone???Ć?Ös responsibility and will not allow anyone to tamper with our security and stability.???Ć?In response to the growing threat of extremism and violence in the region, King Salman condemned terrorism in all forms. He stated, ???Ć?£The Kingdom is focused on the fight against extremism and terrorism in all its forms and manifestations, whatever its sources, and in cooperation with brotherly and friendly countries and the international organizations in the fight against this detestable menace, and in seeking eradicating its roots and causes.???Ć?King Salman reinforced the Kingdom???Ć?Ös commitment to the teachings of Islam, stating that Saudi foreign policy would continue to defend Islamic causes globally. ???Ć?£Saudi foreign policy is always committed to the teachings of our religion, calling for love and peace in accordance with its principles,???Ć?said King Salman. King Salman addressed falling oil prices, assuring the Saudi people that the nation will remain economically stable. ???Ć?£We encourage and support medium and small enterprises to grow so that they become a strong economic area for a large segment of society,???Ć?King Salman stated. ???Ć?£The coming years will witness rich and great achievements that enhance industrial and services roles in the national economy.???Ć?Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz on today announced his domestic and foreign policies to a group of government officials, scholars and other dignitaries in Riyadh. King Salman emphasized his plan to build a more diversified economy and defend Islamic causes globally.

content: The Riyadh-based center was highly commended by sovereign states, community leaders and international and regional organizations working in the areas of relief and humanitarian services as well as visitors of the facility as provider of state-of-the-art organization. Since its inauguration, the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works (KSRelief) has provided a number of projects worth over $600 million to 127 governorates in Yemen in various fields including relief, humanitarian, shelter and environmental sanitation programs.

content: Established in May 2015, KSRelief has provided health, nutrition and sanitation aid and relief to 17 million people impacted by the conflict in Yemen. Partnering with international humanitarian organizations, KSRelief has established more than 100 health facilities, provided emergency food aid and supplied more than 350,000 gallons of fuel to medical facilities. Dr. Al-Rabeeah explained that the new donation comes as part of the $8.2 billion committed by the Kingdom within its humanitarian and developmental assistance to Yemen since April 2015. Saudi Arabia remains the single largest donor of humanitarian assistance to Yemen. Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabeeah the supervisor general for KSRelief, issued the announcement during the Geneva aid conference, a day-long meeting of international donors focused on responding to Yemen???Ć?Ös humanitarian crisis. content: King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (SKRelief) delivered 150 tons of dates as a gift from Saudi Arabia to Jordan.

title: Coalition Calls on UN to Ensure Delivery of Humanitarian Supplies to Yemeni Population
content: The coalition has called on the United Nations to exercise oversight of the Port of Hodeidah to safeguard the free flow of humanitarian deliveries. The coalition added that the other ports including Aden, Mukalla and other airports are open as routes for humanitarian supplies. The coalition accused that Houthi rebels of seizing and selling humanitarian supplies that pass through the Hodeidah.?é?? ???Ć?£The Coalition is deeply concerned about the humanitarian situation in Yemen. We are doing everything in our power to ensure that the Yemeni population receives the food and medicines it needs, the coalition said.?é?? ???Ć?£The Port of Hodeidah is an important entry point for humanitarian supplies. But it is currently in the hands of the Houthi rebels, who use it to smuggle arms and people. Houthi rebels also finance their ongoing violent campaign to overthrow the legitimate government by seizing humanitarian supplies and selling them for exorbitant sums. The Coalition Supporting Legitimacy in Yemen today called on the United Nations to ensure the free flow of humanitarian shipments through the Port of Hodeidah to alleviate the humanitarian crisis in Yemen.

content: King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSRelief) has launched an anti-dengue fever campaign in six governorates in Yemen.?é?? content: In implementation of the order of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to assist the Iraqi people, and in response to an appeal by the World Health Organization to help combat an outbreak of cholera in Iraq, a King Salman Humanitarian Aid & Relief Center (KSrelief) aircraft laden with more than 10 tons of medical supplies arrived at Baghdad International Airport yesterday evening.

content: The Saudi National Campaign for the Support of Brothers in Syria has distributed 231,049 winter items to 73,401 Syrian refugees in Al-Zaatari Refugee Camp in Jordan as part of its winter campaign.

content: The center launched a comprehensive educational program and training support to serve the ???Ć?Äeducation and its staff in Yemen, based on the arms of electronic gate for transmitting the lectures ???Ć?Äwith Yemeni contents presented by Yemeni teachers, satellite broadcasting, and training for ???Ć?Äteachers and replacing the printed educational curricula with digital ones, as well as applications ???Ć?Äon smart devices.???Ć?KSRelief constructed 300 housing units, including schools and mosques in Djibouti.?é?? title: Saudi Arabia Steps Up Assistance to Care for Ill and Injured in Yemen
content: The organization recently transferred a group of injured Yemenis from Taiz for treatment in Sudan, paying for the full cost of their travel and treatment. KSRelief has executed 51 projects in health, nutrition, water and environmental sanitation in Yemen totaling more than $208 million and benefiting 24.5 million Yemeni citizens, in addition to programs providing medical care and treatment for people inside Yemeni territory. KSRelief has entered into a number of agreements with private hospitals in Aden and Taiz aimed at providing full medical care for more than 750 wounded in these provinces. KSRelief seeks to activate the role of government hospitals to treat the injured inside Yemeni territories by providing medical services and treatment devices. Saudi Arabia, working through KSRelief, is the largest donor of humanitarian assistance to the Yemeni people. KSRelief was established in May 2015 under the sponsorship of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz. title: Saudi Arabia Leads International Efforts to Save Children, Rehabilitate Yemen???Ć?Ös Health Sector
content: In collaboration with the World Health Organization (WHO), KSRELIEF provided basic services such as diabetes and cancer medicines, and support the operation of emergency rooms by providing 350,000 gallon of fuel to 88 hospitals and 19 health facilities in Yemen. To date, KSRELIEF provided 12,000,000 vaccines for Yemeni children against measles and rubella diseases and tetanus and whooping cough. KSRELIEF has sent 58 trucks carrying over 600 tons of medical and therapeutic supplies to all Yemeni provinces, starting with Aden and Marib.?é?? KSRELIEF also worked closely with the UN International Children???Ć?Ös Emergency Fund (UNICEF) in training 3,153 health workers on the social management of acute malnutrition and opened 854 centers for therapeutic nutrition program. The program also provided medical treatment and follow up cases of acute malnutrition.?é?? KSRELIEF mobile health teams have treated over a quarter of a million children suffering from acute malnutrition and provided immunization and postnatal care to over 1.2 million children throughout Yemen.?é?? The teams also provided services for pregnant women and post-delivery care for over 220,000 women. The Riyadh-based King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Centre (KSRELIEF) is leading international efforts to rehabilitate Yemen???Ć?Ös health sector which has nearly been destroyed since the Houthi militias and the forces loyal to deposed president Ali Abdullah Saleh usurped power in Sana???Ć?Öa.?é?? The United Nations detailed Houthi militias???Ć?gross violations of international humanitarian law, including attacks on hospitals and medical facilities.?é?? Houthi militias and Saleh loyalists have also prevented medical supplies from reached the needy.

title: Houthi Militias Mining Areas as They Retreat
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid (KSRELIEF) is carrying out numerous projects to alleviate the suffering of all civilians in Yemen, including supporting rehabilitation of injured civilians?é??and supporting International Committee of Red Cross's (ICRC) artificial limbs centers in Yemen. Houthi militias and forces loyal to deposed president Ali Abdullah Salah are minding areas liberate by the Yemeni national army in an effort to slow the advance of pro-government forces. The mines have already claimed the lives of many civilians and prevented the return of others.?é?? Former governor of Al-Jawf Governorate Hussein Awadi said that he believed that the Houthi militias and their allies?é??had planted over 30 thousand mines with varied sizes in the governorate.?é?? Saudi-led Arab Coalition to Support Legitimacy in Yemen are working with the Yemeni national army to clear landmines to help conflict-affected communities to return to their homes and rebuild their lives.

The regional director of the National Saudi Campaign to Support Syrian Brothers, Dr. Badr Al-Samhan, said that more than 83,700 Syrian patients benefited from the Saudi Specialist Clinics 2015.

title: King Salman Center to Provide Yemen with 100,000 Foodstuff Baskets
content: In line with the directives of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works endorsed providing 100,000 baskets of foodstuff to Taiz, Yemeni. The action also came in the light of conclusions reached by participants of the peace talks, sponsored by the UN in Switzerland, in which humanitarian assistance was to be provided immediately to Taiz.

title: Saudi National Campaign Distributes 14,000 Winter Clothes to Syrian Families
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria distributed 14,000 pieces of winter clothes to Syrian refugees in Mafraq Governorate, Jordan during the 14th station of its project My brother, Your warmth is My Goal 3. Through this station, the campaign provided winter clothes necessary for 3,500 refugees and included winter items such as blankets, jackets, sweaters and caps.

title: Kingdom Distributes Winter Clothes to Syrian Refugees
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria is to begin the distribution of winter clothing to Syrian refugees in Jordan, Lebanon and Turkey, in addition to displaced Syrians inside Syria.

title: King Salman Center Begins Distribution of Aid to Cyclone Chapala Victims in Yemen
content: The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Workings has begun the distribution relief supplies to the victims of Cyclone Chapala in Radhum Province, Yemen. The aid is part of the Kingdom???Ć?Ös support of the Yemeni people.

title: Food Distribution Continues in Yemen
content: The 5000 food baskets are part of a 130,000 food basket allotted for Taiz Province, in addition to the overall food security program implemented by King Salman Center to assist all the needy in all parts of Yemen in collaboration with the World Food Program at a total amount of more than $140 million. The King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid distributed 5000 food baskets to the needy in Yemen's Taiz Province. The distribution was carried out by the Islamic Relief Commission, the Islamic Relief Coalition, and local organizations.?é??

title: 5th Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The 5th relief airplane from the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works arrived at Yemen's Socotra Airport.  The aircraft was loaded with 10 tons of relief aid to be distributed in Socotra under supervision of the Center's specialized team.

title: Kingdom Distributes Winter Supplies to Syria Refugees in Lebanon
content: The director of the campaigns Lebanon operation, Walid Jalal, said that the distribution in other regions will be done sequentially according to the plan prepared for this purpose and priorities in provided the most needed areas. The Saudi National Campaign to Support the Brothers in Syria has distributed more than 18,500 pieces of winter clothes to Syrian families in Baalbek, Lebanon.

title: Kingdom Distributes Winter Clothing to Syrian Refugees
content: The Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria has continued its winter project by distributing winter clothing to 428 Syrian families in northern Lebanon.

title: Saudi Campaign Launches New Winter Program for Syria Refuges in Jordan
content: The campaign part of seasonal projects being carried out by the Saudi National Campaign in Jordan, Turkey, Lebanon and inside Syria. The Saudi National Campaign to Support the Syrian Brothers has launched the third stage of its winter campaign distribute 13,000 winter clothes to more than 500 Syrian families at refugees in Ajlun Region in northern Jordan.

title: King Salman Center for Relief Provides Extra Aid for Yemeni Refugees in Djibouti
content: A Saudi aircraft dispatched by King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Aid arrived at today, carrying 10 tons of food and medical supplies for the mobile clinics for Yemeni refugees at the Obouk Camp.

title: Saudi Fund for Development to Guarantee $170 Million in Export Credit for Sudan
content: The Saudi Fund for Development (SDF) will guarantee $170.5 million in export credit to Sudan. The Vice President and Managing Director of the SDF Yousef Al-Bassam said that since the Saudi Exports Program was launched in 2002, the Fund guaranteed over $12.54 billion in export credit to developing countries.

title: Fourteenth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Aden
content: The fourteenth Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport carrying 8 tons of children???Ć?Ös???Ć?supplies, including toys.

title: Eleventh Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The eleventh Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport carrying 11 tons of medical supplies. The relief aid comes from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: Tenth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The tenth Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport carrying 10 tons of medical equipment and supplies. The relief aid comes from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: King Salman Center Signs Sgreement with UNDP
content: The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center signed a cooperation and partnership arrangement with the UN Development Program. Under the agreement, the King Salman Center will help promote small farmers, herders and food producers in Yemen.

title: Kingdom Delivers 100 Tons of Dates to Niger
content: Saudi Ambassador to Niger Turki bin Nagi Al-Ali handed over 100 tons of dates to Niger???Ć?Ös Food Crisis.

title: Ninth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The ninth Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport, carrying 10 tons of medical equipment and supplies. The relief aid comes from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: Eighth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The eighth Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport, carrying 8 tons of infant formula. The relief aid comes from King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: Seventh Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The seventh Saudi relief plane arrived today at Aden International Airport, carrying 10 tons of medical supplies. The relief aid comes from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: Sixth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Aden
content: The sixth Saudi relief plane arrived in Aden carrying 11 tons of foodstuffs, accompanied by a team from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center.

title: Saudi Aid Arrives in Albania
content: Saudi plane loaded with 180 tons of aid arrived today in Albania for victims of the recent floods. The aid consists of food, tents, blankets and floor mats.

title: King Salman Center Launches Food Distribution Campaign in Yemen
content: King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center today began a food aid packages distribution campaign to needy and displaced people in the Yemeni governorate of Hadramout. The Center aims to distribute 100,000 food baskets in cooperation with the International Organization for Human Development of Muslim World League.

title: King Salman Center Supervisor Receives WHO Official
content: Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabeeah, advisor at the Royal Court and General Supervisor King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center, met today with the Director of the Eastern Mediterranean Region of the World Health Organization (WHO) Dr. Alaa Al-Alwan. During their meeting, the two sides reviewed issues on the aid delivery's mechanism to Yemen and discussed the joint cooperation between the two sides in humanitarian and relief works. Dr. Al-Rabeeah and Dr. Al-Alwan also discussed the programs that would be implemented by the WHO in Yemen through a strategic partnership with King Salman Center.

title: Fourth Saudi Relief Plane Arrives in Yemen
content: The fourth Saudi relief plane arrived in Aden International Airport carrying 10 tons of medical supplies for patients with kidney failure. The medical supplies were accompanied by a team from the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center and Doctors Without Borders.

title: Saudi Health Centers Provide Psychological Services to some 1,400 Patients a Week in Zaatari Camp
content: The Vice Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques Prince Mohammad bin Naif instructed that cases that cannot be treated at the Specialized Clinics, should be considered for treatment outside the camp and paid for by the campaign. The Psychological Support Unit of the Saudi Specialized Clinics treats 1,400 patients suffering from depression, after shock, phobia, and all other types of psychological problems. In addition, more than 2700 refugees benefitted from the Saudi Vaccine Program during the first half of 2015. The Saudi National Campaign to Support Syrian Brothers funded medical centers in Zaatari refugee camp in Jordan provided psychological services to about 1,400 Syrian patients per week.

title: Saudi Relief Ship Arrives in Aden Port
content: The Saudi relief ship ???Ć?śPath of the Goodness???Ć?arrived in the Yemeni port of Aden carrying 3,540 tons (7.8 million lbs.) of food and medical supplies. The aid ship arrived under the supervision of The King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center and in coordination with the evacuation and humanitarian operations section at the Saudi Arabia Ministry of Defense.

title: Saudi Arabia Donates 134 Tons of Dates for Refugees in Eastern Cameroon
content: Saudi Arabia has provided 134 metric tones of dates to refugees in the east of the of Cameroon. The donation was handed over by Saudi Ambassador to Cameroon Mohammed Al-Msohr last Tuesday to the United Nations World Food Programme (WFP).

title: UN Special Envoy for Yemen Praises Kingdom???Ć?Ös World-Wide Humanitarian Work
content: The Special Envoy praised the leading role of Saudi Arabia in supporting relief and humanitarian works as well as UN activities worldwide, hailing the efforts exerted in this regard by King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center. General Supervisor the King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center Dr. Abdullah Al-Rabeeahin met today with Special Envoy of UN Secretary General for Yemen Ismail Ould Cheikh Ahmed in Riyadh.

title: Foreign Minister Receives UN Special Envoy for Yemen
content: Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir received today U.N. Secretary-General???Ć?Ös Special Envoy for Yemen Ismail Ould Cheikh Ahmed. During the meeting, they discussed the latest developments in Yemen.

title: King Salman Center for Relief Distributes Ramadan Food Baskets to 7,500 Families in Yemen
content: King Salman Humanitarian Aid and Relief Center has distributed Ramadan food baskets to as many as 7,500 Yemeni families. The Center's spokesman, Rafat Al-Sabbagh, said that Ramadan food baskets were distributed yesterday in Yemen through the World Food Program in the framework of the agreement between the two sides.

title: Crown Prince Approves Fasting Program to Syrians and Somalis
content: Upon directives of Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister, Minister of Interior and Director General of Saudi Relief Campaigns, approved the program of fasting for the Syrian and Somali peoples during the Holy Month of Ramadan at a cost of over $3.31 million.

title: Saudi National Campaign Signs Contract to Supply Medicines in Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan
content: Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria has signed a $32,000 contract to supply the 12th batch of medical supplies to Saudi Specialized Clinics in Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan. The campaign's regional director, Dr. Badr bin Abdulrahman Al-Samhan, clinics provide health care to more than 83,000 Syrian refugees in Al-Zaatari Camp in Jordan.

title: Islamic Relief Organization Opens the Largest Station to Provide Water in West Sudan
content: The Jeddah-based International Islamic Relief has opened a new station to provide drinking and irrigation water in Ghabsha Badein area in western Sudan. The station will provide enough water for 20,000 people.

title: Kingdom Resume Aid Flight to Nepal, Second Relief Plane Arives in Kathmandu
content: Saudi Ambassador to Nepal Abdulnasser Al-Harthy said that the Saudi relief air lift was suspended for a brief time because of high volume of traffic and the limits imposed on large aircraft that carry more of 120 tons. Upon directive of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, a second plane carrying 28 tons of humanitarian supplies for the people of Nepal arrived in Kathmandu yesterday. The aid consists of tent, blankets, food and medical supplies.

title: Saudi Relief Aid Arrives in Comoros
content: In implementation of the directives of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, the Saudi relief plane carrying 100 tons of supplies, including 50 tons of foodstuffs and 50 tons of other supplies, including tents and blankets, arrived in the Comoros.

title: Saudi Committee for Relief of Afghan People Complete Winter Relief Project
content: Dr. Saed Al-Orabi Al-Harthi, an adviser to the Minister of Interior and chairman of the committee, said that the committee???Ć?Ös humanitarian and relief services will continue in a number of areas, including education and health services. Dr. Al-Harthi explained that SR 137 million ($36.5 million) has been approved for educational and health projects by the general supervisor of the committee, Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior. The Committee distributed 55,000 food baskets and 50,000 blankets to 55,000 needy families affected by the natural disasters across Afghanistan.?é?? Saudi Committee for the Relief of the Afghan People ended the third phase of the winter relief project. The $3.2 million project includes the distribution of food baskets and blankets. The relief supplies were distributed in cooperation with Afghan authorities, including the Afghan Red Crescent Society, and the Saudi Embassy in Kabul.

title: Aid ships Arrive in Yemen
content: Official spokesman for the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works Raafat Al-Sabagh said that several ships laden with fuel and humanitarian relief supplies, including medical supplies, have been allowed to enter Yemeni ports. Al-Sabagh said a UN ship loaded with diesel, a Liberian ship carrying 23 million metric tons of gasoline and liquid cargo, and an Indian and Djibouti cargo ships docked at Yemeni ports.

title: King Salman to Attend Opening of Humanitarian Relief Center
content: Dr. Abdullah bin Abdulaziz Al-Rabiah, an advisor at the Royal Court and the general supervisor of the center, said that the center reflects the role the Kingdom plays in humanitarian relief efforts. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz on Wednesday will attend the laying of the cornerstone of the headquarters of the King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works. This was announced by advisor at the Royal Court and General Supervisor of the center

title: Saudi National Campaign Sends 41 Tons of Relief Materials to Southern Syria
content: Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria has dispatched its 28th convoy for displaced civilians in southern Syria. The Campaign's Regional Director Dr. Badr Al-Samhan said that the aid convoy consists of 10 trucks containing 41.6 tons of relief supplies valued at $512,000. The aid convoy is expected to enter Syria across the Jordanian border under the umbrella of U.N. Security Council Resolution 2191.

title: Saudi Arabia, UNHCR Sign MoU to Assist Syrians
content: The funded projects by Saudi Arabia aim to rebuild and rehabilitate a total of 4,650 homes in Damascus, Homs, Hama, Daraa and Idlib, in addition to providing public facilities and services, which will benefit a total of 23,250 displaced Syrians. Saudi Arabia and the High Commissioner of the United Nations for Refugees (UNHCR) signed a cooperation agreement under which the Kingdom will contribute $5 million to help the displaced Syrians. This came on the sidelines of the 3rd International Pledging Humanitarian Conference for Syria held in Kuwait today in the presence of the Minister of Finance Ibrahim bin Abdulaziz Al-Assaf. The agreement was signed by Saudi Fund for Development representing the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia.

title: Saudi Distributes Bread to Displaced Syrians
content: Saudi National Campaign to Support Brothers in Syria continued providing bread for the Syrian displaced people in Aleppo and Idlib through mobile bakeries. Since the program began about two years ago to now, over 60,000 loaves of bread have been distributed daily to nearly 4,000 Syrian families for a total of some 33 million loaves at a cost about $544,000.

title: Foreign Minister: There Is No Blockade of Qatar
content: ???Ć?£We have exercised our sovereign right to prohibit them from using our airspace. We have allowed the movement of families between the two countries to happen so that we don???Ć?Öt divide families, and we are prepared to provide Qatar with food and medical supplies, should they need this, through the King Salman Center.???Ć????Ć?£There is no blockade of Qatar. Qatar is free to go ???Ć?the ports are open, the airports are open. What we have done is we have denied them use of our airspace, and this is our sovereign right. The limitation on the use of Saudi airspace is only limited to Qatari airways or Qatari-owned aircraft, not anybody else. The seaports of Qatar are open; there is no blockade on them. Qatar can move goods in and out whenever they want; they just cannot use our territorial waters,???Ć?the Foreign Minister said. Saudi Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met today with U.S. Secretary of State Rex Tillerson in Washington, D.C. Before the meeting, Minister Al-Jubeir commented on the situation in Qatar, emphasizing that there is no blockade in place.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Receives Phone Call From US Secretary of State
content: During the conversation, they reviewed bilateral relations, latest developments in the region, and the joint efforts of the two countries in combating terrorism, extremism and financing terrorist organizations to achieve security and stability in the region. The Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense received today a telephone call from US Secretary of State Rex Tillerson.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Receives Telephone Call from US Secretary of Defense
content: The two sides looked forward to more work for unifying international efforts in order to achieve security and stability in the region. The US Secretary of Defense lauded Saudi Arabia's active role in combating terrorism and countering extremism. Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, received today a telephone call from US Secretary of Defense James Mattis.?é?? During the telephone conversation, they discussed the current situations and latest developments in the region.

title: King Salman Receives Telephone Call from U.S. President Trump
content: President Trump lauded the leading role of the Kingdom in combating terrorism and its strenuous efforts to dry up its sources as well as the United States of America's gearing forward to enhance joint action between the two countries to fight extremism and exert efforts for achieving security and stability in the region. The Custodian of Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received a telephone call today from U.S. President Donald Trump.?é?? During the conversation, the historical ties between the two friendly countries as well as the developments of situations in the region and the world at large were discussed.

title: King Salman Chairs Weekly Cabinet's Session
content:
The Cabinet stressed the Kingdom's strong condemnation of the bombings in the Iraqi capital, Baghdad, the series of bombings in the Afghan capital, Kabul, the terrorist attacks that took place in London, expressing condolence to the governments and peoples of Iraq, Afghanistan and Britain on the victims of bombings and terrorist incidents, wishing the wounded a speedy recovery. 
?é?? In line with a noble directive, the Cabinet was briefed by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, on the outcomes of his visit to Russia, meeting with the Russian President Vladimir Putin, discussion of reinforcing Saudi-Russian understanding on the oil accord, led by the Kingdom, which guarantee for the first time, in the history of the region the cooperation of OPEC member states and non-member states, including Russia, in addition to review bilateral relations, in the field of investments and various oil and petrochemical industries and uniting efforts to combat terrorism and extremist thought. 
The Cabinet pointed out that the decision of various sisterly countries to severe diplomatic relations with the state of Qatar was an expression of keenness to preserve the unity of the Arab nation and take stance against Qatari acts, aimed at destabilizing security, in the region, contravening accords, charters, principles of international law and good neighborliness. 
?é?? The Cabinet confirmed that the decision to cut diplomatic and consulate relations with Qatar came emanating from the Kingdom's observing its sovereign rights guaranteed by international law and in protection of its national security against the dangers of terrorism and extremism as the Kingdom has taken this decisive decision due to the grave violations practiced by the authorities in Doha secretly and publicly throughout previous years. This aimed to strike a wedge in the Saudi domestic ranks, instigating people to disobey the State and breach its sovereignty through securing safe havens for numerous terrorist and sectarian groups targeting the stability of the region. The Cabinet reiterated that the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia will remain supportive for the Qatari sisterly people and their security and stability regardless of the hostile practice committed by the authorities in Doha. King Salman began the meeting by briefing the Cabinet on the outcome of his audience with King Abdullah Al-Hussein of Jordan, the contents of a verbal message he sent to Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmed Al-Jaber Al-Sabah, Emir of Kuwait; his meeting with Sheikh Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Crown Prince of Abu-Dhabi, the contents of the telephone call the monarch received from President Emmanuel Macron of France, as well as his meeting with the Secretary General of the GCC Dr. Abdullatif bin Rashed Al-Zayani. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the weekly Cabinet meeting at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah this evening.

title: Kingdom of Saudi Arabia Cuts Off Diplomatic and Consular Relations With the State of Qatar
content: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia affirms that it has long been patient, despite the fact that the authorities in Doha have continued to evade their commitments and conspire against the Kingdom, out of concern for the people of Qatar, who remain a natural and genuine extension of their brethren in the Kingdom and an integral part of its history and family. The Kingdom will continue to support the people of Qatar, its security and stability regardless of the hostile practices the authorities in Doha continue to carry out. In accordance with the decision to cut off diplomatic and consular relations, Saudi citizens are prohibited from traveling to, residing in or passing through Qatar. Saudi residents and visitors in Qatar are advised to leave its territories within no more than 14 days. Unfortunately, for security reasons, this prevents Qatari citizens' entry to or transit through the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and Qatari residents and visitors must leave Saudi territories within 14 days. All the while, the Kingdom confirms its commitment and care to provide all the services and facilities for Qatari Haj and Umrah pilgrims. Since 1995, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and its sister nations have made strenuous and continued efforts to urge the authorities in Doha to abide by its commitments and agreements, yet, they have repeatedly violated their international obligations and the agreements they signed under the umbrella of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) for Arab States to cease the hostilities against the Kingdom and stand against terrorist groups and activities of which the latest was their failure to implement the Riyadh Agreement. This decisive decision was taken by the government of Saudi Arabia as a result of grave violations that the authorities in Doha continued to commit over the past years, covertly and publicly, with the aim of dividing internal Saudi ranks, inciting against the State, infringing on its sovereignty, embracing various terrorist and sectarian groups aimed at destabilizing the region including the Muslim Brotherhood Group, Daesh (ISIS) and Al-Qaeda, constantly promoting the literature and plans of these groups through its media, supporting the activities of the Iranian-backed terrorist groups in the governorate of Qatif in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and our sister nation the Kingdom of Bahrain, its continued financing, adopting and sheltering of extremists who seek to undermine the stability and unity at home and abroad, and using the media to fuel the internal strife. Additionally the support of the authorities in Doha for the Houthi militia led coup was made clear to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, even after the announcement of the Coalition to Support the Legitimacy in Yemen. This decision by the Kingdom was also taken in solidarity with the sister Kingdom of?é??Bahrain, which is subjected to terrorist campings and operations supported by the authorities in Doha. An official source stated that the Government of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, emanating from exercising its sovereign rights guaranteed by international law, to protect its national security from the dangers of terrorism and extremism, has decided to cut off diplomatic and consular relations with the State of Qatar. It also decided to close all land, sea and air ports, to prevent crossing into Saudi territories, airspace and territorial waters, and begin immediate legal procedures to create an understanding with our sister nations, allies and partners, and international companies to implement the same procedure as soon as possible for all means of transport to and from the State of Qatar, for reasons relating to Saudi national security.

title: Saudi Arabia Condemns Series of Attacks in Kabul
content: An official source at the Saudi Ministry of Foreign Affairs expressed the Kingdom's strong condemnation of the series of attacks in Afghanistan's capital, Kabul, killing and wounding a number of people.?é?? The source offered the Kingdom's condolences to the families of the victims and to the Afghan Government and people, wishing a speedy recovery to the wounded.

title: King Salman Condoles UK Prime Minister o London Terror Attack
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz sent a cable of condolences to British Prime Minister Theresa May following the terrorist attack that took place in London.?é?? In his cable, King Salman strongly condemn the heinous criminal act and offered his deepest condolences.

title: King Salman Consoles British Prime Minister on Manchester Bombing
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has sent a cable of condolences to British Prime Minister Theresa May following the terrorist bombing in Manchester. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques strongly condemned this criminal act, wished speedy recovery to the injured and stressed the importance of international efforts to confront and eradicate terrorism.

title: King Salman Calls British Prime Minister To Express Support Following Manchester Terror Attack
content: During the conversation, King Salman confirmed the stance of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia with all its might with Britain in the war against terrorism. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz expressed the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's strong denunciation of the Manchester terrorist attack, noting that it contradicts the principles and ethics of Islamic religion. In a telephone call to the British Prime Minister Theresa May today, King Salman extended sincere condolence and sympathy to the families of the victims, wishing the injured a quick recovery.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet also lauded the exchange of the memorandum of understanding between the GCC countries and the US to establish a center to dry-up the sources of funding of terrorism. The Cabinet asserted that the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques' announcing and launching of the Global Center for Combating Extremist Ideology "Etidal", in the presence of U.S. President Donald Trump and world leaders. The King also drew the attention to the importance of achieving peace between Palestinians and Israelis, intensifying efforts to resolve the Syrian crisis, refusing to harm the Islamic countries' relations with friendly countries or exploiting Islam as a cover for political purposes fueling hatred, extremism and terrorism as well as religious and sectarian conflicts, citing acts done by the Iranian regime, its affiliated groups and organizations and other terrorist organizations. The Cabinet highlighted the valuable contents of the speech delivered by the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques pointing to the King's emphasis on the responsibility of the Arab and Islamic countries to be united in the fight against the forces of evil and extremism and terrorism in all its forms. The Cabinet affirmed that the Kingdom's hosting of the U.S.-GCC Summit and the historically unprecedented Arab-Islamic-American Summit reflects the great posture the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia has internationally enjoyed, as well as its remarkable efforts to enhance security, peace and stability in the region and the world. The King also reviewed historical relations between the two countries and ways of enhancing them in various fields and discussed the latest world developments, They also exchanged of a number of trade agreements and investment opportunities exceeding $280 billion. King Salman said that the historic agreement signed by the GCC with the United States take firm measures against the financing of terrorism; and the announcement of the launch of the Global Center for Combating Extremist Ideology aimed at spreading the principles of moderation. King Salman began by expressing his appreciation to US President Donald J. Trump and leaders of Arab and Islamic countries who participated in the 17th Consultative Meeting of Leaders of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), the U.S.-GCC Summit, and the Arab-Islamic-American Summit hosted by the Kingdom yesterday. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh on this afternoon.

title: Joint Statement Between the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the United States of America
content: Joint Statement Between the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and the United States of America

title: Saudi Foreign Minister and US Secretary of State Confirm Strategic Partnership
content: ???Ć?£Now we have a radical militia [Houthis], allied with Iran and Hezbollah in possession of ballistic missiles and an air force, that has taken over a friendly government.?é?? [The] friendly government asked for support, we intervened,???Ć?explained Minister Al-Jubeir. ???Ć?£The Houthis are less than 50,000 in a country of 28 million. It is unacceptable that they would be allowed to seize power and get away with it.???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir expressed an outlook for a peaceful resolution to the conflict in Yemen and called on the Houthi militias to end all separatist activities. ???Ć?£If Iran wants to be a normal country and wants others to treat it like a normal country, it has to act in accord with international law and the values and the mores of the international system that have existed for centuries,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. Minister Al-Jubeir criticized Iran for conducting destabilizing actions throughout the region, including in Yemen, Syria and Lebanon. The foreign minister said the world should judge Iran by its actions, not words. The two leaders outlined a renewed commitment to defense cooperation. Earlier today, the two countries signed a defense agreement worth more than $100 billion that will upgrade Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös military capabilities, allowing it to better protect its borders and aid in the global fight against terrorism. Secretary Tillerson praised the deal???Ć?Ös economic impact, stating that it would generate ???Ć?£hundreds of thousands???Ć?of American jobs. ???Ć?£If we can change the conversation in the Islamic world from enmity towards the U.S. to partnership with the U.S. and if we can change the conversation in the U.S. and in the West from enmity towards the Islamic world to one of partnership, we would have truly changed our world and we would have truly drowned the voices of extremism and we will have drained the swamp from which extremism and terrorism emanates,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. Minister Al-Jubeir also said that the visit is a turning point for the Islamic world???Ć?Ös relationship with the U.S. and will advance international cooperation in the fight against terrorism. He said he strategic vision will address ways to cooperate on ???Ć?£violent extremism, financing of terrorism, increasing defense capabilities, working on a defense architecture for the region ???Ć?trade and investment, education, and working in all fields in order to enhance our common interests and deal with the challenges that face both of our countries.???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir described the day as a truly historic one for U.S.-Saudi relations, further deepening the ties between the two longstanding allies. Minister Al-Jubeir discussed the Joint Strategic Vision Declaration signed by Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and President Donald Trump. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?Saudi Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir and U.S. Secretary of State Rex Tillerson said today that the Saudi.-U.S. partnership is stronger than ever and built on shared security interests, close economic cooperation and mutual trust. They made the remarks during a press conference in Riyadh to brief reporters on the first day of President Trump???Ć?Ös visit to Saudi Arabia.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet's Session
content: The Cabinet condemned the terror attack on a development project in Al-Masurha District in Qatif Province. King Salman expressed his trust May 20 Saudi-US Summit will contribute to strengthening and enhancing strategic relations and global security between the two countries.?é?? He also said he is looking forward to the Gulf-US Summit, and the Arab-Islamic-US Summit which he said will enhance greater cooperation with the US.consultative meeting to be held on May 21 at will contribute to consolidating the Gulf solidarity for enhancing security, stability and peace in the region. The King also stressed that the Arab-Islamic-US Summit to be held on the same day will establish a new partnership in confronting extremism and terrorism, disseminating the values of tolerance and coexistence, and enhancing security, stability and cooperation. The King welcomed the upcoming participation of US President Donald J. Trump, and leaders of Arab and Islamic countries in the 17th consultative meeting of leaders of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC), the Gulf-US Summit, and the Arab-Islamic-US Summit, being hosted in Riyadh this weekend. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's weekly session at Al-Sallam Palace in Jeddah on this afternoon.

title: Foreign Minister Receives Chairman of the Syrian Opposition's High Committee for Negotiations
content: Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir received in Riyadh today Dr. Riad Hijab, chairman of the Syrian opposition High Committee for Negotiations.?é?? During the meeting, they discussed the latest developments on the Syrian arena and international efforts to solve the Syrian crisis, in addition to review issues of mutual interest.

title: King Salman Congratulates Emmanuel Macron on His Election as President of France
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz sent a cable of congratulations to Emmanuel Macron on his election as President of France.

title: Crown Prince Receives US Homeland Security Officials
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, received in Jeddah this evening US Secretary of Homeland Security John Kelly and Assistant to the President for Homeland Security and Counterterrorism Thomas Bossert. During the meeting, they discussed a number of issues of common interest between the two friendly countries including the existing security cooperation.

title: Foreign Minister: US President???Ć?Ös Visit Reflects Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös Pivotal Role in the Arab and Islamic Nations
content: "This visit will enhance, we believe, cooperation between the US and Arab and Islamic countries in combating terrorism and extremism. It will has enormous interests in the region and world," Al-Jubeir concluded. Al-Jubeir said Trump's visit to Saudi Arabia would include a bilateral summit, a meeting with the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) leaders and another with leaders of Arab and Islamic countries. The Kingdom has a key role in pushing the peace process forward. The Kingdom has huge investments in the US economy.?é?? The Kingdom is a big trading partner with the United States, which means existence of enormous economic and financial interests between the two countries. To achieve the goals set by President Trump, whether in restoring the US role, defeating ISIS, containing Iran, promoting peace or enhancing investment, trade and prosperity, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is the key to these issues. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is among the US most important allies in the Arab and Islamic world. The Kingdom is one of the most important international partners with regard to economy, trade and investment. "The US President's visit to the Kingdom is historic by all standards. The Saudi Arabia is the birthplace of Islam and serves the Two Holy Mosques. Terrorism and extremism cannot be defeated without the Kingdom. We are the closest partners in the fight against terrorism and extremism. The Kingdom is at the forefront of countries against Iran's negative and illegal activities.?é?? The Kingdom is at the forefront of countries fighting the terrorist ISIS organization. The Kingdom has provided the Arab Peace Initiative. "President Trump desires to restore the US role in the world which is welcomed by the US allies. He also expressed his desire to destroy the ISIS terrorist organization and the terrorist groups. President Trump also seeks to address the Iranian illegal practices in the region. All these matters are welcomed by the US allies in the region. So, we are allies with the US administration in these issues," Al-Jubeir added. The Foreign Minister stressed that Saudi Arabia is major player in combating terrorism and extremism.?é?? He said that the Kingdom has provided the Arab Peace Initiative which is a key reference to a Palestinian-Israeli peaceful solution where President Trump is seeking to end this conflict.?é?? He described the US President's visit to the Kingdom as historic by all standards. ???Ć?£The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is the first partner for the United States of America in the Arab and Islamic nations.?é?? The Kingdom is the first partner for the United States in combating terrorism and extremism as well as eradicating Daesh (ISIS) and Al-Qaeda. The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is at the forefront of countries addressing the Iranian negative interferences in the regional affairs. This is an important issue for the United States. Therefore, the partnership with the Kingdom is very important," Minister Al-Jubeir said. Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed?é??Al-Jubeir?é??stressed today that the visit by US President Donald Trump to the Kingdom as his first foreign visit, reflects the Kingdom's pivotal role in the Arab and Islamic world.

title: Ambassador Al-Jaber Confirms Saudi Arabia and Coalition Members Have No Ambitions in Yemen
content:
He pointed out that the coalition succeeded in eliminating terrorism, which Iran tried to expand and provoked sectarian and regional strife through its arms in Yemen, Houthi and Saleh. He stressed that the Kingdom and the coalition, especially King Salman Center for Relief and Humanitarian Works (KSRelief), provided assistance to Yemen in excess of $600 million, but the militias Houthi militias and Saleh forces in return steal the aid and sell it on the black market and prevent the needy from receiving the relief supplies.?é?? He stressed that the coalition has given the protection of civilians a high priority and have drawn up a list of thousands of areas of civilian nature that are strictly prohibited.?é?? The coalition is fully committed to international humanitarian law and investigates any accusation against the alliance. He pointed out that Saudi Arabia and the Arab coalition have sought to find a political solution and stressed their willingness to provide support for reconstruction. Ambassador Al-Jaber said that the Kingdom supported the participation of all Yemeni parties in the political process in Yemen in accordance with the three known references, the dismantling of the armed militias and the return of Yemen as an independent Arab state. He said that the war in Yemen was not an option but a necessity in response to the request of the legitimate Government of Yemen and to stop the Iranian intervention in the country and end Iran???Ć?Ös threaten the national security of the Gulf and the region and the international maritime corridors and to maintain the security and stability of the region. The Council reiterated the keenness of the GCC States on the security, stability and unity of the Libyan territories. On Libya: 21. The Council also regrets that no progress has been made in any. The Council calls upon the Iraqi Government and the United Nations Assistance Mission for Iraq (UNAMI) to make every effort to reach a solution to this humanitarian and other related issues. 20. The Ministerial Council expressed its rejection of the statements made by Iraqi Vice President Nuri al-Maliki and the official spokesman of the Iraqi Foreign Ministry on the fair judicial sentences that were committed against those convicted of terrorist cases in the Kingdom of Bahrain, stressing the rejection by the GCC States of any form of interference in their internal affairs. 19. The Ministerial Council reaffirmed the Permanent Council's positions and resolutions on Iraq and expressed its support for the Government of Iraq in taking measures to combat terrorism aimed at achieving security and stability in Iraq. 
On Iraq : The Ministerial Council condemned Houthi and Saleh's forces for targeting of the population centers in Saudi Arabia and Yemen by ballistic missiles. The Ministerial Council lauded the meeting of the Quartet with the participation of the Sultanate of Oman on Yemen held in Bonn on 16 February, 2017, calling upon the Houthi and Saleh group to commit to peace and resume consultations under the auspices of the United Nations in accordance with the Gulf Initiative and its executive mechanism, outputs of the comprehensive national dialogue conference, the Security Council Resolution 2216, and the framework presented by the Special Envoy of the UN Secretary General which includes security and political measures to end the crisis. 18. The Ministerial Council confirmed the GCC firm stances and decisions on the crisis in Yemen and welcomed the issuance of UN Security Council Resolution 2342 under Chapter VII of the Charter of the United Nations which stressed the national, regional and international key points to reach a political solution to the crisis. 17. The Ministerial Council welcomed the final communiqu?ā?? issued by the 9th Coordination Meeting of the Group of Senior Donors to Support the Humanitarian Situation in Syria held in Kuwait on 16 January, 2017. 16. The Ministerial Council welcomed the US President Donald Trump's commitment to sign an executive decision to develop a plan for the establishment of safe regions in Syria, hoping that this will contribute to maintaining the safety of civilians in accordance with the relevant international resolutions. 15. The Ministerial Council stressed the GCC positions and resolutions on the Syrian crisis and followed up the results of the meetings of the Astana Conference between the Syrian opposition delegations and the regime in January and February 2017 and the Geneva Conference of 23 February, 2017.?é?? The Council stressed the need to stabilize the cease-fire and establish an effective and practical monitoring mechanism to achieve a political solution in accordance with the Geneva Declaration 1 (June 2012). 14. The Ministerial Council confirmed the GCC stances and decisions in this regard that Iran abides by the agreement reached with the the P5+1 (the permanent members of the United Nations Security Council plus Germany) in July 2015, on its nuclear program and to implement the Security Council Resolution 2231 (July 2015) on the nuclear agreement. 13. The Ministerial Council expressed its condemnation of the Iranian regime's irresponsible provocative statements and acts of aggression against the Kingdom of Bahrain, calling upon the Iranian regime to stop the policies that feed sectarian conflicts. 12. The Ministerial Council stressed the GCC stances and resolutions regarding the GCC-Iranian relations and confirmed the necessity of fully abiding by the basic principles based on the Charter of the United Nations, the international law, the principles of good neighborliness, respecting the sovereignty of States, non-interference in internal affairs, peaceful settlement of disputes, and rejection of sectarianism.?é?? The Council expressed its full rejection of the continuation of Iranian interference in the internal affairs of the GCC States and the region. The Council calls upon Iran to respond to the UAE's efforts to resolve the issue through direct negotiations or go to the International Court of Justice. 11. The Ministerial Council confirmed the GCC permanent positions and resolutions which were confirmed by all previous statements rejecting Iranian continued occupation of the three islands of Greater Tunb, Lesser Tunb and Abu Musa of the United Arab Emirates.?é?? The Council stressed its support of the UAE sovereign right over its three islands: Greater Tunb, Lesser Tunb and Abu Musa and the territorial water, airspace, continental shelf and exclusive economic zone of the three islands as an integral part of the territory of the United Arab Emirates.?é?? The Council considered that any decisions, practices or acts carried out by Iran on the three islands are null and void as well as do not change any historical and legal facts. Iranian occupation of the three islands belonging to the United Arab Emirates: 10. The Ministerial Council condemned the actions taken by the Israeli occupation authorities in Al-Quds (Jerusalem) to change its identity.?é?? The Council welcomed the Security Council Resolution 2334 which denounced the Israeli settlement.?é?? The Council strongly condemned the decision of the Israeli Parliament to allow settlement gatherings on the Palestinian private lands, which is a flagrant violation of the rights of the Palestinian people and the international law, calling for pressure on the Israeli government to stop its illegal practices in the Palestinian occupied territories. 8. The situation in the Palestinian territories and developments of the Arab-Israeli conflict: 7.
