Governor of the SWCC Fehied Al-Shareef said the corporation would be transformed into a holding company and part of its shares floated for public subscription. Minister of Water and Electricity Abdullah Al-Hussayen said the measure aims to meet the growing demand for water in Saudi Arabia as a result of increasing population and growing industrial and business activities all over the country. In July 2008, Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz approved an executive program for privatizing the Saline Water Conversion Corporation (SWCC), which operates more than 30 desalination plants on the Red Sea and Arabian Gulf coasts. Hattan Hamouda, director of investment planning at Jeddah Municipality, said that the project is expected to help solve Jeddah???Ć?Ös water shortage. He added that the plant would be built on land allocated by the municipality for investment. A new desalination plant with a daily capacity of 50,000 cubic meters will be constructed south of Jeddah by the private sector, the Arab News reported today.

title: King Abdullah Launches Endowment Project in Makkah
content:
Five consulting and engineering companies in the Kingdom competed to design the project. 
According to Minister of Islamic Affairs Saleh Al-Asheikh, the 11 residential towers will cover an area of 30,000 square meters with 7,000 rooms in addition to 1,900 lavatories and parking facilities for 560 cars and 40 buses. Located near the Grand Mosque, the project will be able to accommodate 55,000 pilgrims and visitors. It aims to ease overcrowding during peak hours and facilitate the movement of pilgrims. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz launched the second King Abdulaziz Endowment Project in Makkah September 23.

title: Makkah to be Transformed into a World-Class Smart City
content: The conference will address e-government, safety control systems and security applications, intelligent city management applications, and how to develop a large community of users with ICT skills. Also on the agenda are workshops on e-government services suite, GIS based planning and management applications, mobile GIS emergency response applications, security, law enforcement and safety control applications. ???Ć?£The pilgrims need accommodations, meals, information and communication channels, and various modes of transportation. A combination of the latest technologies that integrate fixed and wireless broadband networks, fixed and mobile GIS and GPS applications, and user-friendly information and communications points can greatly enhance the visitors experience, and the city's objective of quality services to the visitors of the holy sites,???Ć?he explained. The transformation of Makkah into a world-class intelligent city will help meet the needs of pilgrims from around the world, Mohammed Maqazi, head of the conference???Ć?Ös scientific committee, told the Arab News. ???Ć?£This conference is an initiative to apply smart city technologies in the Kingdom and transform it into a knowledge-based society and a digital economy,???Ć?he said. In addition, intelligent city facilities will speed up communication with the outside world and boost cultural, economic and commercial development, Al-Bar noted, adding that the conference would discuss successful experiments in the field at national and international levels. Al-Bar said the conference would contribute to improving the municipal services for both citizens and pilgrims.?é?? ???Ć?£This is the largest conference on intelligent cities to be held in the Kingdom,???Ć?Makkah Mayor Osama Al-Bar told the Arab News. ???Ć?£It aims at expediting the use of smart city technologies in Makkah for the benefit of the millions of pilgrims who come to the city every year for Hajj and Umrah.???Ć?Governor of Makkah Province Prince Khalid Al-Faisal will open the two-day event at Umm Al-Qura University???Ć?Ös King Abdulaziz Historical Hall on January 19. It is being organized by the Makkah Municipality in association with the Communications and Information Technology Commission and the Hajj Research Center. The conference is expected to bring together a large number of experts from around the world to discuss the best ways to apply the intelligent city concept to Makkah. An international conference on intelligent cities will be held in Makkah in January next 2009, the Arab News reported today. The objective is to transform Makkah into a smart city with advanced technological facilities.

title: King Abdullah Names Members of the Allegiance Commission
content: The 35 members of the Allegiance Commission are: Prince Mishaal bin Abdulaziz; Prince Abdulrahman bin Abdulaziz; Prince Miteb bin Abdulaziz; Prince Talal bin Abdulaziz; Prince Badr bin Abdulaziz; Prince Turki bin Abdulaziz; Prince Nayef bin Abdulaziz; Prince Fawaz bin Abdulaziz; Prince Salman bin Abdulaziz; Prince Mamdouh bin Abdulaziz; Prince Abdul-Illah bin Abdulaziz; Prince Sattam bin Abdulaziz; Prince Ahmad bin Abdulaziz; Prince Mash???Ć?Öhoor bin Abdulaziz; Prince Hazloul bin Adulaziz; Prince Muqrin bin Abdulaziz; Prince Mohammed bin Saud; Prince Khalid bin Al-Faisal; Prince Mohammed bin Saad; Prince Turki bin Faisal bin Turki (I); Prince Mohammed bin Nasser; Prince Faisal bin Bandar; Prince Saud bin Abdulmohsin; Prince Mohammed bin Fahd; Prince Khalid bin Sultan; Prince Talal bin Mansour; Prince Khalid bin Abdullah; Prince Mohammed bin Mishari; Prince Faisal bin Khalid; Prince Badr bin Mohammed; Prince Faisal bin Thamir; Prince Mishaal bin Majed; Prince Abdullah bin Musaed; Prince Faisal bin Abdulmajeed; and Prince Abdulaziz bin Nawaf. ???Ć?£King Abdulaziz had urged us to become God fearing people and to enhance the pillars of justice, and to close our ranks, and to settle any difference through discussion and dialogue in a transparent manner, and not to allow anyone to interfere in our special affairs,???Ć?King Abdullah said. ???Ć?£I am confident that you are aware of this and will follow it.???Ć?In remarks on the occasion, King Abdullah called on them to heed the advice of the late founder of Saudi Arabia. King Abdullah met with the Commission members last night in Riyadh, where they swore an oath of allegiance to him. The newly named members of the Commission are all sons and grandsons of the Kingdom???Ć?Ös founder, King Abdulaziz Al-Saud. The Allegiance Commission was established as part of a new law of succession announced in October 2006 to formalize the royal succession and ensure a smooth transfer of power. Under the new system, the King will nominate a Crown Prince, and the Allegiance Commission will vote on the candidate. Previously, the King had the sole right to select the Crown Prince. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz yesterday issued a royal decree naming 35 members of the Saudi royal family to the Allegiance Commission.

title: Crown Prince Discusses Counterterrorism, Regional Hot Spots with Kuwaiti Daily
content: Commenting on development in the Kingdom, Crown Prince Sultan highlighted King Abdullah???Ć?Ös focus on education, training and job creation for young Saudis so that they are able to meet the needs of the job market. He noted that the development process remains ongoing. On the Kingdom???Ć?Ös recent judicial reforms, Crown Prince Sultan said that the new laws embody the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdulaziz???Ć?Ös commitment to development in Saudi Arabia. The Saudi judicial system has long been based on the Shari???Ć?Öah, and the new laws reflect this adherence to Islamic principles. Regarding the situation in Palestine, Crown Prince Sultan said that the Kingdom is concerned by the divisions within the Palestinian ranks which negatively affect their cause. Asked to comment on accusations that Saudis are traveling to Iraq to fight, Crown Prince Sultan replied that Saudi Arabia does not permit Saudis to go to Iraq, and that if some citizens are crossing the border they are doing so at locations other than the Saudi-Iraqi border. He remarked that work is scheduled to begin soon on a fence that will run along the border with Iraq to prevent infiltration. Turning to the crisis in Iraq, the Crown Prince characterized the situation there as a significant humanitarian tragedy. Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös policy towards Iraq has always been rooted in the desire to preserve Iraqi lives and maintain the country???Ć?Ös territorial integrity, he said. The Kingdom has repeatedly urged Iraqis to achieve national unity, renounce foreign interference and guarantee equal rights for all regardless of political or ethnic affiliation. The Crown Prince added that Saudi Arabia would continue to exert diplomatic efforts with Arab nations and international organizations in order to help Iraq regain its rightful place in the international community. Asked if the interrogation of terrorism suspects in the Kingdom had found links to groups outside the country, Crown Prince Sultan replied that the Ministry of Interior would announce any findings once the process has been completed. ???Ć?£The Kingdom is determined to continue its policy of fighting all forms of terrorism,???Ć?Crown Prince Sultan said. ???Ć?£Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös view is that dealing with the phenomenon of terrorism should not be confined to the mere security aspect of it but it should also be at the intellectual level, not only in our region but also at the level of the whole world with all its various faiths.???Ć?The Crown Prince made the remarks in a wide-ranging interview published today in the Arabic-language Kuwaiti daily Al-Rai. Crown Prince Sultan bin Abdulaziz has stressed the Kingdom???Ć?Ös determination to combat terrorism in all its forms and that counterterrorism efforts should focus not just on security, but on combating the terrorist mindset as well.

title: King Abdullah Gives Wide-Ranging Interview to Al-Rai
content: In addition, the King highlighted the close, friendly relations between Saudi Arabia and Jordan. Turning to elections in Saudi Arabia, the King said that they are determined by political and social change as well as the interests of the country and society, and remarked that citizens retain the right to express their views at the various open weekly meetings each week as well as through the municipal, regional and national councils. Moreover, Saudi Arabia does believe in gradual reform in a way that serves the country???Ć?Ös interest, he said. He also denounced regional efforts to undermine the security and stability of Arab countries. These efforts will never reach fruition and will have a negative impact the countries that engage in them, he warned. King Abdullah condemned terrorism as a dangerous phenomenon that is not in line with the teachings of Islam or the human nature. On Iraq, the King said that Saudi Arabia is concerned about the situation in the country, adding that the Kingdom will continue supporting the Iraqi people in any way possible. He stressed the importance of reducing tension in Iraq and noted that the future of Iraq must be determined by the Iraqis. In addition, the Kingdom has not ruled out hosting a reconciliation meeting for the various Iraqi factions if such a meeting would be useful. Regarding Lebanon, he called on the various political factions there to resolve their differences through negotiation, and denounced any intervention that widens the gap between the Lebanese factions. In the interview, King Abdullah urged the Palestinian leadership to act wisely and live up to its responsibility towards the Palestinian people. The King noted that during his recent visits to Spain, France and Poland, he found support for the Arab Peace Initiative to resolve the Palestinian-Israeli conflict. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Abdullah bin Abdulaziz discussed a range of key regional issues in an interview published today in the Jordanian newspaper Al-Rai. The King begins a state visit to Jordan today.

During the meeting, they discussed a number of security issues of mutual interest, including enhancing joint cooperation to combat extremism and terrorism.

content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met this afternoon with US Secretary of State Rex Tillerson at the State Department.?é?? During their talks, they discussed bilateral relations following the the recently visit by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman to Washington.

title: King Salman, President Trump Speak by Phone
content: King Salman extended an invitation to President Trump to visit the Kingdom and the US President also invited the King to visit the US. The two leaders agreed to schedule the visits in the coming period so as to promote cooperation and joint action and activate the strategic partnership between the two countries further. The views of the two leaders were identical on the files that were discussed during the call, including the fight against terrorism, extremism, their finance, formulating the appropriate mechanisms for that, and confronting those who seek to undermine security and stability in the region and interfere in the internal affairs of other states. During the call, they discussed the latest regional and world developments, and the historic relations between their two friendly countries and the strategic partnership as well as the importance of improving the economic, security and military cooperation between them. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz discussed with US President Donald Trump several topics in a telephone call on Sunday.

title: Foreign Minister Underscored the Strength of Saudi-US Relations, Addressing Continued and Expanded Areas of Cooperation
content: ???Ć?ØOur hope is that wisdom will prevail and the Congress will do the right thing and make the adjustments,???Ć?the foreign minister added. ???Ć?£The country that has the most to lose from dilution of sovereign immunities is the United States itself. And American officials know this. Because America has the largest footprint in the world, they operate all over the world. They???Ć?Öre fighting wars all over the world. They provide weapons to countries all over the world. If that principle is eroded, then the US could be sued in virtually every country in the world.???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir said that the Justice Against Sponsors of Terrorism Act (JASTA) legislation passed by the U.S. Congress last year has undercut the foundation of sovereign immunity, turning ???Ć?£the international order into the law of the jungle. title: Kingdom Welcomes US Secretary of State Kerry's Proposals on Final Settlement of Palestinian-Israel Conflict
content: Saudi Arabia today welcomed the proposals put forth by U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry on a final settlement of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict. In a statement, the Saudi Foreign Ministry said the Kingdom views the proposals as being in accord with the majority of the resolutions of international legality and most of the elements of the Arab Peace Initiative adopted at the Arab Summit in Beirut in 2002 and by the Islamic Summit in Makkah in 2005, and that the proposals represent an appropriate basis for achieving a final settlement of the Palestinian-Israeli conflict.

title: Foreign Minister Receives US Officials
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmad Al-Jubeir received in Jeddah today U.S. Assistant Secretary of State for Near Eastern Affairs Anne Patterson and Special Envoy for Guantanamo Closure Lee Wolosky.

title: Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir Concludes Visit to Washington
content: During his visit, Minister Al-Jubeir met with Secretary of State John Kerry, White House National Security Advisor Susan Rice, CIA Director John Brennan and Assistant Secretary of State for Near Eastern Affairs Anne Patterson. In addition, Minister Al-Jubeir conducted meetings with several members of Congress. Minister Al-Jubeir discussed Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös military campaign to restore the legitimate government of Yemen, stating that the Saudi-led coalition is committed to international law and protecting civilians. ???Ć?£The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia was one of the founding members of the coalition against ISIS in Syria. Saudi Arabia was one of the first countries to fly air missions over Syria as part of this coalition, and we continue to be part of this coalition,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. Minister Al-Jubeir reiterated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös plan to deploy ground forces in Syria to fight Daesh (ISIS) if the U.S.-led coalition also deploys ground forces. ???Ć?£Our region, unfortunately, is experiencing many challenges, and the resolution of these challenges is critically important to the future of the region and the world,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. ???Ć?£We are determined to work with our American friends in trying to find solutions to these areas in a way that promotes peace and security for all people of the region as well as people of the world.???Ć?Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir today conducted several high-level meetings in Washington, D.C. this week to discuss regional issues and bilateral relations. During these meetings, Minister Al-Jubeir stressed that Saudi Arabia is a force for regional peace and stability in the region, including its efforts to end conflicts in Syria and Yemen.

title: Foreign Minister Holds Meeting with Secretary Kerry in Washington
content: During his remarks, Secretary Kerry thanked the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and Saudi Arabia for the Kingdom???Ć?Ös ???Ć?£solid efforts???Ć?in supporting the coalition against Daesh. ???Ć?£Our region, unfortunately, is experiencing many challenges, and the resolution of these challenges is critically important to the future of the region and the world,???Ć?Minister Al-Jubeir continued. ???Ć?£We are determined to ???Ć?work with our American friends in trying to find solutions to these areas in a way that promotes peace and security for all people of the region as well as people of the world.???Ć????Ć?£We have a tremendous stake in stability and peace and security in the region,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. ???Ć?£We have a tremendous stake in trying to resolve the problems in the region before they consume all of us. We are determined to work with our allies in the United States and our allies around the world to bring this about, whether it is in the war against terrorism, whether it is in trying to stabilize the Middle East, whether it???Ć?Ös in trying to begin a political transition in Syria, implementing 2254, whether it???Ć?Ös in bringing peace between Israelis and Palestinians, peace to Yemen.???Ć?Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met with U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry today to discuss regional issues, including the conflict in Syria, joint counterterrorism efforts and the Saudi-led military coalition in Yemen. In joint remarks to the media after the meeting, Minister Al-Jubeir reiterated that Saudi Arabia is a force for peace and security throughout the region.

title: King Receives Commander of US Central Command
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, King Salman bin Abdulaziz, received at Al-Yamamah Palace today the Commander of United States Central Command General Lloyd J. Austin. During the meeting, a number of issues of common interest were reviewed. The audience was also attended by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz.

title: Crown Prince Meets with Commander of US Central Command
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziza, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, met today with Commander of US Central Command General Lloyd Austin. The meeting was attended by a number of officials and discussed a number of issues of common interest in addition to the developments in the Middle East.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabient adopted a number of decision, including new rules regulating the acceptance of government officials of gifts awarded to them on official visits and events; and authorized the Chief Executive Officer of Saudi Food and Drug Authority, or his deputy, to discuss a memorandum of understanding for scientific and technical cooperation with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration. The Cabinet emphasized the Kingdom's continuing fight against terrorism, following the statement issued by the Interior Ministry including the classification of names and officials from Hezbollah on the background of their responsibilities for operations for the party in the Middle East. The Cabinet expressed Kingdom's condemnation of the terrorist attacks in Al-Arish in Egypt, Al-Khamis City, Libya, and in Tunisia. The Kingdom reaffirmed the nation???Ć?Ös emphasis on the need to redouble international efforts to root out terrorism. The Cabinet reviewed the latest developments in world events and confirmed the Kingdom's concern about the increasing hostility and racial rhetoric against refugees in general, and Muslims in particular and called for countries, humanitarian agencies and the media to keep away from any racist rhetoric and contribute to raising awareness, offering refugees the necessary protection. At the sessions???Ć?outset, King Salman briefed the Cabinet on his phone call to Sheikh Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al Sabah, the Emir of Kuwait, and his meetings with the Speaker of National Assembly of Kuwait; the Speakers of Shura, Representatives, National and Nation Councils of Gulf Cooperation Council for Arab States participating in the 9th meeting of the councils in the Kingdom; the Executive President and Founder of World Economic Forum; and the Austrian Foreign Minister. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh this afternoon.

content: During the meeting, they reviewed a number of issues of common interest. During their meeting, they discussed violence against the Palestinian population by the Israeli occupation forces and the latest developments in Syria.

title: King Salman Swears in New ambassadors to the US
content: Also sworn in as Ambassadors-designate were Prince Khalid bin Faisal bin Turki bin Abdullah to Jordan; Dr. Khalid bin Mohammed Al-Anqari to French; Dr. Awad bin Saleh Al-Awad to Germany; and Badr bin Ali bin Ahmed Kahil to the Maldives. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz today held a swearing in ceremony at Al-Yamamah Palace in Riyadh for Prince Abdullah bin Faisal bin Turki bin Abdullah, Ambassador-designate to the United States.

title: King Salman Departs the US Following Official State Visit
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz left Washington today after an official visit to the United States at the invitation of President Barack Obama.

title: King Salman Receives US Retirees of Saudi Aramco
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz met today at his residence in Washington American retirees of Saudi Aramco.

title: King Salman, President Obama hold Meeting at White House
content: King Salman was accompanied by Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense; Minister of State Dr. Musaed Al-Aiban; Minister of Finance Dr. Ibrahim Al-Assaf; Minister of Commerce and Industry Dr. Tawfiq Al-Rabee'a; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Al-Toraifi; Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir; Minister of Health Khalid bin Abdulaziz Al-Faleh; and Khalid bin Ali Al-Homaidan, Chief of General Intelligence. The King and President expressed their support for the Iraqi government's efforts to defeat Daesh and encouraged the full implementation of recently-agreed reforms by the Iraqi Parliament. On the Palestinian-Israeli conflict, leaders stressed the importance of the 2002 Arab Peace Initiative, reaching a comprehensive, just and lasting settlement for a two-state solution. Both leaders stressed the importance of reaching a lasting solution to the current conflict in Syria on the principles of Geneva declaration including the departure of Bashar Al-Assad who has lost legitimacy to lead Syria. King Salman and President Obama also focused on the importance of confronting extremism and terrorist organizations. On Yemen, the two leaders stressed the urgent need to reach a political solution within the GCC Initiative and the outcomes of the National Dialogue and UN Security Council Resolution 2216. Both leaders expressed concern over the humanitarian situation over which the King conveyed Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös commitment to continue providing assistance to the Yemeni people. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz met today with US President Barack Obama at the White House. The two leaders had a fruitful discussion in which they reviewed the enduring relationship between Saudi Arabia and the United States. The two leaders also discussed regional issues.

title: King Salman Receives Secretary Kerry
content: The meeting was attended by Prince Abdullah bin Faisal bin Turki; Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense; Minister of State Dr. Musaed bin Mohammed Al-Aiban; Minister of Finance Dr. Ibrahim bin Abdulaziz Al-Assaf; Minister of Commerce and Industry Dr. Tawfiq bin Fawzan Al-Rabiah; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Altoraifi; Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir; and President of the General Intelligence, Khalid bin Ali Al-Humaidan. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at his residence in Washington yesterday evening US Secretary of State John Kerry. During their meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations between the two friendly countries and discussed a number of issues of common interest.

title: Foreign Minister Meets with Senior US Officials
content: Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met at his residence in Washington today with the U.S. Assistant Secretary of State for Near Eastern Affairs Anne Patterson.

title: Foreign Minister Briefs Media on King???Ć?Ös Upcoming Meeting with President Obama
content: Minister Al-Jubeir added that the coalition forces and the forces of the legitimate government have been able to respond to these aggressive moves and achieve very important victories in different parts of Yemen, which led to a deterioration in the position of Houthis and Saleh loyalists. He hopes that these defeats will pave the way to finding a political solution to the crisis in Yemen. With regard to Yemen, Minister Al-Jubeir said, ???Ć?£the strategic goal of both countries is one, which is the implementation of UN Security Council Resolution 2216 fully and without conditions, and trying to reach this through diplomatic channels or political work." The Foreign minister added that it is up to the Houthis and former president Ali Abdullah Saleh, who refuse to accept this decision and continue their military operations, and attempt to occupy more towns and villages, to bring the conflict to an end.?é?? In remarks to reporters today in Washington, the minister said the visit will focus on the strategic relations with the US and moving them to a new horizon to help serve the interests of the two peoples and the two countries as well as security and stability in the region. Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir said today that the historical visit of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz to Washington and his meeting with President Barack Obama tomorrow in the White House comes within the framework of the distinctive and strong relations between the two countries.

title: King Salman Arrived in Washington
content: King Salman was received at received at Andrews Air Force Base by US Secretary of State John Kerry. King Salman's delegation includes Prince Abdul Ilah bin Abdulaziz, Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, and Prince Khalid bin Fahd bin Khalid. The King's official delegation includes Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, the Second Deputy Prime Minister and Defense Minister, other ministers and senior officials. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, King Salman bin Abdulaziz, arrived in Washington this evening on an official visit at the invitation of President Barack Obama.

title: King Salman to Meet with President Obama in Washington on Sept. 4
content: During his visit, King Salman discuss bilateral relations and ways of enhancing them in all fields, as well as regional and international issues of mutual interest. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz will pay an official visit to the United States on the invitation of President Barack Obama. King Salman is schedule to arrive in Washington on Thursday September 3 and meet with President Obama at the White House on Friday, September 4.

title: Foreign Minister Meets with Secretary Kerry
content: You may have the floor, Your Excellency Thank you and thank the Secretary for his visit to Saudi Arabia, and for the constructive talks between us. And to conclude, I would like to stress the keenness of Saudi Arabia and its commitments that there will be a political process in Yemen that will lead to the solution of this crisis peacefully. We are looking forward to the coming Riyadh conference that will be held on the 17th of May. It???Ć?Ös ???Ć?our Yemeni brothers are discussing their internal affairs, and based on the initiatives and the results of their dialogue. We welcome any efforts done by the United Nations in holding talks between the concerned Yemeni parties anywhere in the world. We???Ć?Öre looking at this ???Ć?that it will be a support to the efforts we are making in Riyadh conference, because objective is to come to a peaceful solution in Yemen. I debriefed him on the (inaudible) of Saudi Arabia. The Houthis and their allies should abide by this ceasefire period and do not interfere into ???Ć?in the way of these efforts, and no hostility actions against ???Ć?during this period. This will be defined ???Ć?this period will be defined soon and the details at the ???Ć?to conclude. I debriefed the Secretary on the big step done by the king in correcting the status of the Yemenis who are in Saudi Arabia in an illegal status, from 2 million to 3 million Yemenis living on the Saudi land and correcting their immigration status. And this will allow them to have jobs to help their families in Yemen back ???Ć?back in Yemen. This is different from other countries; they are deporting and extraditing the Yemenis and put them in camps for the refugees. And Saudi Arabia working for the interest and the dignity of our Yemeni brothers, whether in Yemen or in Saudi Arabia. For this reason, Saudi Arabia has taken, by direction of the king ???Ć?this big step is unprecedented in the history of the world. On the topics of mutual interest, including the ???Ć?we ???Ć?I briefed the Secretary on the meeting of the GCC, and the talks (inaudible) tomorrow on the Paris with the ???Ć?in Paris with the GCC foreign ministers. We talked about the Camp David on the 13th and 14th of May. We talked about that ???Ć?the Iranian interventions in the region, the negative interventions, whether in Lebanon, Syria, Iraq, or Yemen, or any other places. With regard to Yemen, I have been ???Ć?I debriefed him on the keenness of Saudi Arabia to send the humanitarian aids to the Yemeni people and the donation of $274 million, a donation from the (inaudible) aids to Yemen. I debriefed (inaudible) with the king (inaudible) and establishing a center in Riyadh to coordinate the relief work. But unfortunately, the keenness of the king and the Saudi Arabia to send the aids to Yemen was very difficult in view of the continuous ???Ć?of the war operations there, and the (inaudible) and their allies there. And he met His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Naif, the Crown Prince, the First Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of the Interior. He met His Royal Highness Prince Mohammad bin Salman, the Deputy Crown Prince and the Minister of Defense. We held bilateral talks at the ministry of foreign affairs. Minister Al-Jubeir's statement as provided by the Department of State: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met today with US Secretary of State John Kerry at the State Department.

title: Foreign Minister Receives US Special Envoy for Syria
content: Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir received in Jeddah today with Michael Ratney, the US Special Envoy for Syria. During the meeting, they discussed the latest developments of the Syrian crisis and the ongoing international efforts.

title: Minister Al-Jubeir Holds Meeting with Kerry on Nantucket Island
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met yesterday with Secretary of State John Kerry at his summer house on Nantucket Island, Massachusetts. During the meeting, they reviewed the latest developments and situations in the region, bilateral relations between the two countries, and ways of enhancing them in various fields.

title: King Salman Receives US Defense Secretary
content: The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received today at Al-Salam Palace in Jeddah U.S. Secretary of Defense Ashton Carter. During their meeting, they reviewed bilateral relations and discussed the latest regional developments. The meeting was also attended by Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior; Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense; Minister of State Dr. Musaid Al-Eiban; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel Altoraifi; and Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel Al-Jubeir.

title: Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir Meets with President Obama, Vice President Biden
content: The foreign minister and president also discussed regional issues including Syria, Iraq, Yemen and ongoing cooperative efforts to combat terrorism and extremism. They also discussed ways of moving forward with the implementation of the initiatives agreed upon among the GCC countries at the recent Camp David summit. Minister Al-Jubeir and President Obama discussed the agreement between the P5+1 countries and Iran regarding its nuclear programs. Minister Al-Jubeir reaffirmed Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös support for an agreement that prevents Iran from obtaining a nuclear capability. The foreign minister conveyed to the president greetings from the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and reiterated the King???Ć?Ös support and appreciation for the close ties between the two nations, as well as the ongoing cooperation in all areas. (WASHINGTON, DC) ???Ć?Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir met with President Barack Obama today at the White House for discussions on regional issues and Saudi-U.S. relations. The foreign minister also met separately with Vice President Joe Biden and National Security Advisor Susan Rice.

title: King Salman Receives Phone Call from President Obama
content: During the conversation, King Salman and President Obama reviewed the latest developments in the region, particularly in Yemen. They also confirmed the strategic and distinguished relations between Saudi Arabia and the United States. In response, King Salman confirmed that Saudi Arabia supports any arrangement that guarantees preventing Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon, and at the same time includes an inspection mechanism of all sites. Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received a telephone call from US President Barack Obama early this morning (Saudi time). The President briefed King Salman on the details of the Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action (JCPOA) regarding Iran???Ć?Ös nuclear program, agreed upon by the P5+1, the European Union and Iran. President Obama underscored that the United States is committed to peace and stability in the region.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Receives Telephone Call from U.S. Secretary of State
content: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, received a telephone call from U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry during which they reviewed the overall situations in the region and exchanged view points on the best ways of achieving international peace and security. They also confirmed the distinguished and strategic relations between the two friendly countries.

title: King, Crown Prince Congratulate President Obama on Independence Day
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz has congratulated President Barack Obama on America???Ć?Ös Independence Day. The King conveyed the congratulations in a cable on behalf of himself and the people of Saudi Arabia. Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif sent a similar cable of congratulations to the president.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Receives U.S. Director of National Intelligence
content: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, received in Jeddah today U.S. Director of National Intelligence James Clapper. During the meeting, they discussed a number of issues of common interest between the two friendly countries. The meeting was attended by Chief of General Intelligence Khalid Alhmaidan.

title: Foreign Minister Al-Jubeir Meets with Deputy Assistant Secretary of State
content: Adel bin Ahmad Al-Jubeir, Foreign Minister, received here today US Deputy Assistant Secretary of State for the Arabian Peninsula in the Bureau of Near Eastern Affairs Susan Ziadeh. During the meeting, they discussed the latest developments of the issues of common interest in the region.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman Meets with US Defense Secretary
content: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman was accompanied by Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir and other senior officials. Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, met with U.S. Secretary of Defense Ashton Carter today at the Pentagon. After reviewing the Honor Guard, the two ministers discussed bilateral and regional issues.

title: Crown Prince Meets with Secretary of Homeland Security and Directors of CIA, FBI
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, met separately today met with Secretary of Homeland Security Jeh Johnson, FBI Director James Comey, and CIA Director John Brennan. The meetings were attended by ministers of state Dr. Saad bin Khalid Al-Jabri and Dr. Musaid bin Muhammad Al-Eiban; and Chief of General Intelligence Khalid bin Ali Al-Humaidan.

title: Crown Prince, Deputy Crown Prince Met with President Obama
content: The meeting was attended by ministers of state Dr. Saad bin Khalid Al-Jabri and Dr. Musaid bin Muhammad Al-Eiban; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel bin Zaid Al-Toraifi; Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir, and Chief of General Intelligence Khalid bin Ali Al-Humaidan. Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, met today with U.S. President Barack Obama at the White House prior to the US-GCC Summit. Prince Mohammad bin Naif conveyed greetings to the president on behalf of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz. During their meeting, President Obama confirmed his support for the legitimate government in Yemen and lauded the strength of the strategic and historical relations between the two countries.

title: Crown Prince and Deputy Crown Prince Arrive in US for Talks with President
content: Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, arrived in Washington today for talks with U.S. President Barack Obama and attend the US-GCC Summit at Camp David.

title: King Salman Chairs Cabinet Session
content: The Cabinet approved a new, the Princess Nora bint Abdulrahman for Females Excellence, to be awarded for theoretical studies in literature, humanitarian studies, natural science, health science, as well as social works, economic projects, charity and technical works. The Cabinet then approved the following: authorized the Minister of Justice, or his deputy, to negotiate and sign a draft memorandum of understanding for cooperation in the judicial and legal field with Afghanistan; the application of the resolution of the GCC Supreme Council in its 34th which was held in Kuwait on December 10-11, 2013, on the approval of Manama Document for GCC Unified Judicial Authority Law as a guide regulation for four years to be automatically renewable in case of absence of any observations by the Member States and the guide strategy's document of GCC e-Government; authorized the Minister of Foreign Affairs, or his deputy, to negotiate and sign a draft general agreement for cooperation with Niger; and a Ministry of Transport's recommendation to join the 1988 protocol regarding the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea (SOLAS) 1974. The Cabinet reiterated the determination of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia and its intention firmly to continue its efforts to combat terrorism and its financing, praising in this context the Kingdom's hosting of the second meeting of the working group for combating the financing of the ISIS terrorist organization under the patronage of the Crown Prince, noting that the meeting reflects the extent of interest of the international community to combat terrorism and its financing and being aware of the risks that threaten the security of all human societies. The Cabinet condemned the Houthi militia???Ć?Ös Katyusha and mortar attacks on civilian targets in Najran and Jizan and commended the swift Saudi Armed Forces???Ć?response to these attacks. The Council of Ministers reviewed a number of reports on the latest regional and internationals developments, including the latest developments in Yemeni. The Cabinet stressed the importance of committing to the humanitarian crease-fire, which will begin on Tuesday, to safeguard the relief operations in Yemen. The Cabinet also said it welcomed the proposed Yemeni all party conference in Riyadh under the umbrella of GCC. The king briefed the Cabinet on the telephone call he received from King Abdullah II of Jordan and his talks with Niger President Issoufou Mahamadou and his meeting with U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry. King Salman commended the U.S. positions and its support for initiatives aimed at achieving security and stability in the region. King Salman hope that the US-GCC Summit contributes to enhancing close relations, coordination and cooperation between the GCC and the US and promote regional security and stability in region. King Salman commended the participation of French President Francois Hollande as the first guest of honor at the consultative summit, stressing that the participation of the French President embodied the strong relations between the GCC and France. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz this afternoon chaired the Cabinet's session at Al-Yamamah palace in Riyadh. At the outset, King Salman expressed his thanks to the leaders of Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) for their efforts in the 15th Consultative Meeting.

title: Crown Prince Mohammad to Lead Saudi Delegation to the US-GCC Summit
content: Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir announced today that Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of the Interior, will lead the Saudi delegation to the United States-GCC Summit. Minister Al-Jubeir said that The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz deputized Crown Prince Mohammad to lead the delegation with the participation of Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense, due to the timing of the summit, the scheduled humanitarian ceasefire in Yemen and the opening of the King Salman Center for Humanitarian Aid. Minister Al-Jubeir reiterated King Salman???Ć?Ös commitment to achieving peace and security in Yemen and his eagerness to the speedy delivery of humanitarian aid to the brotherly people of Yemen.

title: Saudi Arabia Announces Five-Day Ceasefire in Yemen
content: Other topics discussed during the meeting included Iran???Ć?Ös nuclear program, fight again Daesh (ISIS) and the upcoming U.S.-GCC Summit. During his visit, Secretary Kerry also met with the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz, Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif and Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman. During their meeting, Minister Al-Jubeir briefed Secretary Kerry on the illegal residence status of the millions of Yemeni expatriates currently in Saudi Arabia, which will allow them to find work and help their families in Yemen. ???Ć?£This is the opposite of what the other countries do -- expelling illegal residents and putting refugees in camps. But the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is keen on the interests, position and dignity of our Yemeni brothers, whether in Yemen or in Saudi Arabia,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. Secretary Kerry said, ???Ć?£Today, we particularly welcome a new Saudi initiative to try to bring about a peaceful resolution through the announcement of their intent to establish a full, five-day renewable ceasefire and humanitarian pause ???Ć?no bombing, no shooting, no movement or repositioning of troops to achieve military advantage, understanding ???Ć?an understanding that needs to be reached that neither party is going to exploit the humanitarian pause.???Ć?During his remarks, Minister Al-Jubeir stressed Saudi Arabia's commitment to a political process in Yemen and solving the crisis in a peaceful manner. ???Ć?£We welcome any effort made by the United Nations to hold talks between the Yemeni concerned parties anywhere in the world,???Ć?said Minister Al-Jubeir. During a joint press conference in Riyadh today with U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry, Saudi Foreign Minister Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir announced that Saudi Arabia and its coalition allies will hold a five-day ceasefire in Yemen to better facilitate humanitarian aid. Both leaders noted that the ceasefire is conditioned on the Houthis committing to all the agreements related to the ceasefire. The date and requirements of the ceasefire will be announced soon.

title: King Salman Receives US Secretary of State Kerry
content: The meeting was attended by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior; Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Crown Prince, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense; Minister of State Dr. Musaid bin Muhammad Al-Eiban; Minister of Culture and Information Dr. Adel Altoraifi; and Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir. The Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz received at Al-Yamamah Palace today U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry. During their meeting, they discussed bilateral relations in addition to issues of common interest, and overall regional and international developments.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Meets with US Secretary of State
content: Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Defense met today with U.S. Secretary of State John Kerry at the Royal Court. During their meeting, they discussed bilateral relations and reviewed the latest regional and international developments. The meeting was attended by Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel Al-Jubeir and Chief of General Staff Gen. Abdulrahman Al-Bunyan.

title: Crown Prince Meets with US Congressman Jason Chaffetz
content: The audience was attended by Minister of State and Cabinet's Member Dr. Saad bin Khalid Al-Jabri, Minister of Foreign Affairs Adel Al-Jubeir and Deputy Chief of U.S. Mission Timothy A. Lenderking. Crown Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior received at Al-Yamamah Palace today the Chairman of the United States House of Representatives Committee on Oversight and Government Reform Jason Chaffetz. During the meeting, issues of common interest were reviewed in addition to discussing the overall latest developments in the region.?é??

title: 93 Suspected ISIS Terrorists Arrested, Plots Thwarted
content: Maj. Gen. Al-???Ć?ÄTurki reiterated the Kingdom???Ć?Ös unwavering commitment to confront all those who threaten the peace and security of the country. A Saudi citizen with a criminal history was arrested on April 18, 2015 in Al-Baha Province transporting weapons, weapons components, and bomb making material. Preliminary results of the investigation indicate that he was influenced by Daesh???Ć?Ös online propaganda. On March 13, 2015, authorities discovered a plot by a Saudi national and two Syrians who live in a Gulf country, to carry out a suicide car bomber attack on the U.S. Embassy in Riyadh. On March 14, the Saudi and one of the Syrian plotters who entered the country a few days earlier, were arrested. The investigation in the plot is ongoing. The security agencies also arrested a cell made up of nine Saudi nationals, including a including a woman who was unsuccessfully used to assassinate a military personnel. The cell was using social media to try to recruit young members and facilitate their travel to conflict zones. A cell consisted of 65 people, including a Palestinian, a Yemen, and two unknown nationalities, was arrested on March 8, 2015. The group was planning to attack residential complexes and incite sectarian strife similar to the Nov. 3, 2014 attack in the Al-Dalwah village in the Eastern Province. The group also targeted security forces and the General Intelligence prisons. They were also recruiting young people, disseminate Daesh propaganda, secure safe houses for wanted suspects, establish training areas for the young recruits and provide assistance to those who want to travel to conflict zones. The group was also involved in illegal fund raising activates to carry out their depraved objectives. Ministry of Interior spokesman Major General Mansour Al-???Ć?ÄTurki said the first cell, which called itself Soldiers of the Land of the Two Holy Mosques, was arrested on Dec. 31, 2014. The cell was made up of 15 Saudi nationals and led by an explosives expert. Another Saudi explosives expert was arrested on Jan. 31, 2015. Maj. Gen. Al-Turki said that the suspect arrested in January acknowledged pledging allegiance to Daesh leader Abu Bakr Al-Baghdadi and that he was making improvised explosive devices (IEDs) to attack security personnel. He also admitted his role in posting how to make explosives on social media. The Ministry of Interior announced today 93 suspected terrorists affiliated with Daesh (ISIS) have been arrested since December 2014, including a woman, who were planning terrorist attacks in Saudi Arabia, including attack against the U.S. embassy in Riyadh.

title: Prince Saud Al-Faisal Receives Egyptian Foreign Minister
content: Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Saud Al-Faisal, Minister of Foreign Affairs met today in Riyadh with Egyptian Foreign Minister Sameh Shoukry. During the meeting, the ministers reviewed bilateral relations and the latest regional and international developments, especially in Yemen.

title: Deputy Crown Prince Receives US Speaker Boehner
content: Prince Mohammed bin Naif bin Abdulaziz, Deputy Crown Prince, Second Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior, received at his office in Riyadh today Speaker of the US House of Representatives John Boehner and the accompanying delegation. During the meeting, they discussed issues of common interest in the context of the prospects for cooperation and the historical relations between the two friendly countries. The meeting was attended by Minister of State and Member of the Cabinet Dr. Saad Al-Jabri; Chief of General Intelligence Khalid Al-Humaidan; Deputy Minister of Interior Abdulrahman Al-Rubaiaan; Director General of the General Directorate of Investigation Gen. Abdulaziz Al-Hoireny; Saudi Ambassador to the United States Adel bin Ahmed Al-Jubeir, and US Ambassador to Saudi Arabia Joseph W. Westphal.

title: Minister of Defense Meets with U.S. Senator
content: Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz, Minister of Defense, Chief of the Royal Court and Special Advisor to the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques, met today with Senator Jim Inhofe, senior member of the US Senate Armed Services Committee. During the meeting, they reviewed cooperation between Saudi Arabia and the US in view of the coalition of Operation Decisive Storm, led by Saudi Arabia at an invitation President Abdrabu Mansur Hadi of Yemen to liberate his country from extremist terrorist group, including the Houthi militias and their supporters so that Yemen could restore legitimacy and stability.

title: King Salman, US President Obama Discussed Regional Issues in Telephone Call
content: Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz and US President Barack Obama talked ???Ć?Äabout the recent developments in Yemen. During the telephone conversation, President Obama expressed full US ???Ć?Äsupport for the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia???Ć?Ös Determination Storm and underscored the US commitment to Saudi ???Ć?ÄArabia???Ć?Ös security. King Salman thanked the president for the US offer to provide the Kingdom with the support it ???Ć?Äneeds.???Ć?
